 Civil and Infrastructure Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Amit Sharma 
 
 
 
 
 
 Amit Sharma 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Amit Sharma
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
  Ph.D.(Environmental Engineering): Indian Institute of Technology Madras 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        amit[dot]sharma[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Atmospheric Chemistry; Air Pollution; Climate change; Impact on crops and human health 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1658 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About:
                     
 
 
 Dr. Amit Sharma  is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil and Infrastructure Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur. His specialization lies in environmental engineering, with a specific focus on air pollution. His research involves weather forecasting, climate modeling, and air pollution studies using complex model simulations on supercomputers. This work is supplemented by comprehensive data analysis from satellites, radiosondes, and various ground-based instruments. He has been serving as an Assistant Professor at IIT Jodhpur since July 2020. Prior to this, he worked as a Postdoctoral Research Associate at Washington State University, where he focused on wildfire modeling. He has also held visiting scholar positions at the University of Manchester, specializing in aerosol chemical speciation, and at the Max Planck Institute for Chemistry, where he contributed to weather research and forecasting. 
 Dr. Sharma’s expertise and research continue to contribute to advancements in environmental engineering and air pollution studies.

 school

 email 
 
 
 
                                        dguharoy[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Rock Mechanics; Engineering Geology; Energy Geomechanics; Landslide. 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1653 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About:
                     
 
 
 Dr. Debanjan Guha  Roy is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil and Infrastructure Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur. His research focuses on advancing critical areas in energy and infrastructure, contributing to sustainable and resilient development. His expertise includes geological energy storage and production, emergent geo-energy solutions, and geological engineering for infrastructure development and protection. Over the past five years, Dr. Roy’s research group has published extensively in highly respected journals, including the  International Journal of Hydrogen Energy, Microporous and Mesoporous Materials, Geomechanics for Energy and the Environment, Measurement,  and the  Journal of Rock Mechanics and Geotechnical Engineering. 
 His work continues to drive progress in geo-energy and infrastructure engineering, shaping innovative solutions for the future.

 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Monika Dubey 
 
 
 
 
 
 Monika Dubey 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Monika Dubey
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD. Indian Institute of Technology Roorkee 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        monikadubey[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Water and wastewater treatment; Monitoring and removal of emerging contaminants; Bioremediation; Biotransformation of trace organic pollutants; Risk assessment for water reuse 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1668 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About:
                     
 
 
 Dr. Monika Dubey is an Assistant Professor in the
Department of Civil and Infrastructure Engineering at the Indian Institute of
Technology (IIT) Jodhpur. Educational Background 
Dr. Dubey received her Ph.D. from the Indian Institute of Technology Roorkee,
specializing in wastewater treatment, the fate of organic micropollutants, and
environmental risk assessment. 
 
 Research Interests 
Dr. Dubey’s research expertise lies in environmental monitoring, wastewater
treatment, environmental remediation, and mass spectrometry techniques such as
UHPLC-MS/MS, with a particular focus on emerging contaminants in the
environment and their fate during wastewater treatment. She actively engages in
collaborative and interdisciplinary research to develop innovative and
sustainable solutions, contributing to advancements in sustainable wastewater
management and environmental protection. 
 Sustainable Environmental Systems (SES) Lab 
The Sustainable Environmental Systems (SES) Lab at IIT Jodhpur is dedicated to
developing innovative and sustainable technologies for environmental protection
and resource recovery. The research spans wastewater treatment, nature-based
solutions, the fate and removal of emerging contaminants, bioelectrochemical
processes, and carbon sequestration strategies. By integrating advanced
analytical tools and AI-driven approaches, SES Lab aims to address critical
challenges in water, environment, and climate sustainability. 
 
 Research Positions Open 
Research positions are available in the fields of biological wastewater
treatment, advanced oxidation processes for water and wastewater treatment,
nature-based solutions, and micropollutant/transformation product analysis.
Please email your CV to   monikadubey@iitj.ac.in

 People 
 
 
 P Ravi Prakash 
 
 
 
 
 
 P Ravi Prakash 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            P Ravi Prakash
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD. Indian Institute of Technology Gandhinagar 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        rp[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Computational Mechanics; Structural Fire Engineering; Application of AI in Structural Engineering, Building Information Modelling (BIM) 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1661 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About:
                     
 
 
 Dr. P. Ravi Prakash   is an Assistant Professor in the  Department of Civil and Infrastructure Engineering  at the  Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur  and an  Affiliate Faculty  at the  School of Artificial Intelligence and Data Sciences . His research focuses on  structural fire engineering, computational mechanics, and building information modeling (BIM). 
 His research group works on  fire-induced progressive collapse, discontinuum modeling of materials and structures, BIM-driven sustainable building design, and virtual twins. 
 Educational Qualifications 
 
 Ph.D.  – Indian Institute of Technology Gandhinagar (2018) 
 B.Tech.  – Acharya Nagarjuna University (2013) 
 
 Work Experience 
 
 Visiting Researcher  – Michigan State University, USA (September 2023 – February 2024) 
 Assistant Professor  – Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur (March 2021 – Present) 
 Assistant Professor  – National Institute of Technology Warangal (January 2020 – February 2021) 
 Postdoctoral Fellow  – Universidade do Minho, Portugal (December 2018 – December 2019) 
 
 Research Grants & Awards 
 Dr. Prakash has been awarded the  Extramural Research Grant  from the  DRDO, Ministry of Defence, Government of India (Armaments Research Board)  and the  Start-up Research Grant  from the  Anusandhan National Research Foundation (ANRF).  He is also a recipient of the prestigious  SIRE Fellowship  from ANRF. 
 Key Publications 
 His research has been published in leading journals, including  Engineering Fracture Mechanics, Fire Safety Journal, Computers & Structures,  and  Engineering Structures. 
 His expertise continues to contribute to advancements in  fire safety, computational modeling, and sustainable infrastructure design.

 Civil and Infrastructure Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Tekcham Gishan Singh 
 
 
 
 
 
 Tekcham Gishan Singh 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Tekcham Gishan Singh
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD. Indian Institute of Technology Guwahati 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        tekcham[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Steel structure testing and design, high-strength steel, tubular and thin-walled structures, concrete-filled steel tubes, fire-resistant structural design, steel corrosion and retrofitting, sustainable infrastructure. 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1660 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About: 
                     
 
 
 Dr. Tekcham Gishan Singh  is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil and Infrastructure Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur. His research focuses on  structural testing, numerical modelling, and the design of steel structures. His broader research interests include  high-strength steel, tubular structures, concrete-filled steel tubes, fire-resistant structural design, corrosion, retrofitting of steel structures, hybrid structural systems, fatigue and fracture mechanics, and sustainable infrastructure .  His expertise contributes to advancements in structural engineering and the development of fire-resistant and earthquake-resilient infrastructure, ultimately aiding in the creation of safe and reliable design methods for steel structures. Dr. Singh earned his Ph.D. from the  Indian Institute of Technology Guwahati  in 2019 and completed his B.Tech. from the  National Institute of Technology Karnataka  in 2013. Before joining IIT Jodhpur in 2021, he worked as a Postdoctoral Researcher at  The Hong Kong Polytechnic University  from 2019 to 2021. He also gained industry experience as an Assistant Engineer at  Bangalore Metro Rail Corporation Ltd.  from 2013 to 2014. As part of his professional engagements, Dr. Singh serves as an  Editorial Board Member for  Scientific Reports , a journal published by the Nature Portfolio.

 People 
 
 
 Yogiraj Sargam 
 
 
 
 
 
 Yogiraj Sargam 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Yogiraj Sargam
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D. Iowa State University, USA 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        ysargam[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Carbon Capture, Utilization, and Storage (CCUS), Waste valorization, Nanomaterials, Cement-based materials 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1674 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Yogiraj Sargam is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil and Infrastructure Engineering at IIT Jodhpur. He works in the research areas of carbon capture, utilization, and storage (CCUS), waste valorization, and nanomaterials within the cement and concrete value chain. Dr. Sargam earned his Ph.D. (2021) and M.S. (2019) from Iowa State University, USA, under the supervision of Prof. Kejin Wang. His doctoral work on the dispersion and stability of nanomaterials in cementitious systems earned him the Research Excellence Award from the university. His master’s research was focused on the measurement and prediction of mass concrete parameters and thermal conductivity. After his graduate studies, Dr. Sargam worked at CarbonCure Technologies, Canada (2021–2025), where he held roles of Research Scientist, Director of Research, and Senior Director of Research. During his tenure, he played a key role in advancing the understanding of CO2 mineralization mechanisms and developing CO2 utilization technologies for low-carbon concrete. Dr. Sargam has published extensively in high-impact journals, including Cement and Concrete Research, Journal of CO₂ Utilization, and Composites Part B, and actively collaborates with renowned researchers worldwide. His detailed research profile is available on  Google Scholar . If you are interested in joining Dr. Sargam’s research group or would like to collaborate with him, please reach out to him at  ysargam@iitj.ac.in .

 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Sreejith Krishnan 
 
 
 
 
 
 Sreejith Krishnan 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Sreejith Krishnan
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD. Indian Institute of Technology Delhi 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        skrishnan[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Material Characterization; Cement Chemistry; Concrete Technology; Durability and Repair of Structures;  
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1670 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About:
                     
 
 
 Dr. Sreejith Krishnan  is an  Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil and Infrastructure Engineering  at the  Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur . His research focuses on the  chemistry of cementitious materials , with a particular interest in  low-carbon cement development, hydration and microstructure of alternative binders, and sustainable construction materials . Before joining IIT Jodhpur, Dr. Krishnan was a  Research Fellow at the University of Leeds, UK (2021–2023) , where he worked on the  MUSE project  under  Prof. Susan Bernal , developing novel  low-carbon cement from activated clays . Prior to that, he was a  Postdoctoral Researcher at EPFL, Switzerland (2019–2020) , working with  Prof. Karen Scrivener  on the  chemistry of belite in Portland cement  through a  Swiss Government Excellence Postdoctoral Fellowship . He obtained his  Ph.D. in Civil Engineering from IIT Delhi (2019)  under the supervision of  Prof. Shashank Bishnoi , as part of the  Low Carbon Cement Project , funded by the  Swiss Agency for Development and Cooperation . His research focused on  hydration and microstructure development in limestone calcined clay cements (LC3) and optimizing LC3 blend compositions . He also holds an  M.Tech. in Construction Engineering and Management from IIT Delhi (2014)  and a  B.Tech. in Civil Engineering from NIT Calicut (2012) . Dr. Krishnan has been recognized for his contributions to cement chemistry and sustainable construction. He received the  Distinction in Doctoral Research Award (IIT Delhi, 2019) , the  Swiss Government Excellence Fellowship (2019) , the  Advances in Cement Research Prize from the Institute of Civil Engineers, UK (2020) , and the  Prime Minister’s Early Career Research Grant from ANRF, India (2025) . 
 His expertise continues to drive advancements in  sustainable construction materials and the development of alternative cementitious systems .

 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Pradeep Kumar Dammala 
 
 
 
 
 
 Pradeep Kumar Dammala 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Pradeep Kumar Dammala
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D. (Geotechnical Engineering): Indian Institute of Technology Guwahati 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        pkdammala[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Soil Dynamics, Seismic Soil Liquefaction, Seismic Soil Structure Interaction, Monopile Foundations, Sand Dune Dynamics, Computational Fluid Dynamics, Sustainable Geotechnics 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1655 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About:
                     
 
 
 Dr. Pradeep Kumar Dammala  is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil and Infrastructure Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur. His research focuses on soil dynamics, soil-structure interaction, pile foundations, seismic requalification studies, aeolian sand dune dynamics, and sustainable geotechnical solutions. 
 
 
 
 
                        Soil and Advanced Foundation Analysis for Resilient Infrastructure (SAFARI) Lab
                     
 
 
 The  SAFARI  research group at IIT Jodhpur is dedicated to enhancing infrastructure resilience against natural and human-induced disasters by studying soil behavior and designing stable foundations. SAFARI Group  has received several prestigious accolades for their research: DST-DAAD Fellowship - Dr. Pradeep and Ms. Sumaja in collaboration with Prof. Eric Parteli Research Impact Award from the Commonwealth Scholarship Commission in the UK - Dr. Pradeep SERB SIRE Fellowship - Dr. Pradeep Dr. APJ Abdul Kalam Research Fellowship - Dr. Pradeep and Ms. Sumaja Prime Minister’s Research Fellowship (PMRF) to Ms. Sumaja Additionally, members of his group have been recognized with the Science and Engineering Research Board (SERB) OVDF, the International Society for Soil Mechanics and Geotechnical Engineering (ISSMGE) Travel Grants, the Institute of International Finance (IIF) Fellowship, and the Commonwealth Split-site Fellowship. 
 
 
 
 
                        Research positions open
                     
 
 
 Several research positions (PhD/research interns) are available in the SAFARI Group. Interested aspirants can email a short note (1-page strictly) of research interests and motivation to join our group to  pkdammala@iitj.ac.in .

 Civil and Infrastructure Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Mayank Suman 
 
 
 
 
 
 Mayank Suman 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Mayank Suman
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD. Indian Institute of Technology Kharagpur 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        mayanksuman[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Effect of changing climate on hydrological extremes; Climate change mitigation strategies; Remote sensing application in hydrology 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1665 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About: 
                     
 
 
 Dr. Mayank Suman  is an  Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil and Infrastructure Engineering  at the  Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur . His research focuses on  studying and modeling climate change impacts on hydrological systems, statistical analysis of hydrological time series, and the application of wavelet transformation and copula theory in hydroclimatic analysis . He earned his  Ph.D. from IIT Kharagpur (2020) , where his research, under the supervision of  Prof. Rajib Maity , focused on the  analysis and modeling of climate change impact on hydrological extreme events across India . He also holds an  M.Tech. in Civil Engineering from IIT Kharagpur (2015)  and a  B.Tech. in Civil Engineering from NIT Hamirpur (2013) . Before joining IIT Jodhpur, Dr. Suman held research positions at leading institutions. He worked as a  Postdoctoral Researcher at Pohang University of Science and Technology, South Korea (Sep 2021 – Feb 2022)  in the  Division of Environmental Science and Engineering . He also served as a  Research Associate at IIT Kharagpur (Jan 2021 – July 2021)  in the  Department of Civil Engineering . 
 Beyond academia, Dr. Suman is an  open-source enthusiast  and a  contributor to multiple open-source projects , earning the title of  Arctic Code Vault Contributor for GitHub's 2020 Code Archive Program . Additionally, he is a  martial artist , holding a  First-Degree Black Belt (Shodan) in Kyokushin-kan Karate .

 Mohit Kumar Jangid 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Mohit Kumar Jangid
                         
 
                                 Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 The Ohio State University, Columbus, USA 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        mjangid[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Automated Formal Verification; System Security and Privacy; Trusted Execution Environment; Cryptography and Protocol Design 
 
 
 
 call 
 
  0291 280 1271 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 I am Assistant Professor at IIT, Jodhpur in the department of Computer Science and Engineering.  I graduated with Ph.D. with Cybersecurity in the department of Computer Science and Engineering at The Ohio State University (OSU) in Summer 2024. My advisors are  Dr. Zhiqiang Lin  and  Dr. Yinqian Zhang . I graduated from Malaviya National Institute of Technology, Jaipur (MNIT) with the Bachelor in Technology of Computer Engineering in 2013. My Bachelor theis project was supervised by  Dr. Manoj Singh Gaur .  I am interested in research areas of Automated Formal Verification, System Security and Privacy, Trusted Execution Environment, Cryptography and Protocol Design.

 Angshuman Paul    
 
 
 
 
 
 Angshuman Paul

 school 
 
 PhD. Indian Statistical Institute Kolkata 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        apaul[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Synthesis and Analysis of Medical Images; Deep Learning; Computer Vision 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1274 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About:
                     
 
 
 Angshuman Paul is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Computer Science & Engineering at IIT Jodhpur. His research primarily focuses on the development of deep learning techniques for the synthesis and analysis of medical images, aiming to advance affordable healthcare solutions. Prior to joining IIT Jodhpur, he served as a postdoctoral fellow at the National Institutes of Health (NIH), USA.

 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Susil Kumar Mohanty 
 
 
 
 
 
 Susil Kumar Mohanty 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Susil Kumar Mohanty
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D. : Indian Institute of Technology Patna 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        susilmohanty[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Blockchain and Off-chain Technologies, Payment Channel Networks, Cybersecurity and Privacy, Applied Cryptography, Zero-Knowledge Proofs, Decentralized & Secure AI/ML 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1281 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 I am currently working as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur. Prior to this, I served as a Post-doctoral Researcher at the Faculty of Mathematics, Informatics, and Mechanics (MIM), University of Warsaw, Poland, where I worked in the Cryptography and Blockchain Group led by Prof. Stefan Dziembowski. I earned my Ph.D. from the Department of Computer Science and Engineering, Indian Institute of Technology Patna, under the supervision of Prof. Somanath Tripathy in the SLIP: Security Lab @ IIT Patna. My research interests include Blockchain and Off-chain Technologies, Payment Channel Networks, Cybersecurity and Privacy, Applied Cryptography, Zero-Knowledge Proofs (ZKPs), and Decentralized & Secure AI/ML.

 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Mayank Vatsa   
 
 
 
 
 
 Mayank Vatsa   
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Mayank Vatsa  
                         
 
                                Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD. West Virginia University 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        mvatsa[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Computer Vision; Machine Learning; Deep Learning; Biometrics; Trusted AI 
 
 
 
 call 
 
  0291 280 1266 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About:
                     
 
 
 Mayank Vatsa is a Professor at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jodhpur, a Fellow of IEEE and IAPR, and a member of the National Academy of AI (USA). His research focuses on Artificial Intelligence, with an emphasis on Generative AI, Responsible AI, computer vision, and biometrics. He is a recipient of India's prestigious Swarnajayanti Fellowship and the IEEE Meritorious Service Award (2023). He serves as Co-Editor-in-Chief of the IEEE Transactions on Biometrics, Behavior, and Identity Science, a journal he led the efforts to establish during his tenure as Vice President of the IEEE Biometrics Council. He has also held senior roles at premier conferences like AAAI, CVPR, ICCV, IJCB, and FG. Dr. Vatsa has contributed in several of India's key technology initiatives, contributing to UIDAI (Aadhaar) standards, DigiYatra, the NITI Aayog 'Responsible AI for All' policy, national biometric standards, and the formation of the Technology Innovation Hub at IIT Jodhpur.

 Computer Science & Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Anand Mishra 
 
 
 
 
 
 Anand Mishra 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Anand Mishra
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD. International Institute of Information Technology Hyderabad 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        mishra[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Computer Vision; Deep Learning; Knowledge Graph; Multimodal Machine Learning 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1264 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Anand Mishra serves as a faculty member in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering. He leads the Vision, Language, and Learning Group (VL2G) at IIT Jodhpur, which focuses on problems at the intersection of vision and language. Specifically, the group is focusing on video understanding, efficient vision and language models and multilinguality. Anand has made significant contributions to the field through influential resources and systems, including the IIIT-5K and OCR-VQA datase ts, as well as IndicPhotoOCR, an open-source OCR system for Indian language text in natural images. His research group has received support through various industry and government of India grants, including Microsoft Academic Partnership Grant (2021 and 2023), MeitY Grant on National Language Translation Mission, a research grant from HealthcareNLP, a Gift Grant from Accenture, and a Start-up Research Grant from SERB, Govt. of India. Anand is an active reviewer for top-tier computer vision, language and AI venues, and his reviewing contributions have been recognized with Outstanding Reviewer Awards at ICCV 2021 and CVPR 2023. He served as Program Co-Chair for ICVGIP 2025 and is currently serving as an Area Chair for CVPR 2026 and ECCV 2026.

 People 
 
 
 Tanmay Inamdar   
 
 
 
 
 
 Tanmay Inamdar   
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Tanmay Inamdar  
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD. The University of Iowa 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        taninamdar[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Approximation Algorithms; Computational Geometry; Parameterized Complexity; Graph Algorithms 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1268 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 I am an Assistant Professor in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at IIT Jodhpur. In 2020, I obtained my PhD in Computer  Science from the University of Iowa, advised by  Prof. Kasturi Varadarajan . Then,  during 2020-2023, I was a postdoctoral researcher at University of Bergen, where I was hosted by  Prof. Saket Saurabh .  I am interested in theoretical computer science in general, but in particular this includes design of approximation and parameterized algorithms for clustering and geometric optimization problems. For more information, please check out my website:  https://sites.google. com/view/taninamdar/

 People 
 
 
 Hardik Jain 
 
 
 
 
 
 Hardik Jain 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Hardik Jain
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD. Technical University Berlin, Germany 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        hardik[dot]jain[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 3D Computer Vision, Edge AI, VR/AR Visualization 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1276 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Since March 2025, Dr Jain is an assistant professor at the CSE department of IIT Jodhpur. In June 2025, Dr Jain, recevied DST INSPIRE Faculty Fellowship Grant for five years. Before joining academics, he was in industrial research at Hitachi Rail, Berlin for little over two years. There Dr Jain led R&D projects dealing with digitalization in the railway domain, including a patent in Digital Twin. For his PhD at Technical University Berlin, he worked on Deep Learning Mesh Parameterization of 3D Shapes for various applications. During his PhD, he developed 3D data registration softwares for medical instrument manufacturing companies. His focus areas include 3D Computer Vision, Edge AI, VR/AR Visualization amongst others.

 Computer Science & Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Debasis Das   
 
 
 
 
 
 Debasis Das   
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Debasis Das  
                         
 
                                Associate Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D: Indian Institute of Technology, Patna 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        debasis[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Vehicular Ad-Hoc Networks (VANETs) / V2X systems, Artificial Intelligence (AI), Urban Intelligence, Distributed Deep Learning, Cryptography, and blockchain security 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1261 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About:
                     
 
 
 Dr. Debasis Das is currently working as an Associate Professor in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering and Associate Faculty at the School of Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (AIDE), as well as Coordinator of IoT and Applications (Inter-disciplinary Research Platform (IDRP) at Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jodhpur, Rajasthan, India. His research interests include Vehicular Ad-Hoc Networks (VANETs), Blockchain, the Internet of Things (IoT), Machine Learning (ML), Artificial Intelligence (AI), and Quantum Technology. Dr. Das published 170 research papers in top international journals( Like IEEE Transactions, ACM Transactions, etc) and top B/A/A* Conferences. Dr. Das has 23 sponsored projects and 2 consultancy projects to his credit. He has been awarded the Early Career Research (ECR) Award,  BRICS Young Scientist Award, Young Researcher Award-2024, DST-NSF Project Award 2025, NSTC Research Project Award 2025 from Science & Engineering Research Board (SERB), Department of Science & Technology (DST), MoE,  NSF, and NSTC, respectively. He has many research collaborations with premier institutions in India and different universities across the globe. He has been engaged in different academic activities inside and outside the Institute. He has supervised 10 Ph.D. students, and 10 are currently pursuing their theses. He has also supervised more than 50 M.E./M.Tech. thesis. He is a senior member of IEEE and VTS and a professional member of ACM.

 Richa Singh   
 
 
 
 
 
 Richa Singh   
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Richa Singh  
                         
 
                                Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD. West Virginia University 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        richa[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in 
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Machine Learning; Pattern Recognition; Biometrics; Forensics; Trusted AI 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1267 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 ​D​r. Singh is a Professor in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jodhpur. She earned her Ph.D. in Computer Science from West Virginia University, USA. She is a globally recognized leader in artificial intelligence and machine learning, with a focus on pattern recognition, biometrics, medical image analysis, and Responsible AI. Her contributions to the field have been recognized with the highest distinctions, including being named a Fellow of the IEEE, IAPR, and Indian National Academy of Engineering (INAE). She is also an ACM Distinguished Member and a member of the National Academy of AI (USA). A prolific researcher, Prof. Singh has authored over 400 publications in top-tier venues, which have garnered more than 16,200 citations (h index: 71). Her research program has attracted external grants exceeding Rs. 56 crores. She holds editorial leadership roles, serving as the Founding Co-Editor-in-Chief of ACM AI Letters and the Associate Editor-in-Chief of the prestigious Pattern Recognition Journal. Prof. Singh's work has demonstrated profound real-world impact. Her face recognition technology was instrumental in the challenging task of identifying victims of the Balasore train accident, while her deepfake detection systems were deployed to ​p​revent the​ spread of misinformation ​i​n India's 2024 general elections. Her contributions have been honored with numerous awards, including the Nasscom AI Gamechangers Award and the Facebook Award for Ethics in AI. She has supervised 14 Ph.D. students to graduation (14 ongoing PhDs) and guided over 200 M.Tech and B.Tech students. She has a strong record of service to the academic community, notably serving as the Program Committee Chair for CVPR 2022, becoming the first researcher from India to hold this position in the conference's 40-year history. She regularly serves in leadership roles for premier conferences such as ICCV, ECCV, AAAI, and major biometrics venues.

 People 
 
 
 Dip Sankar Banerjee  
 
 
 
 
 
 Dip Sankar Banerjee  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Dip Sankar Banerjee 
                         
 
                                Associate Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD. International Institute of Information Technology Hyderabad 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        dipsankarb[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 High Performance Computing; Computer Architecture; Data Analytics 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1272 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About:
                     
 
 
 Dr. Banerjee is curently working as an Associate Professor in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering of IIT Jodhpur. His research interests are broadly in the areas of high performance computing, big data analytics, accelerated computing, and computer architectures. Previously he was a post-doctoral researcher at the Network Based Computing Laboratory of The Ohio State University where he worked under the mentorship of Prof. Dhabaleswar K. Panda. Before that, he completed my Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) from International Institute of Information Technology, Hyderabad in 2014 where he worked under the guidance of Dr. Kishore Kothapalli. He was a part of the Center for Security, Theory, and Algorithmic Research (CSTAR) laboratory. His PhD research was broadly in High Performance Computing where he worked towards developing novel algorithms and implementations for heterogeneous computing platforms that could utilise multiple multi-core and many-core processors at the same time leading to better performance and higher system efficiency.

 People 
 
 
 Romi Banerjee   
 
 
 
 
 
 Romi Banerjee   
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Romi Banerjee  
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD. Calcutta University, Indian Statistical Institute 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        romibanerjee[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Embodied Cognitive Architectures; Artificial General Intelligence; Computing with Words; Natural Language Understanding 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1269 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Romi Banerjee, is an Assistant Professor, in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering, at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) - Jodhpur, an alumnus of the Diverse Intelligences Summer Institute (DISI), and part of the digital-curator team for the Society of Neuroscience of Creativity. She is also affiliated with the Centre of Education Technology and the Centre for Brain Science and Application - IIT Jodhpur.
 
It has been a while that she has been trying to understand and model how we do what we do and why we do what we do, towards the design of embodied, social (~empathetic) "thinking machines / systems". She draws inspiration from studies on the origin, evolution and development of fundamental cognitive abilities (e.g. attention, experience-formation, language acquisition, aesthetic-sense, time-space continuum, spontaneous thought, contemplation, wisdom, etc.) across all living species. 

Lately, she has begun exploring EdTech solutions for embodied learning, Digital story-telling for heritage preservation, Human-AI co-creation, and Socio-cultural biases in AI.

 People 
 
 
 Mahesh Kumar 
 
 
 
 
 
 Mahesh Kumar 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Mahesh Kumar
                         
 
                                Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: Indian Institute of Science Bangalore 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        mkumar[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 2D materials, Sensors: Gas, Photo, Bio, Ions & Gamma Ray, Memristors 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1357 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 He has
received INSA Medal for Young Scientists-2014 by Indian National Science
Academy, the MRSI Medal-2016 by Materials Research Society of India, DAE-Young
Achiever Award-2016 during Solid State Physics Symposium, Research Excellence
Award 2020 by IIT Jodhpur and Friedrich Wilhelm Bessel Research Prize by The
Alexander von Humboldt Foundation, Germany. He is recipient of the NAWA
ULAM-2024 fellowship by Polish National Agency for Academic Exchange, Brain
Pool Program-2022 by Korean National Research Foundation, Fulbright-Nehru
Academic and Professional Excellence Fellowships-2021; The Royal Society
International Exchange Award-2021; JSPS Invitation Fellowship-2021, PIFI
Visiting Scientist fellowship-2021 by Chinese Academy of Science; Duo-India
Professor Fellowship-2020, Bhaskara Advanced Solar Energy Fellowship-2018 and
INSA-DFG International Bilateral Exchange Award-2015. Recently INAE awarded him
Abdul Kalam Technology Innovation National Fellowship 2022. He is also
Editorial Board member of Nanotechnology, IEEE Sensors Journal, Bulletin of
Materials Science and Nano Express. He is also Fellow of Royal Society of
Chemistry and Institute of Physics. He is founding Member and served as Chair
of Indian National Young Academy of Sciences. He published more than 200
research articles. His research interests are focused on 2D materials,
Nanomaterials, Sensors, Semiconductor materials and devices

 Electrical Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Jai Narayan Tripathi 
 
 
 
 
 
 Jai Narayan Tripathi 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Jai Narayan Tripathi
                         
 
                                Associate Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: Indian Institute of Technology Bombay 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        jai[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 High-Speed VLSI Systems, Signal Integrity, Power Integrity 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1374 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Jai Narayan Tripathi received the Ph.D. degree in electrical engineering from Indian Institute of Technology Bombay, Mumbai, India, in 2014. He is currently an Associate Professor at Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur, Rajasthan, India; Visiting Professor at Politecnico Di Torino, Italy, as well as an Adjunct Research Professor at Carleton University, Ottawa, Canada. In past, he has worked with STMicroelectronics, India, for almost 7 years where he was involved in the design issues of various high-speed Systems-on-Chip (SoCs). He was a Visiting Scientist with the Politecnico di Torino, Turin, Italy, in 2016 and 2017, where he was also a Visiting Postdoctoral Fellow, in 2016. His current research interests include signal integrity, power integrity, electromagnetic interference/electromagnetic compatibility, metaheuristic optimization, and RF circuits. He has authored or coauthored over 100 research papers in refereed journals, as book chapters and in the proceedings of top international conferences. His papers have won best paper awards in a couple of inter-national conferences. His current research interests include signal integrity, power integrity, electromagnetic interference/electromagnetic compatibility, metaheuristic optimization, and RF circuits. Dr. Tripathi has received a couple of sponsored research grants as well as SIRE Fellowship from Science and Engineering Research Board (SERB), Govt. of India. Dr. Tripathi has served as a TPC Member for over 30 international conferences including the premier conferences such as IEEE EPEPS, IEEE SPI, VLSI Design, IEEE EDAPS, VDAT, etc. He was a recipient of the Young Investigator Training Program Research Award by Associazione Di Fondazioni E Di Casse Di Risparmio Spa (ACRI), Italy, in 2016 and 2017, consecutively. He was an Invited Speaker in IEEE EDAPS 2015, Seoul, South Korea, where he also served as a Session Co-Chair for the session High-Speed Channels and Interconnects. He has served as a TPC Co-Chair for IEEE EDAPS 2018 and will be serving for the same position in IEEE EDAPS 2024. He has been a Session Chair in several international conferences including the flagship conferences such as IEEE ICECS, IEEE EDAPS, IEEE CAFE, etc. He has delivered invited talks at various universities in India and abroad. He has served as a reviewer for many international journals, such as IEEE TCAS-I: Regular Papers, IEEE TCAS-II: Express Briefs, IEEE TVLSI, IEEE TEMC, IEEE TPES, PIER, Microelectronics Journal, etc. He has delivered invited talks at various universities and international forums. He is a member of a technical committee TC-12 EDMS of IEEE EPS Society. Dr. Tripathi was a member of the ‘Best Paper Award Committee’ of IEEE EDAPS 2020. Dr. Tripathi is currently working as an Associate Editor for IEEE Transactions on Signal and Power Integrity, IEEE Transactions on Components, Packaging and Manufacturing Technology and IEEE Electromagnetic Compatibility Magazine. In past, he has also served as an Associate Editor for IEEE Open Journal of Circuits and Systems. He is a Senior Member of IEEE.

 Electrical Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Akshay Moudgil 
 
 
 
 
 
 Akshay Moudgil 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Akshay Moudgil
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D: Indian Institute of Technology Delhi 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        akshaymoudgil[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Nano/Microelectronics, Sensors, Wearable Devices, Hybrid (Organic/Inorganic) Transistors, Bioelectronics, Optoelectronics,  Printed Electronics
 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1383 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        Akshay Moudgil
                     
 
 
 Dr. Akshay Moudgil is an Assistant Professor at the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur, leading cutting-edge research on next-generation electronic sensor systems. His work spans across sensors, hybrid transistors, printed and wearable electronics, point-of-care bioelectronic devices, and optoelectronics, with a strong emphasis on field-deployable intelligent sensing technologies for healthcare, defense, and environmental applications. Dr. Moudgil earned his Ph.D. from IIT Delhi under the prestigious  Visvesvaraya Ph.D. Fellowship , awarded by the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), Government of India. Before joining IIT Jodhpur, he served as a Research Fellow at  Nanyang Technological University (NTU), Singapore , where he worked extensively on the development of wearable biosensors utilizing state-of-the-art cleanroom facilities (class 10/100) and advanced semiconductor device fabrication techniques. He has substantial expertise in microelectronic device fabrication and characterization and has developed novel semiconductor architectures and flexible electronics for various sensing platforms. His collaborative research network includes renowned institutions such as the  University of Cambridge ,  University of Oxford ,  A*STAR Singapore ,  University of Massachusetts (UMass) ,  Technical University of Munich (TUM) , and industry partner  Insectta Pte. Ltd., Singapore . Dr. Moudgil has published over  35 peer-reviewed articles  in top-tier journals, including  ACS Nano ,  Advanced Functional Materials ,  Biosensors and Bioelectronics , and  IEEE Electron Device Letters .  He is also a contributing author to a book chapter, holds a patent in biosensor technologies, and has been honored with  Research Excellence, Teaching Excellence,  and  International Travel Awards  by IIT Delhi, IIT Jodhpur, and CSIR, respectively. He actively serves the academic community as a reviewer for prestigious journals such as  IEEE Transactions on Nanotechnology ,  ACS Nano ,  Advanced Materials ,  IEEE Sensors Journal ,  Sensors and Actuators B , and  Nano-Micro Letters . He has also reviewed for international conferences, including  IEEE NANO 2020  (Canada) and  IEEE NANO 2023  (South Korea). Dr. Moudgil is a professional member of the  Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE)  and the  Institution of Engineers (India) [IEI] .

 call

 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        Rajlaxmi Chouhan
                     
 
 
 Dr. Rajlaxmi Chouhan is an Associate Professor in the Department of Electrical Engineering at IIT Jodhpur and currently serves as the Head of the Center for Education Technology (CET). She received her PhD from IIT Kharagpur in 2015. Her current research interests include education technology and image processing. Dr. Chouhan is a Senior Member of the IEEE. For her teaching contributions, she was awarded the Teaching Excellence Award in 2019, the Dr. Vandana Sharma Memorial Award for Teaching Innovation 2021, and the Distinguished Teacher Award 2025 at IIT Jodhpur.  
 For more information, please visit  Dr. Chouhan’s webpage .

 call 
 
 0291 280 1388 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Abhishek Sharma received his
PhD in electrical engineering from IIT Kanpur in 2019. Before joining IIT
Jodhpur as an assistant professor, he worked as a postdoctoral fellow at City
University of Hong Kong, HKSAR, China, and Stevens Institute of Technology, New
Jersey, United States. He was also associated with the Advanced Electronics
Group at the National Institute of Standards and Technology, Maryland, as an
international research associate from Nov. 2023 to Nov. 2024.  Dr. Sharma
received the URSI-InRaSS Young Indian Radio Scientist Award in 2019 at URSI
Asia-Pacific Radio Science Conference held in New Delhi. He has
authored/co-authored over 50 journal and conference papers. He is currently
serving as an associate editor for Electronics Letters and an active reviewer
for numerous journals, including IEEE, Optica, and IOP. Dr. Sharma is a senior
member of URSI and a member of IEEE, Optica, and APS.

                        About
                     
 
 
  Prof. Rajpurohit is a dynamic
academician and researcher adept at Renewable Energy and Power Electronics

project-related activities. Prof. Rajpurohit has more than 15 years of
involvement with the energy sector in various capacities: graduate-level
teaching in energy, research, and industrial experience.

 Electrical Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Manoj Gupta 
 
 
 
 
 
 Manoj Gupta 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Manoj Gupta
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD: Nanyang Technological University, Singapore 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        manojgupta[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Terahertz Communication, In-Plane Silicon Photonics, Metamaterials, Low-Dimensional Materials, Sensing Technologies, Hybrid Electronic-Photonic Integration 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1365 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Manoj Gupta is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Electrical Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jodhpur, Rajasthan, India. Prior to joining IIT Jodhpur, he served as a Program Manager for the TERACOMM initiative, where he led the development of a terahertz 6G communication testbed during his postdoctoral research at the Centre for Disruptive Photonic Technologies (CDPT), Nanyang Technological University (NTU), Singapore. Dr. Gupta received his Ph.D. in Terahertz Photonics from NTU Singapore in January 2019. He earned his B.Tech. degree in Electrical Engineering from the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Roorkee in 2007. In 2025, he established the Millimeter-wave and Terahertz Microsystems Laboratory (MMTμL) in the Department of Electrical Engineering at IIT Jodhpur. His research focuses on terahertz devices and systems for on-chip and free-space communication, imaging, and sensing applications, with an emphasis on millimeter-wave and next-generation 6G technologies. He has published his research in leading high-impact international journals (Nature Materials, Nature Communications, Advanced Materials) and conferences in the areas of terahertz photonics, metamaterials, millimeter-wave systems, and 6G communications, and actively contributes to advancing terahertz-enabled technologies through both fundamental and applied research.

 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 I am an Assistant Professor in the department of Electrical Engineering,
IIT Jodhpur. I received my doctoral degree from IIT Delhi. My research is
focused on analog and mixed signal circuit design, CMOS image sensors and
intelligent circuits and systems. I have designed and characterized various
integrated circuits like comparators, LDO, ADCs and CMOS image sensors for high
speed and low power applications.

 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Aashish Mathur 
 
 
 
 
 
 Aashish Mathur 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Aashish Mathur
                         
 
                                Associate Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: Indian Institute of Technology Delhi 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        aashishmathur[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Optical Wireless Communications, Physical Layer Security, 5G and Beyond Communications 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1362 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Aashish
Mathur (Senior Member, IEEE) received the B.E. degree (Hons.) in electronics
and instrumentation engineering from the Birla Institute of Technology and
Science (BITS) Pilani, Pilani, India, in 2011, the M.Tech. degree in
telecommunication technology and management from the Indian Institute of
Technology (IIT) Delhi, New Delhi, India, in 2013, and the Ph.D. degree in power line communications from the Department of Electrical
Engineering, IIT Delhi, in 2016. He was a Software Engineer with Intel Technology India Pvt. Ltd., Bengaluru,
India, briefly before joining IIT Delhi for his Ph.D. in 2013. He is currently
an Associate Professor with the Department of Electrical Engineering, IIT
Jodhpur, Jodhpur, India. He has also worked as an Assistant Professor with the
Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering, BITS Pilani and the Department of Electronics Engineering, IIT (BHU) Varanasi,
Varanasi, India. He was engaged as a Visiting Faculty with the Indian Institute
of Information Technology Kota, Kota, India, for the second semester, from 2018
to 2019. He served as an Adjunct Faculty (part-time) with IIT Delhi from 2019
to 2022 and 2022-2025. His research interests include visible light
communications, free-space optical communications, power line communications,
and physical layer security. Dr. Mathur received the Best Student Paper Award for his co-authored paper in
2017 Conference on Decision and Game Theory for Security (GameSec 2017),
Vienna, Austria. He was awarded the Early Career Research Award by the Science
and Engineering Research Board, DST, Government of India, in 2019. He was
awarded the Teaching Excellence Award at IIT Jodhpur in 2019. He was recognized
as an Exemplary Reviewer 2021 for IEEE Transactions on Communications and IEEE
Open Journal of the Communications Society for 2024. He is serving as an
Associate Editor of IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society.

 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Viswanathan Ramachandran is an Assistant Professor at the Department of Electrical Engineering, Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur. He received the Ph.D. degree in electrical engineering from the Indian Institute of Technology Bombay. Subsequently, he has done postdoctoral stints at Technical University of Eindhoven, Netherlands as well as KTH Royal Institute of Technology, Sweden. His research interests are in information theory and communications, particularly in multi-terminal settings, with applications in wireless and optical domains. He was a recipient of the Award for Excellence in Ph.D. research from IIT Bombay in 2021, and also the Marie-Curie individual fellowship 2022 from the European Commission.

 biotech 
 
 Microwave and millimeterwave Antennas, active and passive circuits 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1361 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 I am an Associate Professor in the Department of Electrical Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur. I did my PhD in the Department of Electrical Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology, Kanpur (IITK). I completed my Master of Engineering (M.E.) in the Department of Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering at Bengal Engineering and Science University, Shibpur (BESUS) (currently Indian Institute of Engineering Science and Technology, Shibpur).
 
At IIT Jodhpur, our research group has developed novel mmwave solutions using Substrate Integrated Coaxial Line (SICL) technology. Apart from this, we are extensively working on SIW antennas and circuits, mmwave active circuits, reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS), etc. Apart from that, our research group is extensively working on developing telecommunication standards in leading standards organizations

 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Bhupendra Singh Reniwal 
 
 
 
 
 
 Bhupendra Singh Reniwal 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Bhupendra Singh Reniwal
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D: Indian Institute of Technology Indore 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        bhupendrar[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Spintronic and Advanced Spin Based STTRAMs for Future Computing GPIO, CFIO, Design IBIS Model, ESD and its implications to overall IO functionality AI, Hardware Accelerators and IMC Circuits & Architectures Design Variation/Radiation Aware Preferential Design of Embedded Memory for Energy Efficient and Robust Space Applications
 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1396 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 I am currently working as
Assistant Professor in the Department of Electrical Engineering, Indian
Institute of Technology Jodhpur, India. Post Ph.D. I have worked as a Senior
Product Development Engineer, Semiconductor Vertical in UST Global Bangalore, India
Mixed-Signal IP Solution Group (MIG) at Intel Corporation Penang, Malaysia, and
Systems & Technology Group, ASIC Foundry, IBM Bangalore where I was
involved on developing Energy Efficient Memory Architecture, I/O Circuit
Design, and its pre-silicon validation, for Internet of Things (IoT),
applications in subnanometric trigate FinFET processes. In IBM he was involved
in R&D on low power methodology definition at Schematic2GDS level for
sub-nanometric nodes, especially for FinFET memory design. I also served the
Department of Electronics & Communication Engineering IIITDM Kancheepuram
and BITS Pilani, as an Assistant Professor from Nov-2019 to Oct-2022 and
May-Dec 2017, respectively. Recipient of the prestigious SIRE-2022 Faculty
Fellowship from the Department of Science & Technology and joined the
University of Virginia, USA as a Visiting Faculty. He received the User Design
Best Research Paper Award in the IEEE 29th International Conference on VLSI
Design and the Best Poster Presentation Award for Ultra Low Power SRAM Design
in Ramanujan Conclave 2016. Recipient of the International Travel Award as
early recognition in Solid State Circuit Design from the Association of
Computing Machinery (ACM), NY, USA, and DST.​

 People 
 
 
 Ravi Yadav 
 
 
 
 
 
 Ravi Yadav 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Ravi Yadav
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph. D.: Indian Institute of Technology Kharagpur 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        raviyee[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Power system dynamics, Wide area monitoring systems, anomaly detection and characterization, AI/ML applications to power systems, and cyber-attack modelling and diagnosis 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1380 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        Ravi Yadav
                     
 
 
 Dr. Ravi Yadav completed his M.tech and P.h.D degree from the electrical engineering department at IIT Kharagpur. His doctoral work, titled "Power system monitoring and analysis using synchrophasor data" was under the guidance of Prof. Ashok Kumar Pradhan, IIT Kharagpur.  After his doctorate, he worked as a subject matter expert (WAMS) and product owner at General Electric (GE) Hyderabad center, where he contributed towards R&D of WAMS-based analytics products.  He received the GE-Impact Award and GE-Spotlight Awards for contributions to WAMS-based analytics products.  He has experience with industry-accepted WAMS and data exchange standards, signal processing algorithms, software development, microservices, database management, machine/deep learning tools, etc.  His current research interests include power system dynamics, wide-area measurement systems, dynamic interactions of large-scale renewable integration, anomaly detection, AI/ML applications to power system automation, and cyber-attack modeling and diagnosis.

 स्मारक दिवस @ भा. प्रौ. सं. जोधपुर   
 
 
 भर्ती   
 
 
 पत्र-व्यवहार   
 
 
 सूचना का अधिकार   
 
 
 निविदाओं   
 
 
 टेकस्केप   
 
 
 संपर्क   
 
 
 पुरानी वेबसाइट   
 
 
 राष्ट्रीय साइबर अपराध रिपोर्टिंग पोर्टल   
 
 
 भा. प्रौ. सं. जोधपुर कैसे पहुंचें?   
 
 
 संस्थान रिपोजिटरी   
 
 
 अनुदान   
 
 
 वेब नीति   
 
 
 वेब सूचना प्रबंधक   
 
 
 प्रतिक्रिया   
 
 
 CERT-इन   
 
 
 सहायता   
 
 
 एनआईआरएफ   
 
 
 आंतरिक समिति   
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 इंट्रानेट लिंक   
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 कॉपीराइट © 2025 सभी अधिकार सुरक्षित | यह पोर्टल  डिजिटल इन्फ्रास्ट्रक्चर एंड ऑटोमेशन , भा. प्रौ. सं. जोधपुर द्वारा स्वामित्व, डिज़ाइन और विकसित किया गया है।   एन.एच. 62, नागौर रोड, कारवार, जोधपुर - 342030 राजस्थान (भारत) 
 किसी भी टिप्पणी / पूछताछ / प्रतिक्रिया के लिए, कृपया  डब्ल्यू आई एम  को ईमेल करें।

 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Sandeep Kumar Yadav 
 
 
 
 
 
 Sandeep Kumar Yadav 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Sandeep Kumar Yadav
                         
 
                                Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        sy[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Signal Processing, Machine Learning, Condition Monitoring, Image Processing
 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1354 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Sandeep Kumar Yadav is a Professor in the
Department of Electrical Engineering at IIT Jodhpur. He holds a Ph.D. in
Electrical Engineering and an M.Tech. in Nuclear Engineering and Technology
from IIT Kanpur, and a B.Tech. in Electrical Engineering from H.B.T.I. Kanpur.
His research interests include signal processing, condition monitoring, blind
signal demodulation, image processing, artificial neural networks, and machine
learning. He has led several funded research projects in collaboration with agencies
such as DRDO, IBM, UNICEF, DST, and MHRD. Dr. Yadav has received numerous
accolades, including the Young Scientist Award (System Society of India, 2011),
the Graphical System Design Achievement Award (National Instruments, 2012), the
IBM Shared University Research Award (2014), and the Teaching Excellence Award
from IIT Jodhpur (2020). He regularly teaches undergraduate and postgraduate
courses such as Signals and Systems, Digital Signal Processing, Intelligent
Instrumentation, Machine Learning, Adaptive Signal Processing, and Circuit
Theory. He is actively involved in mentoring Ph.D. and M.Tech. students in
emerging areas of intelligent systems and signal analysis.

 Electrical Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Nishant Kumar 
 
 
 
 
 
 Nishant Kumar 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Nishant Kumar
                         
 
                                Associate Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph. D.: Indian Institute of Technology, Delhi 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        nishantkumar[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Power Electronics and Advanced Control; Power System Optimization ; Renewable Energy Generation; Microgrid & EV; Cybersecurity for Electric Power Infrastructure 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1382 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        Nishant Kumar
                     
 
 
 Dr.
Nishant Kumar is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Electrical
Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jodhpur, with research
interests spanning power electronics, smart grid technologies, renewable energy
integration, electric vehicle infrastructure, and digital twin-based energy
systems. He received his Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering from IIT Delhi, where
his doctoral work was recognized with the national Best Thesis Award. He later
completed his postdoctoral research at the National University of Singapore,
contributing to innovative solutions for sustainable port electrification,
which also led to the establishment of a technology startup. As the Co-founder
and Chief Technology Officer of SCPscan Pvt. Ltd., he is actively involved in
commercializing intelligent energy monitoring and control solutions. 
 Dr. Kumar has an extensive academic and research portfolio, with over 100
publications, including 38 in reputed IEEE Transactions journals. He holds
several patents for his pioneering work in power quality control, electric
vehicle charging, and solar PV systems, with six Indian patents granted and one
filed in the USA. His work has earned wide recognition, including the Young
Researcher Award and Research Excellence Award from IIT Jodhpur, and the Global
Distinguished Young Scientist Award from IEEE IAS. He was also named among the
World’s Top 2% Scientists by Stanford University in the fields of Electrical
& Electronics Engineering and Energy Science. 
 His current research is supported by national funding agencies and focuses on
developing AI-enabled digital twin frameworks for solar power plants and
advanced power electronic systems for grid resilience and electric mobility. A
committed educator and mentor, he supervises multiple Ph.D. scholars and
actively participates in national and international collaborations, including
projects with Imperial College London, the University of Warwick, and MIT USA.
He has delivered more than sixty invited expert lectures and keynote talks, and
serves as an Associate Editor for IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I:
Regular Papers. Dr. Kumar remains dedicated to advancing smart and sustainable
energy technologies through a strong synergy of research, innovation, and
academic leadership

 Nitin Bhatia 
 
 
 
 
 
 Nitin Bhatia

 school

                                        nitin[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Fiber Optics, Photonics, Multimode Interference devices, Multimode Optical Beams, Few mode fiber devices, Optical Sensors 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1366 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Nitin Bhatia received the B.Tech.
degree from Rajasthan Technical University, Kota, Rajasthan, and the Ph.D.
degree from the Department of Electrical Engineering, Indian Institute of
Technology (IIT) Bombay, Mumbai, India. In May 2019, he joined the Department
of Electrical Engineering, Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur, Jodhpur,
India, where he is currently an Associate Professor. Before joining IIT
Jodhpur, he also served as an Assistant Professor at NIIT University,
Rajasthan, India. He was a Postdoctoral Fellow with the Department of ECE,
Indian Institute of Technology Kharagpur, Kharagpur, India. His research
interests include Fiber Optics, Photonics, Multimode Interference devices,
Multimode Optical Beams, Few mode fiber devices, Optical Sensors.

 biotech

 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About Us
                     
 
 
 Saakshi works in the development of gas- and bio-sensors based on nanomaterials, heterostructures, and MEMS. Before joining IIT Jodhpur, she worked as a SERB Research Scientist and INSPIRE Faculty at the Centre for Biomedical Engineering and the Centre for Applied Research in Electronics (CARE), IIT Delhi, respectively, from 2013 to 2019. She was working as an Assistant Professor at the Department of Electrical Engineering, IIT Jodhpur, from September 2019 to June 2023.

 Ph.D.: Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur

 biotech 
 
 Sparse Signal Processing in 5G/6G Wireless Systems, mmWave and Tera-Hertz Communication, Orthogonal Time-Frequency Space (OTFS), Joint Radar and Communication (RadCom), Optimization and Machine Learning, Low resolution MIMO Communications 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1387 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Prof.
Suraj Srivastava (MIEEE, MIET, MIETE, YA-INAE) received the Ph.D. degree in
Electrical Engineering from Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur, India, and
M.Tech. degree in Electronics and Communication Engineering from Indian
Institute of Technology Roorkee, India. From October 2022 to November 2023, he
worked as a senior lead engineer in Qualcomm India Pvt. Ltd., where he was
involved in the development of 5G-IoT modems. Previously, he was also employed
as a Staff-I systems design engineer with Broadcom Research India Pvt. Ltd. and
as a lead engineer with Samsung Research India, where he worked on designing
Layer-2 of the 3G UMTS/WCDMA/HSDPA modems. He was awarded the prestigious
Qualcomm Innovation Fellowship (QIF) twice for the years 2018 and 2022, and
received the Outstanding Ph.D. Thesis award and Silver Medal from IIT Kanpur.
In 2023 he joined the Department of Electrical Engineering, Indian Institute of
Technology Jodhpur, where he is currently an Assistant Professor. He has been
honoured with the prestigious INAE Young Associateship 2024 in recognition to
his outstanding contributions in Wireless Communications

 Deepak kumar M Fulwani 
 
 
 
 
 
 Deepak kumar M Fulwani

 school

                                        df[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Microgrids, Power Electronics, Control Theory 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1355 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Deepak
Fulwani received the Ph.D. degree in control systems from the Indian Institute
of Technology (IIT) Bombay, Mumbai, India, in 2009. He was an assistant
professor at IIT Guwahati, Guwahati, India, and served as a visiting faculty at
IIT Kharagpur, Kharagpur, India. He is currently a professor with the
Department of Electrical Engineering, IIT Jodhpur, Jodhpur, India. He has
authored or coauthored several articles in reputed international journals and
conferences. His current research interests are power electronics, microgrids,
and control systems. Prof. Fulwani was a guest associate editor for a special
issue on structured DC microgrids for the IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected
Topics in Power Electronics in 2017. He was an associate editor for IEEE
Transactions on Industry Applications from 2019 to 2022. He is currently an
Editorial Board Member of Nature Scientific Reports and Associate Editor of
IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics since June 2024. He was the
recipient of the Excellence Award for his Ph.D. thesis work in the IDP program
in Systems and Control in the 48th Convocation of IIT Bombay. He was also
awreded Teaching Excellence Award (for class size 30 to 60) by IIT Jodhpur in
2020

 Electrical Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Vineeth V 
 
 
 
 
 
 Vineeth V 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Vineeth V
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph. D.: Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        vineeth[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Power Systems, Active Distribution System Operational Planning, Smart Grids, Distributed Energy Resources, Optimization 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1399 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Vineeth Vijayan is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Electrical Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur and a Faculty Affiliate at the Rishabh Centre for Innovation and Research in Clean Energy. He obtained his Ph.D. from IIT Kanpur (2023), M.Tech. from the University of Kerala, Thiruvananthapuram (2013), and B.E. from Anna University, Chennai (2010). From November 2023 to April 2025, he was a Postdoctoral Researcher at the Center for Energy Systems Research, Tennessee Tech University, USA. His research focuses on the operational planning of active electric distribution networks, including renewable energy integration, demand-side management, Volt/VAR control, and topology optimization. His emerging interests include grid-scale energy storage systems, e-mobility, cyber-resilience, and quantum applications in power systems. Dr. Vijayan has received several notable recognitions, including the Dr. Shankar Dayal Sharma Medal for best all-round post-graduating student of IIT Kanpur, the Trilok Chandra Goel Memorial Gold Medal for research contributing to sustainable energy, and the IIT Kanpur Excellence Award for leadership in student affairs (all in 2023). He was also awarded the IIT Kanpur Gymkhana Blues (2022), the DST AWSAR Award (2021), and the IEEE India Council Outstanding Volunteer Award (2020). He has published research in reputed venues, including IEEE Transactions and leading international conferences. An active IEEE volunteer, he has served as Secretary of the IEEE Power & Energy Society (Asia-Pacific Region, Region 10) and as Chair of the Educational and Technical Activities Subcommittee of the IEEE PES Global Student Chapters Committee.
