 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        Binod Kumar
                     
 
 
 Binod
Kumar is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Electrical Engineering at
the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur (IITJ). He earned his Ph.D. and
Master's degrees in Electrical Engineering from the Indian Institute of
Technology Bombay (IITB) in 2020, and holds a Bachelor's degree in Electronics
& Communication Engineering from the National Institute of Technology
Silchar (NITS).  ​ At IIT Jodhpur, Dr. Kumar teaches courses such as Embedded Systems, Digital
Systems Laboratory, Hardware-Software Co-design, Formal Verification, Digital
ASIC Design Lab, Processor Design, Network-On-Chip, and ML in VLSI CAD. He has
been recognized with the Distinguished Teacher Award from IIT Jodhpur in
September 2024 and the Qualcomm Faculty Award in April 2024

 biotech 
 
 Cooperative Control, Multi-Agent Systems, Autonomous Systems, Robotics, Nonlinear Control, Cyber-Physical Systems
 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1371 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Anoop
Jain is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Electrical Engineering at
the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur. He received his B.Tech. degree in
2009 from Krishna Institute of Engineering and Technology, Ghaziabad,
affiliated with Uttar Pradesh Technical University. He completed his M.E. in
2011 and Ph.D. in 2017 from the Department of Aerospace Engineering at the
Indian Institute of Science (IISc), Bangalore. From 2017 to 2019, he was a
Postdoctoral Fellow at the Faculty of Aerospace Engineering, Technion – Israel
Institute of Technology, where his research was supported by the Lady Davis
Fellowship. His research interests include cooperative control of multi-agent
systems, robotics, cyber-physical systems, and nonlinear control theory.

 Electrical Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Saurav Kumar 
 
 
 
 
 
 Saurav Kumar 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Saurav Kumar
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor 
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD. The University of Texas at Dallas 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        sauravk[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Wearable Robotics, Nonlinear Control, Modeling of legged robots, Prosthetic Legs, Exoskeletons, Biomechanics. 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 290 1386 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Saurav Kumar received the Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering from The University of Texas at Dallas in 2020 under the supervision of Prof. Nicholas Gans and Prof. Robert D. Gregg. He was a postdoctoral fellow in the Robotics Institute at Carnegie Mellon University with Prof. Hartmut Geyer (2020–2022) and subsequently a Research Scientist at the University of Texas at Arlington Research Institute (2023–2025). He was a finalist for the Best Student Paper Award at the 2017 IEEE Conference on Control Technology and Applications (CCTA). Dr. Kumar joined the Department of Electrical Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jodhpur as an Assistant Professor in 2025.  

At IIT Jodhpur, Dr. Kumar directs the Wearable Robotics and Control (WeRoCon) Laboratory.  His research lies at the intersection of robotics, control, and biomechanics, with a focus on lower-limb wearable robots, including powered prosthetic legs and lower-limb exoskeletons. He develops nonlinear, adaptive, and data-driven control methods that personalize assistance to each user while ensuring robust, real-time performance across everyday terrains and tasks.

 Electrical Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Harshit Agarwal 
 
 
 
 
 
 Harshit Agarwal 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Harshit Agarwal
                         
 
                                Associate Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph. D.: Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        agarwalh[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Semiconductor Device Modeling and Characterization, RF microelectronics, NVM, ESD Modeling 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1376 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 My research group (Nano Devices
and Application Lab) specializes in compact modeling for analog/RF applications
of advanced semiconductor devices, including bulk MOSFETs, high-voltage MOSFETs
(HV-MOS), and state-of-the-art technologies such as FinFETs, Nanosheets, and
Gate-All-Around (GAA) FETs. I have over a decade of experience in developing
industry-standard compact models, which are currently utilized in commercial
design workflows. This includes contributions to BSIM-Bulk, BSIM-Bulk based
high-voltage models, BSIM-CMG, among others. We are developing 2d memories for
hardware security, developing industry standard compact model for ElectroStatic
Discharge FETs, and investigating semiconductor behaviour at cryogenic
temperatures for quantum applications.

 Rajendra Nagar 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Rajendra Nagar
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: Indian Institute of Technology Gandhinagar 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        rn[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Digital Geometry Processing, 3D Computer Vision, Multidimensional Signal Processing on 3D Shapes 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1369 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr.
Rajendra Nagar is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Electrical
Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur, India. He received
his B.Tech. degree in Electrical Engineering from the Indian Institute of
Technology Jodhpur in 2013 and his Ph.D. degree in Electrical Engineering from
the Indian Institute of Technology Gandhinagar in 2019. He was awarded a gold
medal for the best academic performance in B.Tech. Electrical Engineering at
IIT Jodhpur in 2013. He received the NCC 2019 Best Ph.D. Thesis Award, as well
as the Best Ph.D. Thesis Research Award at IIT Gandhinagar, 2019. His research
interests include 3D Computer Vision, Digital Geometry Processing, and
Multidimensional Signal Processing on 3D Shapes

 People 
 
 
 Amit Bhardwaj 
 
 
 
 
 
 Amit Bhardwaj 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Amit Bhardwaj
                         
 
                                Associate Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: Indian Institute of Technology Bombay 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        amitb[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Haptics 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1372 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        Amit Bhardwaj
                     
 
 
 Amit Bhardwaj received his PhD degree from the Department of Electrical
Engineering at IIT Bombay in June 2016. From June 2016 to January 2017, he was
a research associate at IIT Bombay, Mumbai. From February 2017- December
2017, he was an Alexander von Humboldt postdoctoral fellow at the Chair of Media
Technology, Technical University of Munich, Germany. After that, he joined the
Pohang University of Science and Technology (POSTECH), South Korea as a
postdoctoral researcher. Currently, he is working as an associate professor in the
Department of Electrical Engineering, at IIT Jodhpur.
He has research experience in the area of virtual reality and multi-sensory human
perception.  He has published his research work in top journals and conferences in
the area of haptics and robotics. He has also published a book in the area of
perception

 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Kunwar Aditya 
 
 
 
 
 
 Kunwar Aditya 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Kunwar Aditya
                         
 
                                Associate Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph. D.: Ontario Tech University (UOIT), Oshawa, ON, Canada 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        kunwar[dot]aditya[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Power Electronics, Automotive Electronics, Wireless Power Transfer, Transportation Electrification, Electrical Energy Storage Systems 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1384 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Kunwar
Aditya received the B.E. degree from Bharati Vidyapeeth University, Pune,
India; the M.Tech. degree from the Indian Institute of Technology (Banaras
Hindu University), Varanasi, India; and the Ph.D. degree from the Ontario Tech
University (UOIT), Oshawa, ON, Canada, in 2009, 2012, and 2016,
respectively—all in electrical engineering. 
 From July to November 2017, he was a Research Fellow at the Rolls-Royce
Corporate Lab at Nanyang Technological University, Singapore. In December 2017,
he joined BorgWarner Inc. in Canada as a Hardware Engineer, where he worked on
the development of electric vehicle motor controllers for 800V battery packs.
In April 2019, he joined Vitesco Technologies Canada as an Associate Electrical
Engineer.There he led the development of sensors and actuators for electric vehicle
thermal management systems. 
 Currently, he is working as an Associate Professor in the Department of
Electrical Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jodhpur,
India. 
 His research interests include power electronics and control applied to
wireless charging, electrical energy storage systems, and transportation
electrification. Dr. Aditya was awarded the IIT (BHU) Gold Medal for securing
the highest academic standing among all electrical engineering students of the
2012 cohort. He also received the Outstanding Thesis Award for his Ph.D. work
from the Faculty of Engineering and Applied Science at UOIT.

                                        pradyumna[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Wireless communication networks, Next-generation WiFi networks, Integrated Sensing and Communication (ISAC) networks, 5G/6G mobile networks, IoT networks, Network Economics, Game Theory and Mechanism Design. 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1400 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Pradyumna Kumar Bishoyi received the Ph.D. degree from Indian Institute of Technology Kharagpur, India, in 2022. He is currently an Assistant Professor in the Department of Electrical Engineering, Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur. Prior to joining IIT Jodhpur, he was a Postdoctoral Researcher with the Mobile Communications and Computing (MCC) group at RWTH Aachen University, Germany, from July 2022 to September 2025. His current research includes performance modeling and analysis, resource allocation, distributed optimization, game theory and mechanism design,  all within the context of wireless networks, integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) networks, mobile edge computing (MEC), IoT, and 5G/6G mobile systems. Currently, he is serving as an Associate Editor of the EURASIP Journal on Wireless Communications and Networking.

 Arun Kumar Singh 
 
 
 
 
 
 Arun Kumar Singh

                                Associate Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: Telecom Paris, France 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        singhak[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Wireless Communications, Communication Theory, Spread spectrum systems, and Satellite based navigation systems 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1358 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Arun
Kumar Singh (M'14-SM'20) received the M. Tech. degree in Communication and
Radar engineering from the Indian Institute of Technology Delhi, India, in
December 2002 and the Ph.D. degree in Electronics and Communications from the
Télécom Paris (also known as École Nationale Supérieure des
Télécommunications), France, in February 2012. He is an Associate Professor in
the Department of Electrical Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology
Jodhpur, which he joined in 2013. Earlier, from April 2003 to January 2009, he
worked as a scientist at Space Applications Centre, Indian Space Research
Organization, India, where he was part of the system engineering team for
communication and navigation satellite payloads. From May 2012 to April 2013 he
was a research engineer at the Mobile Communications Department, EURECOM,
France.

 Electrical Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Bandi Ravi Kumar 
 
 
 
 
 
 Bandi Ravi Kumar 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Bandi Ravi Kumar
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D. – Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Kanpur 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        ravik[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Power system protection, Power system monitoring (State estimation, Low frequency oscillations monitoring, etc), Synchrophasors and their applications for power system, Optimization. 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 2351 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Bandi Ravi Kumar completed his Ph.D. from the electrical engineering department at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Kanpur in December 2023. He did his Ph.D. in power system protection under the guidance of Prof. Abheejeet Mohapatra and Prof. Saikat Chakrabarti, IIT Kanpur.  
Post Ph.D., he worked at EEGrid analytics from Jan-March 2024 as a power system planning engineer. Later, he worked at Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) Research as a Scientist from April 2024-Dec 2025.  
He received "Grid India Power Systems Award (GIPSA) 2024 in the Doctoral category (formerly known as POSOCO Power Systems Award).  
His current research interests include Power system protection, Power system monitoring (State estimation, Low frequency oscillations monitoring, etc), Synchrophasors and their applications for power system, Optimization.
 He is a member of IEEE and the reviewer for IEEE Trans. on Power Delivery, IEEE Trans. on Instrumentation & Measurement, IEEE Trans. on Industrial Informatics, and IJEPES.
 He has completed three consultancy projects during his Ph.D., namely,  Technical Verification of Electrical Relay Settings for 750V DC Traction System and 220kV and 33kV AC Systems in Kanpur Metro Project, Uttar Pradesh Metro Rail Corporation Ltd. (UPMRC).  Development of DSP algorithms for numeric protection relay, Synergy Systems and Solutions.  Validating Similarity of Power Transformers for Short Circuit Testing With Reference To IEC 60076(2006), Part5, Annexure B, Technical Associates Ltd.

 Electrical Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Shree Prakash Tiwari 
 
 
 
 
 
 Shree Prakash Tiwari 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Shree Prakash Tiwari
                         
 
                                Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: Indian Institute of Technology Bombay 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        sptiwari[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Flexible Electronics, Microelectronics, Sustainable and Biodegradable Systems
 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1356 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Shree Prakash Tiwari is currently
working as Professor in Department of Electrical Engineering. Prior to joining
IIT Jodhpur as a faculty member in 2011, Prof. Tiwari had worked as a
Postdoctoral Fellow in School of ECE at Georgia Institute of Technology,
Atlanta, USA, for 3 years (2008-2011). He had done his Ph.D. from EE
Department, IIT Bombay from 2003 to 2008, during which he had also worked at
NTU Singapore for about 7 months. Prof. Tiwari is a Senior Member of IEEE and
serves as selected member of technical committees on “Flexible Electronics and
Displays” and “Electronic Materials” of IEEE Electron Devices Society. Prof.
Tiwari was Visvesvaraya Young Faculty Research Fellow of Ministry of
Electronics and Information Technology, Government of India, for 2018-2023. He
was part of Indian Delegate to attend Indo-German Frontiers of Engineering
Symposium in Bremen, Germany (2022) organized by Alexander von Humboldt
Foundation and Department of Science and Technology, followed by CONNECT
Follow-up Programme Award by Humboldt Foundation and IGSTC-CONNECT Plus Award
to visit and work in Germany in 2023. At IIT Jodhpur, he leads the Flexible
Large Area Microelectronics (FLAME) Research Group, with focus towards
biodegradable electronics. He was recently conferred with “Institute Award for
Research Excellence-2024, Senior Researcher Award (Engineering)” from IIT
Jodhpur. He has co-authored over 160 research articles, including more than 80
in journals of high repute with many in IEEE, ACS, and Wiley Journals

### faculty_MME | https://www.iitj.ac.in/People/Profile/982ad0e4-22d8-453d-b568-e8375cce2ed8 ###

 
 Amitava Banerjee | Materials Engineering | IIT Jodhpur

 Materials Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Amitava Banerjee 
 
 
 
 
 
 Amitava Banerjee 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Amitava Banerjee
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: Uppsala University, Sweden 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        amitava[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 DFT, Molecular Dynamics, Crystal structures prediction, Hydrogen, and oxygen evolution reaction (HER, OER), 2D catalysts, Solar cell, 2D anodes, Battery Thermodynamics and Kinetics modeling, Defect engineering, Oxide growth

 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1564 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Amitava Banerjee is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Materials Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur (IITJ). He joined IITJ in December 2021 after completing his postdoc as a Director's Postdoctoral Fellow in the Materials Science and Technology Division at Los Alamos National Laboratory, USA. Dr. Banerjee earned his Ph.D. in materials modeling for energy conversion and storage from Uppsala University, Sweden, in 2019. For his Ph.D. thesis, he received the Graduate Student Silver Award from the Materials Research Society (MRS), USA and the "Bjurzons Premium" prize from Uppsala University for the best Ph.D. thesis in the natural sciences. He completed his M.Tech. from IIT Kanpur, with CGPA of 10 out of 10, and was honored with both the Academic Excellence Award and the Gold Medal from IIT Kanpur, as well as recognition from the Indian Institute of Metals (IIM) with Gold Medal. Dr. Banerjee also holds an M.Sc. in Materials Science, graduating with first position with distinction from Sardar Patel University in Gujarat. Additionally, he earned a B.Sc. in Industrial Chemistry, finishing in first class—first position from Ramakrishna Mission Vidyamandira in Belur Math, under Calcutta University. 
 
 
 
 
                        Overview of Research Domain
                     
 
 
 Dr. Banerjee specializes in density functional theory, molecular dynamics, and
materials informatics for designing catalysts for solar fuel production and
storage. He employs genetic algorithms to screen organic structures for battery
electrodes and studies defect thermodynamics and kinetics to understand oxide
growth. He has developed several computational methodologies for designing
energy materials. In a board, Dr. Banerjee's research focuses on materials
modelling for oxide growth (material degradation), energy conversion, and
storage using the avant-grade computational method. More specifically on: 
 Random structure search  
 Solar fuel
     production, photocatalyst- Water Splitting 
 Catalytic reaction mechanism
     (HER, OER, ORR, NRR, CO2 reduction) 
 Hybrid perovskites solar cell-structural
     modification and stability 
 Organic crystal structure
     search for organic green battery 
 Thermodynamics and kinetics of
     battery/supercapacitor material 
 Oxide growth: Formation and
     transport of cationic and anionic defects in the oxide 
 Diffusion through metal-oxide
     interface and grain-boundary 
 2D Materials, 3D porous structures
     for various application 
 Nano-grinding/surface finishing of
     ceramic materials

Friction Stir Welding and Processing

 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Pranay Ranjan 
 
 
 
 
 
 Pranay Ranjan 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Pranay Ranjan
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: Indian Institute of Technology Patna 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        pranay[dot]ranjan[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 2D Materials: Synthesis and Applications in Sensing and Environmental Remediation
 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1563 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 I did my Ph.D. from the Indian Institute of Technology Patna in Feb 2019 and then carried out my post-doctoral research at the UAE University where I worked in Dept.of Physics and National Energy and Water Center respectively. My research thrust includes exploring novel large-scale synthetic approaches for various 2D materials (including graphene, graphene oxide, borophene, borophene oxide, alpha lead oxide, white lead, and their hybrids) and their frontline applications in molecular as well as gas sensing, wettability manipulation, electronics/optoelectronics/spintronic devices, and environmental remediation.

 Materials Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Bhabani K Satapathy 
 
 
 
 
 
 Bhabani K Satapathy 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Bhabani K Satapathy
                         
 
                                Professor 
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D: Indian Institute of Technology, Delhi 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                         satapathy[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Morphology and Phase Behaviour of Block Copolymers, Polymer Blends and Composites, Micromechanics, Fracture and Fatigue of Polymer Nano-composites, Tribology of Polymer Based Materials, Biotribology, Thermo-Mechanical Behaviour of Biomaterials 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1566 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Bhabani K. Satapathy is a professor in the Department of Materials Engineering. He did his Ph.D. from IIT Delhi in the
area of polymer composites and specifically on the tribological aspects of
composites for automotive braking applications. He subsequently pursued his
research career as a Postdoctoral fellow at Leibniz Institute of Polymer
Research Dresden (IPF Dresden) and in Mechanics of Functional Materials (MfM)
group of Institute of Material Science and Technology at Friedrich Schiller
University Jena, Germany. He also has an M. Tech. degree in Polymer Science and
Engineering from IIT Delhi. His main research interests are on phase behaviour,
tribology and fracture mechanics of polymer based blends, composites, block
copolymers and allied materials.  Research Interests: Morphology and Phase Behaviour of Block Copolymers, Polymer
Blends and Composites, Micromechanics, Fracture and Fatigue of Polymer
Nano-composites, Tribology of Polymer Based Materials, Biotribology,
Thermo-Mechanical Behaviour of Biomaterials.

                            Sumeet Rajesh Khanna
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: Indian Institute of Science Bengaluru 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        sumeetk[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Magnetic materials for EV, ICME (Integrated Computational Materials Engineering), AI applications in materials, CALPHAD, phase field, Alloy Design 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1567 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Sumeet Khanna is a Faculty member in the Department of Materials Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur. He completed his B.Tech. - M.Tech Dual Degree in Materials Engineering from IIT Kharagpur in 2015. He earned his Ph.D. from the Indian Institute of Science Bengaluru in 2021, where his research projects spanned microstructure modelling and machine learning in materials. He has post-doctoral industrial research experience working on advanced magnetic and high-temperature materials for energy, EV, and automotive sectors. His present research interests are on magnetic materials, multiscale materials modelling using CALPHAD, phase field, cellular automata, and Artificial Intelligence  applications in materials. 
 
 
 
 
                        Research
                     
 
 
 Our group is primarily involved in Integrated Computational Materials Engineering (ICME) across different length scales (CALPHAD, phase field method, cellular automata), Material Informatics, applications of Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning in Materials, and manufacturing and characterization of electromagnetic materials for EVs. The scope of material systems studied encompasses strong permanent magnets, soft magnets, high-temperature materials (such as superalloys and steels), and lightweight materials, utilizing both advanced computational and experimental methods.

 Materials Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Abhay Gupta 
 
 
 
 
 
 Abhay Gupta 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Abhay Gupta
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: Materials Engineering, IISc Bangalore, 2022 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        abhaygupta[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Electrochemistry; proton exchange membrane water electrolysis; metallic/alloy coatings; corrosion analysis; microstructure-corrosion correlation. 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1568 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Abhay Gupta is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Materials Engineering at IIT Jodhpur. He completed his B. Tech degree from IIT Roorkee in 2014 and later obtained his M. Tech degree and Ph.D. in Materials Engineering from IISc Bangalore in 2022. His Ph.D. research work primarily focused on the correlation between electrochemical and microstructural properties within electroplated Tin-based coatings.  

Before joining IIT Jodhpur, he was a postdoctoral researcher at the University of Lorraine, France, and the University of Quebec, Canada. During his postdoctoral research, he focused on clean energy technologies, with a particular emphasis on green hydrogen production. His work focuses on proton exchange membrane (PEM) water electrolyzers, including the development of electrodes, the study of oxygen evolution reaction (OER) catalysts, and the enhancement of device performance through coatings for porous transport layers (PTLs) and bipolar plates (BPPs). He also examines corrosion behaviour in electrolyzer environments and material durability under prolonged operation.
 
Dr. Abhay has also worked in the R&D divisions of L&T Green Energy, Mumbai, and Tata Steel's Metaliks Division, Kharagpur, after completing his Ph.D., and at SAIL, Bokaro, after obtaining his B.Tech degree.  

The primary focus of his research group is:
 Performance evaluation of PEM electrolyzer components for green hydrogen production.  Electrode fabrication for water electrolyzers.  Electrochemistry – corrosion analysis.

 Nitin Kumar Sharma 
 
 
 
 
 
 Nitin Kumar Sharma 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Nitin Kumar Sharma
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        nitinksh[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Phase Transformations; Grain Boundary Engineering; Thermo-mechanical Processing; Electron Microscopy; Atom Probe Tomography; Computational Thermodynamics; Finite Element Simulation
 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1560 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Nitin Kumar Sharma is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Materials Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur. He received his Ph.D. from the Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur and worked as a postdoctoral fellow at the University of Alberta, Canada. His primary research interests include microstructural engineering and the design of novel advanced alloys. He utilizes computational thermodynamics and advanced characterization using electron microscopy and atom probe tomography.

 Materials Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Sk Md Hasan 
 
 
 
 
 
 Sk Md Hasan 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Sk Md Hasan
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: Indian Institute of Technology Kharagpur 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        hasan[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Advanced Steel Design; Phase Transformations; Deformation and Wear Behaviour of Metals/alloys; Processing-structure-performance correlations in steels.
 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1561 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Sk Md Hasan is an Assistant Professor in Materials Engineering at IIT Jodhpur. He completed his Ph.D. and M.Tech. in Metallurgical & Materials Engineering at Indian Institute of Technology Kharagpur, and B.E. in Metallurgy & Materials Engineering at Indian Institute of Engineering Science and Technology (IIEST), Shibpur.    Before joining IIT Jodhpur, he worked as post-doctoral research fellow at KTH Royal Institute of Technology, Sweden, where he worked on the prediction of austenite stability in medium-Mn automotive steels. Dr. Hasan has also worked at the R & D and Scientific Services division of Tata Steel Limited, Jamshedpur, where he was involved in the failure investigation of engineering components occurred during service conditions.    His research group primarily focuses on: ·           Advanced steel design through computations & experimentations; ·           Thermodynamics & kinetics of phase transformations; ·           Processing-microstructure- performance correlations in steels; ·           Deformation & failure mechanisms in materials; ·           Wear and Tribology.   Some of the courses which Dr. Hasan has taught to the UG and PG students include: Advanced Phase Transformations, Diffusion in Solids, Casting & Solidification, Physical Metallurgy, Structure of Materials etc.

 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Vikramsingh Thakur is a polymer scientist specializing in functional polymer composites, sustainable materials, and advanced packaging systems. He completed his Ph.D. in Materials Science and Engineering from IIT Delhi as a Prime Minister’s Research Fellow (PMRF), India’s most prestigious doctoral fellowship. His doctoral research focused on multifunctional polymer nanocomposite films with enhanced barrier, mechanical, antioxidant, and sensing functionalities for smart and sustainable packaging. He holds an M.Tech. in Polymer Science and Technology from IIT Delhi (with distinction), a B.Tech. Gold Medalist in Plastic and Polymer Engineering from MIT Aurangabad, and a Diploma from Government Polytechnic, Amravati, reflecting a strong and consistent academic foundation. Dr. Thakur has extensive industry–academia research experience. He has worked at ITC Life Sciences & Technology Centre (ITC Ltd.) on industrial-grade biodegradable and high-barrier packaging materials for food, tobacco, and personal care sectors, and at Bambrew Plant Fiber & Technology, contributing to next-generation sustainable and meta-material-based packaging innovations. Earlier, he served as a Research Engineer at Saint-Gobain Abrasives R&D, where his work on nonwoven products earned the Business Impact Award. His research training also includes stints at Resil Chemicals (silicone-modified resins) and DRDO Pune (electrospun nanofiber-reinforced laminates). His achievements include two patents, notably an Indian patent on bilayered halochromic freshness-indicator films and sustainable. His honors include the PMRF, Research Excellence Travel Award, Best Teaching Assistant Award, Devidasrao Ashtaputre Memorial Award, Research Scholar Travel Award and Best Poster Award at a national pharmaceutical symposium. Dr. Thakur serves on the Advisory Board of Bambrew Plant Fiber Pvt. Ltd. as a Consultant, playing a key role in steering materials innovation and accelerating the development of disruptive, sustainable packaging technologies at the interface of research and industrial deployment. 
 
 
 
 
                        Research
                     
 
 
 We are a young and dynamic team engaged in cutting-edge research in functional polymer composites and advanced materials. Our group thrives on curiosity, creativity, and collaboration as we explore both fundamental science and real-world applications of polymer composite technologies for a sustainable future and the benefit of society. Our research includes: Advanced processing strategies  for blends, composites, and nanocomposites with tailored microstructures and superior performance. Processing-Structure- Performance Correlation Development of high-performance, lightweight, and multifunctional composite  for strategic defence applications. Sustainable and Biodegradable Materials   and  Advanced Packaging Films & Laminates for  Engineering next-generation packaging solutions with enhanced barrier, mechanical, and functional properties. Electrospun Nanofibrous Materials in  architectures for filtration, biomedical, and structural applications. We actively welcome enthusiastic students and researchers to join our lab. For internships, Ph.D. admissions, or self-sponsored positions, please reach out with your CV.

 People 
 
 
 Anurag Sharma 
 
 
 
 
 
 Anurag Sharma 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Anurag Sharma
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D. : Nanyang Technological University (NTU) Singapore 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        anuragsharma[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Advanced Composite Materials; Thermoplastic composites; Fatigue and Impact behavior of Composites; Multifunctional Composite Design; Composite Manufacturing & Processing; Structural testing; Damage mechanics & Failure analysis. 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1569 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Anurag Sharma is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Materials Engineering (jointly with Mechanical Engineering) at IIT Jodhpur. He obtained his Ph.D. in Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering from Nanyang Technological University (NTU), Singapore (2021–2025). He earned his M.Tech. in Materials Science and Engineering from IIT Gandhinagar (2019–2021) and holds a B.Tech. (Honors) in Mechanical Engineering from Government Engineering College Ajmer (2015–2019). His Ph.D. research primarily focused on enhancing the cyclic and impact performance of thermoplastic composites using filler reinforcement. During his doctoral studies, he gained collaborative research experience with Leonardo Helicopters and the Agency for Science, Technology and Research (A*STAR), Singapore. He has received several notable accolades, including the NTU Research Scholarship and the IITGN Institute Fellowship. At IIT Jodhpur, his research focuses on advancing composite materials for next-generation structural components. His research interests include fiber-reinforced thermoplastic composites; fatigue and impact behavior of composites; multifunctional composite design; composite manufacturing and processing; structural testing; and damage mechanics and failure analysis. His work aims to develop high-performance, lightweight, and sustainable composite materials through innovative design, manufacturing, and performance evaluation.

 Materials Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Ravi Prakash Srivastava 
 
 
 
 
 
 Ravi Prakash Srivastava 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Ravi Prakash Srivastava
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D: Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        ravip[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Fabrication and characterization of solar cells, Sodium-ion Batteries, Energy Materials, Thin-films, Nanomaterials, Materials Characterization, and Structure-property correlation 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1553 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 I am Ravi P. Srivastava, currently serving in the Department of Materials Engineering at IIT Jodhpur, which I joined in 2024. I hold an M.Tech. in Materials Science and Engineering from Thapar University and earned my Ph.D. in Materials and Metallurgical Engineering from the Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur. My academic and research journey has provided me with a strong foundation in the fundamental and advanced concepts of Materials Science. I have worked as a postdoctoral researcher in the Department of Materials Science and Engineering at Yonsei University, Seoul, South Korea, where I focused on the fabrication and characterization of various types of solar cells, including perovskite, hybrid, inorganic, and those with transparent electrodes. My research has explored innovative strategies such as interfacial engineering, electrode and composition tuning to enhance device performance. I have also developed and studied a novel transfer printing method for the cost-effective fabrication of solar cells. Beyond energy harvesting, my research extends into energy storage technologies. I have worked on the development of solid-state sodium-ion batteries, employing composite polymer electrolytes to improve their efficiency and stability. At IIT Jodhpur, my ongoing research encompasses the fabrication and characterization of solar cells, sodium-ion batteries, sensors, and medical instruments. My work aims to contribute to sustainable energy solutions and the advancement of smart functional materials.

 People 
 
 
 Devendra Singh Negi 
 
 
 
 
 
 Devendra Singh Negi 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Devendra Singh Negi
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: JNCASR Bangalore 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        devendra[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Experimental and computational electron microscopy; EMCD; DFT; Quantum material;Thermoelectric materials; Magnetic materials; Quantum materials

 
 
 
 
 call 
 
  0291 280 1562 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Devendra Singh has done his Ph.D from JNCASR, Bangalore, India in 2017, under the guidance of Prof. Ranjan Datta. He has obtained the Carl Tyggers Postdoctoral Fellowship at Uppsala University, Sweden. Dr. Negi has obtained Max-Planck Institute Postdoctoral Fellowship, in Stuttgart, Germany for postdoctoral research in 2019. Currently, he is an assistant professor in the Department of materials engineering, at IIT Jodhpur, India. His research interests are in understanding and developing the various functional materials e.g, magnetic materials, 2D materials, thermoelectric materials, quantum materials, nanomaterials etc. Apart of materials development Dr. Negi holds a vast expertise in advanced imaging and spectroscopic techniques in advanced aberration corrected transmission electron microscopy. Dr. Negi is interested in developing various analytical techniques e.g, EELS, EMCD, DPC, STEM technique in TEM.

 Materials Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Jaiveer Singh 
 
 
 
 
 
 Jaiveer Singh 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Jaiveer Singh
                         
 
                                Associate Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D: Indian Institute of Technology, Bombay 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        jaiveer[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Microstructure-mechanical property correlation; Deformation and fracture mechanisms; Metal forming processes; Additive manufacturing; Thermo-mechanical processing of materials; Design and development of alloys; Mechanical behavior of materials

 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1521 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Jaiveer Singh is an Associate Professor in the Department of Materials Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur. He is also an affiliate faculty and a Program Management Committee member of the Centre for Medical Technologies at IIT Jodhpur. His research primarily focuses on microstructure and texture evolution, thermo-mechanical processing, mechanical behavior of materials, and the design and development of light alloys and biomaterials. He is also actively involved in research on additive manufacturing technologies used for patient-specific implants. Before joining IIT Jodhpur, Dr. Singh worked as a Research Professor at Sunchon National University, Suncheon, Republic of Korea. During his tenure there, he investigated deformation and fracture mechanisms in magnesium (Mg) alloys under mini-V-bending and Erichsen testing at room temperature. He received his Ph.D. in Metallurgical Engineering and Materials Science from IIT Bombay in 2015. His doctoral research focused on the microstructural and textural evolution during deformation in zirconium. To date, he has published more than 50 research articles in SCI/SCIE-indexed journals and has 5 patents filed. He also serves as a reviewer for several esteemed journals and international conferences.

 Dr. Jitendra
Kumar is Joint Faculty in the Department of Physics and the Department of Materials Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology
Jodhpur. He completed his B.Tech. in Electronics & Communication
Engineering from Uttar Pradesh Technical University, Lucknow, in 2009, followed
by an M.Tech. in Nanotechnology from the Indian Institute of Technology Roorkee
in 2011. Dr. Kumar earned his Ph.D. from the Indian Institute of Technology
Kanpur in 2019, where his research focused on the synthesis and
characterization of solution-processed Cu 2 ZnSnS 4  thin
films for solar cell applications. After completing
his Ph.D., Dr. Kumar worked as an Institute Postdoctoral Fellow at the Indian
Institute of Technology Delhi until 2020. During this tenure, his research
primarily focused on thesynthesis of inorganic perovskite nanocubes (CsPbI x Br 3-x )
using a hot-injection technique forsolar cell applications. From 2020 to 2023,
he held a postdoctoral position at Ben-Gurion University of the Negev, Israel,
where his research centered on (Bi x Sb 1-x ) 2 Se 3 -based
solar cells.Between August 2023 and April 2025, he served as an INSPIRE Faculty
at IIT Dhanbad, leading research on BaZrS 3  thin-film solar cells.In
February and March 2025, he was Marie Curie Fellow at INL –
InternationalIberian Nanotechnology Laboratory.  
 Dr. Kumar has
received several prestigious honors, including the Marie Sklodowska-CurieAward
in 2024 and the Marie Sklodowska-Curie Seal of Excellence in 2023. He was also
awarded the INSPIRE Faculty Fellowship in 2023.Additionally,he received
international travel grants from EPFL and DST, which enabled him to present his
research in Switzerland in 2021 and in the USA in 2018. He won the Best Poster
Presentation Award at theIEEE Conference in Bangalore in 2018. Dr. Kumar has
actively participated in and presented his work at numerous international
conferences.Furthermore, he has been a visiting researcher atMartin Luther
University, Halle-Wittenberg, Germany, in 2015 and at the Centre forNanoscience
& Engineering at the Indian Institute of Science Bangalore in 2014. 
 
 
 
 
                        Research
                     
 
 
 Our research
group is dedicated to the exploring advanced materials for energy applications,
with a special emphasis on the development of inorganic thin-film solar cells.
We strive to contribute to a sustainable future by advancing the innovation in
materials design, fabrication and device engineering. Feel free to
explore our work, meet our team, and join us in building a cleaner and greener
future.To learn more about our research, visit:  https://sites.google.com/view/jitendra-kumar/home

 Mathematics 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Rajat Gupta  
 
 
 
 
 
 Rajat Gupta  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Rajat Gupta 
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD: Indian Institute of Technology Gandhinagar, India 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        rgupta[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Analytic Number Theory, q-series, and Combinatorics. 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Rajat Gupta is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Mathematics at the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur, a position he has held since December 2025. He received his Ph.D. in Mathematics from the Indian Institute of Technology Gandhinagar in July 2022. Prior to this, he completed his M.Sc. in Mathematics from the Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur during 2013–2015. Following his Ph.D., Dr. Gupta held several postdoctoral positions. He was a Postdoctoral Fellow at the Institute of Mathematics, Academia Sinica, Taiwan, from May to December 2022. He subsequently held positions at the University of Texas at Tyler (January 2023 -- August 2024) under Professor Madeline Dawsey, the University of Maine (September 2024 -- August 2025), and worked under the mentorship of Professor Kathrin Bringmann at the University of Cologne, Germany, until December 2025. His research interests lie in number theory, including analytic number theory, the theory of partitions, modular forms, harmonic Maass forms,  q -series, and special functions. Dr. Gupta is interested in supervising motivated summer interns and Ph.D. students, and in mentoring postdoctoral researchers. Prospective candidates are encouraged to contact him via his institute email.

 Mathematics 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Bimal Mandal 
 
 
 
 
 
 Bimal Mandal 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Bimal Mandal
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph. D.: Indian Institute of Technology Roorkee 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        bimalmandal[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Cryptology, Cryptographic Boolean Functions 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1466 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Bimal Mandal is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Mathematics at the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur. His research interests include cryptographic Boolean functions and cryptanalysis of block and stream ciphers. He has published several research papers in reputed journals and conferences in the area of cryptography. His research contributes to the analysis and characterization of the basic building of symmetric key cryptosystems. Dr. Mandal completed his Master of Science degree in Mathematics from Jadavpur University in 2012 and PhD degree from Indian Institute of Technology Roorkee in 2018.  Before joining IIT Jodhpur in July 2022, he worked as an Assistant Professor in Institute for Advancing Intelligence (IAI) at TCG Centres for Research and Education in Science and Technology (TCG CREST), Kolkata. He worked as a visiting scientist in the R C Bose Centre for Cryptology and Security, Indian Statistical Institute, Kolkata, and as a research associate in the Department of Mathematics, Indian Institute of Technology Madras. He worked as a postdoctoral fellow in the Cryptology, Arithmetic: Algebraic Methods for Better Algorithms (CARAMBA) team, National Institute for Research in Computer Science and Automation (INRIA), France for more than a year, and in the Department of Computer Sciences and Networks (INFRES), Telecom Paris, France for one year.

 Mathematics 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Dilpreet Kaur 
 
 
 
 
 
 Dilpreet Kaur 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Dilpreet Kaur
                         
 
                                Associate Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: Indian Institute of Science Education and Research (IISER) Mohali 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        dilpreetkaur[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Algebra (Group Theory) 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1460 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dilpreet Kaur is an Associate Professor in the Department of Mathematics at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jodhpur. Her academic journey includes a PhD in Mathematics from IISER Mohali, followed by postdoctoral fellowships at IISER Pune under the NBHM and SERB National Post Doctoral Fellowships. She was awarded a Gold Medal for securing the first position in her MSc Mathematics program at Panjab University in 2009. She has been serving as a faculty member at IIT Jodhpur since 2019. She has taught a wide range of mathematics courses, including Real Analysis, Number Theory, Abstract Algebra, Linear Algebra, Optimization, and specialized subjects like Group Theory for Machine Learning and Representations of Finite Groups. She is also teaching regularly in national level schools sponsored by National center of Mathematics (NCM) and Indian Women and Mathematics (IWM). She has also organized workshops and academic programs, such as the Annual Foundation School-I (2022), Women in Maths: Progress and Challenges (2022), and the Online SageMath Workshop (2021).  In research, Dr. Kaur has made significant contributions to algebra, particularly in the areas of finite groups, conjugacy classes and representations of finite groups, quadratic forms,  quandles and quandle rings. She is the Principal Investigator (PI) of a project titled "Computations in Wreath Product", funded by the Science and Engineering Research Board (SERB) under the MATRICS scheme.

 People 
 
 
 Kuntal Som 
 
 
 
 
 
 Kuntal Som 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Kuntal Som
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD: Indian Institute of Technology Madras 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        kuntals[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Mathematical optimization, Set-valued Optimization and Bilevel Programming 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1474 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Kuntal Som is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Mathematics at the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur. He received his Ph.D. from the Indian Institute of Technology Madras and worked as a Postdoctoral Fellow in the Department of Economic Sciences, Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur. His primary research interests is broadly in mathematical optimization theory and specilaizing in set-valued optimization and bilevel Programming. Other interest includes, game theory, quantum information theory, but mainly from the perspective of optimization.

 Mathematics 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Kriti Goel 
 
 
 
 
 
 Kriti Goel 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Kriti Goel
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D: Indian Institute of Technology Bombay 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        kgoel[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Commutative Algebra and Its Interaction with Combinatorics and Algebraic Geometry 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1479 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Kriti Goel joined the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur as an Assistant Professor in December 2025. She received her Ph.D. in Mathematics from the Indian Institute of Technology Bombay in August 2020 under the supervision of Dr. Jugal K. Verma. Dr. Goel has held several postdoctoral positions since. She was an Early Career Fellow at the Indian Institute of Technology Gandhinagar, India (October 2020 - October 2021), working with Dr. Indranath Sengupta. She received the Fulbright-Nehru Postdoctoral Research Fellowship to attend the University of Utah, USA (January 2022 - January 2023) and work with Dr. Anurag Singh. It was followed by a 2-year position in the Singularity Theory and Algebraic Geometry group at the Basque Center for Applied Mathematics, Spain. Before joining IIT Jodhpur, she was a Postdoctoral Fellow at the University of Missouri-Columbia, USA (August 2025 - December 2025), working with Hema Srinivasan.
 Her research interests include the study of the local theory of singularities from an algebraic viewpoint, with emphasis on numerical invariants of singularities, in particular, multiplicity theory and positive characteristic methods.

 People 
 
 
 Pradip Sasmal 
 
 
 
 
 
 Pradip Sasmal 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Pradip Sasmal
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph. D.: Indian Institute of Technology Hyderabad (IITH) 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        psasmal[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Frame theory ; Sparse optimization theory ; Reinforcement learning and their applications
 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1462 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Pradip Sasmal is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Mathematics at the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur (IITJ), Rajasthan, India. He holds a Ph.D. in Mathematics from IIT Hyderabad (2017), an M.Sc. from the University of Hyderabad (2011), and a B.Sc. from the University of Calcutta (2009).​ Before joining IIT Jodhpur, Dr. Sasmal was a postdoctoral fellow at IIT Hyderabad (April 2021–May 2022) and at the Indian Institute of Science (IISc) Bangalore (August 2017–March 2021).​ His research interests encompass finite frame theory, compressed sensing (CS), physics-informed neural networks (PINNs), reinforcement learning (RL), and lightweight neural networks.

 Mathematics 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Moumita Mandal 
 
 
 
 
 
 Moumita Mandal 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Moumita Mandal
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: Indian Institute of Technology Kharagpur 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        moumita[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Numerical Functional Analysis, Integral Equation 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1459  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Moumita Mandal is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Mathematics at the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur. She earned her Ph.D. and M.Sc. in Mathematics from IIT Kharagpur, following a B.Sc. (Hons.) in Mathematics from Bankura Christian College. Her research specializes in Numerical Functional Analysis, particularly in approximation methods for integral and integro-differential equations, fixed point theory, and operator theory. With teaching experience spanning IIT Jodhpur and VIT University, she has offered a wide range of undergraduate and postgraduate courses, including Numerical Analysis, Functional Analysis, and Scientific Computations. She has authored over 15 journal articles in reputed international journals and contributed to several conferences and workshops. Dr. Mandal has been recognized with prestigious fellowships and grants, including a Postdoctoral Fellowship from NBHM and multiple research grants from NBHM and IIT Jodhpur. She serves as a reviewer for Mathematical Reviews and holds memberships in several mathematical societies such as AMS, RMS, and IMS. She is also on the editorial board of journals in mathematics and computational sciences. Her supervision includes guiding multiple master’s and doctoral students, with active research projects funded by Indian government agencies.

 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Puneet Sharma is working as an Professor in the Department of Mathematics at Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur. He served as a faculty member at Department of Mathematics, IISER Bhopal for about six months after which he joined IIT Jodhpur as an Assistant Professor at IIT Jodhpur in 2012. He also served as a postdoctoral Fellow at School of Physical Sciences, JNU, New Delhi for more than a year. He received his Masters and Ph.D. degrees in Mathematics from Indian Institute of Technology Delhi in 2005 and 2011 respectively. His research interests include Topological Dynamics, Symbolic Dynamics, Chaos Theory and their applications. Among his notable achievements, he was awarded first prize for best academic performance in B.Sc(H) Mathematics. He also secured All India Rank 21 in GATE (MA) 2004 Examination.

 Md Abu Talhamainuddin Ansary

 Ph. D.: Indian Institute of Technology Kharagpur

 biotech 
 
 Numerical Optimization; Multi-objective Optimization; Interval Analysis 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1465  
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Md Abu Talhamainuddin Ansary is an  Assistant Professor of Mathematics at IIT Jodhpur. He earned his M. sc. as well as Ph.D. from IIT Kharagpur. He did his postdoc in the department of economic sciences, IIT Kanpur form June 2019 to March 2021. He served as an Assistant Professor in VIT Bhopal prior to join IIT Jodhpur in 2021.  His research primarily focuses on multi-objective optimization, interval analysis etc.. 

 Among notable achievements, he was awarded Prof. Probodh Chandra Sanyal award for the best project work in 2nd Year M. Sc. in Mathematics, IIT Kharagpur in 2012.

 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Rakesh Ratnakar Pawar 
 
 
 
 
 
 Rakesh Ratnakar Pawar 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Rakesh Ratnakar Pawar
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D (Mathematics): Tata Institute of Fundamental Research Mumbai 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        rakeshpawar[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Algebraic Geometry, Homotopical methods in Algebraic Geometry and Commutative Algebra 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1477 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Rakesh Pawar is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Mathematics at the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur, a position he has held since December 2025. He received his Ph.D. in Mathematics from the Tata Institute of Fundamental Research, Mumbai, in September 2017. Before this, he completed his M.Sc. in Mathematics from the Indian Institute of Technology Bombay from 2010–2012.
 
Following his Ph.D., Dr. Pawar held several postdoctoral positions. He was a Postdoctoral Fellow at the University of Duisburg-Essen, Germany, from October 2017 to September 2018. He subsequently held a Postdoctoral position at IISER Mohali (2019-2021) and an NBHM postdoctoral fellowship at IISER Pune (2021-2023). He was a postdoc at École Normale Supérieure de Lyon, France (September 2023-August 2024). He was a Visiting Scientist at HRI Prayagaraj (Sept 2024-July 2025) and then a Visiting Assistant Professor at Kerala School of Mathematics, Kerala (August 2025-December 2025).
 
His area of research is Algebraic Geometry, and his interests lie in Algebraic cycles, Motives, Milnor-(Witt) K-theory, the applications of homotopical methods in Algebraic Geometry, and commutative algebra.

 People 
 
 
 V V M S Chandramouli 
 
 
 
 
 
 V V M S Chandramouli 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            V V M S Chandramouli
                         
 
                                Associate Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: SUNY at StonyBrook, USA and RUG 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        chsarma[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Dynamical Systems, Renormalization Theory, Dynamical Neuroscience 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1456 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. V. V. M. S Chandramouli is an Associate Professor in the Department of Mathematics, Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur. In December 2008, he has completed his Ph.D in Dynamical Systems  from the Department of Mathematics, University of Groningen, The Netherlands and State University of New York, Stony Brook, USA. He arrived at IIT Jodhpur in November 2009.  His research focuses on Dynamical Systems, including Renormalization theory in low-dimensional dynamics, Dynamical Neuroscience,  Fractal Geometry, and q-deformed nonlinear systems. His work has been published  in the leading journals such as Ergodic Theory and Dynamical  Systems, Discrete and Continuous Dynamical Systems, Journal of Statistical Physics, Chaos Solitons and Fractals, Journal of Difference Equations and Applications, Applied Mathematics and computation, Nonlinear Dynamics. etc.

 People 
 
 
 Subhash Bhagat 
 
 
 
 
 
 Subhash Bhagat 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Subhash Bhagat
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph. D.: Indian Statistical Institute 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        sbhagat[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in  
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Distributed algorithms for Swarm Robotics, Multi-agent systems, Distributed network algorithms 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1468 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 I am currently an Assistant Professor in the Department of Mathematics, IIT Jodhpur, Rajasthan, India. Before joining IIT Jodhpur, I was a postdoctoral fellow at the  Department of Computer Science and Engineering of the University of Quebec in Outaouais, Canada under the supervision of Prof. Andrzej Pelc. I also worked  as a postdoctoral fellow at the University of Ottawa, Canada under the supervision of Prof. Paola Flocchini of University of Ottawa  and Prof. Nicola Santoro of Carleton University, Canada.  I earned my PhD from the Advanced Computing and Microelectronics Unit of Indian Statistical Institute, Kolkata. My Ph.D. supervisor was Prof. Krishnendu Mukhopadhyaya.

Research Interests: My primary research interests lie in the areas of distributed systems and algorithms. In particular, I am interested in the following areas:

Distributed algorithms for Swarm Robots and Autonomous Multi-Agent systems.

Internet of Robotic Things (IoRT) and Autonomous Mobile Robotic Sensors.

Distributed Network algorithms.

 Security in Distributed Computing

 Mathematics 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Brahadeesh Sankarnarayanan 
 
 
 
 
 
 Brahadeesh Sankarnarayanan 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Brahadeesh Sankarnarayanan
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: Indian Institute of Technology Bombay 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        brahadeesh[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Extremal combinatorics and graph theory 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1469 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About me
                     
 
 
 I am an Assistant Professor (Grade II) in the Department of Mathematics at IIT Jodhpur. I received my Ph.D. from IIT Bombay in 2024. Prior to joining IIT Jodhpur, I was an Institute Postdoctoral Fellow at IIT Bombay, and then a Research Associate at IIT Hyderabad. My current research interest lies in extremal graph theory and combinatorics. In graph theory, I have worked on list colorings of graphs, particularly on the behavior of the gap between the choice number and chromatic number for graphs embeddable on a fixed surface. In extremal set theory, I have worked on a fractional variant of set systems with restricted intersection sizes. This work also led me to try to compute the ranks of a large class of dense matrices which are conjectured to have "high" rank.  I am also interested in entropy methods in combinatorics, as well as Brualdi–Hoffman-type problems in spectral graph theory.

 Gaurav Bhatnagar | Mathematics | IIT Jodhpur

 Mathematics 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Gaurav Bhatnagar 
 
 
 
 
 
 Gaurav Bhatnagar 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Gaurav Bhatnagar
                         
 
                                Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: Indian Institute of Technology Roorkee 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        goravb[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Wavelet Analysis, Fractional Transform Theory, Multimedia Security, Image Processing, Information Fusion 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1457 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Gaurav Bhatnagar is a Professor in the Department of Mathematics at Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur since June 2023. He was an Associate Professor and Assistant Professor in the same Department from September 2013 onward. Prior to joining IIT Jodhpur, Dr. Bhatnagar spent four years as a Post-doctoral Research Associate in the Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering at University of Windsor, Canada from September 2009 to August 2013. Dr. Bhatnagar received his PhD in interdisciplinary area of Applied Mathematics and Computer Science and an MSc in Applied Mathematics from the Indian Institute of Technology Roorkee, India, in 2010 and 2005, respectively. He is also the Project Director of TIH iHub Drishti, a Section 8 Not-for-profit Organisation, promoted by and at IIT Jodhpur established under National Mission on Interdisciplinary Cyber Physical Systems (NM-ICPS) of the Government of India. His research interests lie in the broad area of wavelet and fractional transform theory, with applications in multimedia security, digital watermarking and encryption techniques, hashing protocols, biometrics, image analysis, segmentation and completion. Dr. Bhatnagar is the author of over 80 scholarly publications in peer reviewed technical journals and conference proceedings. He was recently listed under researchers in Artificial Intelligence & Image Processing in the newly released Stanford University listing of the top 2% of researchers globally. He is a member of several professional bodies, including IEEE, ACM, SIAM and AMS. He is associated with several journals as the technical committee member and has been regularly associated with international conferences/workshops in various roles such as Program Chair, Area Chair, Session Chair and TPC Member.

 People 
 
 
 Sahil Gehlawat 
 
 
 
 
 
 Sahil Gehlawat 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Sahil Gehlawat
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D. : Indian Institute of Science Bangalore 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        sahilg[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Several Complex Variables, Theory of holomorphic foliations. 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1470 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About me
                     
 
 
 I am currently working as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Mathematics, IIT Jodhpur. I obtained my Ph.D. in Mathematics from IISc Bangalore in 2022. After completing Ph.D., I pursued postdoctoral research at several institutes: first at Harish-Chandra Research Institute (HRI), Prayagraj; then at the University of Lille, France, for two years; and most recently at TIFR-CAM, Bangalore, for one year, before joining IIT Jodhpur. My area of expertise is Several complex variables, with current research interests including the theory of singular holomorphic foliations on complex manifolds, the reduced Bergman kernel for planar domains, and curvature properties of the Grauert metrics. Among some notable achievements, I received the Shyama Prasad Mukherjee Fellowship (SPMF) from CSIR in 2018 for my doctoral research. In 2019, I was awarded the Professor P. L. Bhatnagar Gold Medal for the best Integrated Ph.D. student in the Division of Physical and Mathematical Sciences at IISc Bangalore. Recently, in 2025, I have been awarded the DST INSPIRE Faculty Fellowship.

 biotech 
 
 Analysis of Partial Differential Equations

 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 I am an Assistant Professor in the Department of Mathematics, IIT Jodhpur, Rajasthan, India. I have completed my Ph.D. from IIT Delhi in the year 2018. Prior to this, I mastered in Mathematics and Computing from the Department of Mathematics, IIT Guwahati in 2014.  My Bachelor's degree is from Lady Brabourne College, University of Calcutta in 2012. My research area of specialisation is "Analysis of Partial Differential Equations".

 People 
 
 
 Anuj Kumar 
 
 
 
 
 
 Anuj Kumar 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Anuj Kumar
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: Indiana University Bloomington, USA 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        anujkumar[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Mathematical Fluid Dynamics, Turbulence 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1476 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 I am an Assistant Professor in the Department of Mathematics at IIT Jodhpur. I received my Ph.D. in Mathematics from Indiana University Bloomington, USA, in 2022. Following my doctoral studies, I spent two years as a Postdoctoral Researcher at Florida State University, USA. In my research, I study partial differential equations arising from hydrodynamic models, such as incompressible Euler and Navier-Stokes equations, and geophysical models such as the surface quasi-geostrophic equation (SQG) and its generalizations.
