 Mathematics 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Kirankumar R Hiremath 
 
 
 
 
 
 Kirankumar R Hiremath 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Kirankumar R Hiremath
                         
 
                                Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: University of Twente 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        k[dot]r[dot]hiremath[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in  
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Theoretical, mathematical and computational aspects of wave-matter interactions 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1453 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Kirankumar Hiremath is an Professor of Mathematics at IIT Jodhpur. He is an applied mathematician working in wave-matter interactions, mathematical physics, scientific computations, and applied analysis. His specialization is mathematical optics-photonics and the design of efficient methods to solve real life problems. He did his post-graduation from IIT Bombay and PhD from the University of Twente (The Netherlands). After that, he did his post-doc at  Optoelectronics Center, University of North Carolina at Charlotte (USA), and  Karlsruhe Institute of Technology (Germany). He worked at Zuse Institute Berlin (Germany). Since 2012, he has been with IIT Jodhpur. He is also a member of the Interdisciplinary Research Group on Quantum Information. His mathematical work on bent waveguides, microresonators, and coupled mode theory is very well received.

 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Abhishek Sarkar 
 
 
 
 
 
 Abhishek Sarkar 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Abhishek Sarkar
                         
 
                                Associate Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: Tata Institute of Fundamental Research Centre for                      Applicable Mathematics 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        abhisheks[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Elliptic partial differential equations 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1461 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Abhishek Sarkar is an Associate Professor of Mathematics at IIT Jodhpur. He earned his Ph.D. from TIFR-CAM, Bangalore. He did his postdoc at University of West Bohemia, Pilsen, Czech Republic for more than three year. He served as an Assistant Professor at BITS Pilani KK Birla Goa Campus prior to join IIT Jodhpur as an Assistant Professor in 2019. 

His research primarily focuses on nonlinear partial differential equations (PDEs), including elliptic (of second and higher orders) and nonlocal equations.​

Among notable achievements, he was awarded the DST-INSPIRE Faculty Award (DST, Govt. of India) in the year 2018.

 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Kiran Meena 
 
 
 
 
 
 Kiran Meena 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Kiran Meena
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: Banaras Hindu University, Varanasi 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        kiranmeena[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Differential Geometry, Riemannian Geometry 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1467 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Kiran Meena is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Mathematics at the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur. Earlier, she served as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Mathematics, Lady Shri Ram College for Women (University of Delhi). She obtained a Ph.D. from Banaras Hindu University and has post-doctoral research experience at Harish-Chandra Research Institute.
 
Her research interests lie in Differential Geometry. In particular, she studies geometric aspects of some special smooth mappings between various structured Riemannian manifolds. Precisely, these smooth mappings include Riemannian submersions, Riemannian maps, conformal submersions, conformal Riemannian maps, etc.

 Nil Kamal Hazra 
 
 
 
 
 
 Nil Kamal Hazra

                                Associate Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: IISER Kolkata 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        nilkamal[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Reliability Theory, Applied Probability 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1458 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Nil Kamal Hazra is currently working as an Associate Professor at IIT Jodhpur. He obtained  his Ph.D. degree in Reliability Theory from IISER Kolkata. After completion of his PhD, he joined as a Visiting Scientist at ISI Kolkata. Then, he moved to South Africa to pursue his postdoctoral research career, and he joined as a Claude Leon Postdoctoral Fellow in the Dept. of Mathematical Statistics at University of Free State, Bloemfontein. Then he started his professional career as an Assistant Professor in the Dept. of Mathematics at IIITDM Kancheepuram, Chennai. After spending one year at IIITDM Kancheepuram,  he moved to IIT Jodhpur where he is currently working in the Dept. of Mathematics. His current research interest includes Reliability Theory, Applied Probability, Robust Statistical Inference, Sequential Data Analysis and Time Series Analysis. He has been appointed as an Associate Editor in leading statistical journals, namely, Opsearch, Communications in Statistics-Theory and Methods, Communications in Statistics-Simulations and Computations, and Communications in Statistics- Case Studies and Data Analysis.

 Shaily Verma 
 
 
 
 
 
 Shaily Verma 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Shaily Verma
                         
 
                                 Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: Indian Institute of Technology Delhi 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        shailyverma[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Graph Algorithms, Parameterized Complexity 
 
 
 
 call 
 
  0291 280 1475 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Shaily Verma is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Mathematics at IIT Jodhpur. Before joining IIT Jodhpur, she was a postdoctoral researcher at the Hasso Plattner Institute (HPI), Germany. Prior to that, she was a postdoctoral researcher at the Institute of Mathematical Sciences (IMSc), Chennai. She earned her Ph.D. in Mathematics in 2020 from IIT Delhi under the supervision of Prof. B. S. Panda. Dr. Verma's research interests lie in graph algorithms and parameterized complexity, with a focus on the design and analysis of efficient algorithms for hard combinatorial problems. She has published her research in leading international conferences and journals in theoretical computer science and algorithms, and continues to explore problems at the intersection of graph theory, algorithms, and computational complexity.

 Shrutidhara Sarma | Mechanical Engineering | IIT Jodhpur

 Mechanical Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Shrutidhara Sarma 
 
 
 
 
 
 Shrutidhara Sarma 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Shrutidhara Sarma
                         
 
                                Associate Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD. Indian Institute of Technology Guwahati 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        shrutidhara[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Smart Respiration System, Flexible and stretchable sensors, Portable assistive devices, Self-powered sensors, Airframe sensing skin technologies, Structure- property relations, Electrospun nanofibers, Laminated nanocomposites, Thin film gauges 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1515 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Shrutidhara Sarma is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Mechanical Engineering at IIT Jodhpur, where she leads the FERN (Flexible Sensors from Nanocomposites) Lab (https://www.shrutidhara.com/fern). A distinguished researcher in thin-film sensors, laminated nanocomposites, and flexible sensing technologies, Dr. Sarma has received prestigious awards and fellowships, including the INAE Young Associate 2024, WISER Award 2023 by Indo-German Science & Technology Centre, ISEES Young Scientist Award 2023, and Smt. Sheela Baya National Award (IEI, 2023), among others. She completed her Ph.D. in 2017 partly from IIT Guwahati and Aristotle University of Thessaloniki in Greece, through the Erasmus Mundus fellowship from the European Commission. During her Ph.D., she pioneered thin-film gauges for transient temperature and heat flux measurements, before she served as a post-doctoral fellow at Kunsan National University of South Korea. Dr. Sarma's research has led to transformative advancements in wearable and assistive sensor technologies. Her work on Smart Respiration Monitoring with Synchronized Ventilation system for neonates integrates ultrasensitive strain sensing patches that enables real-time, non-invasive monitoring and reduces infection risks. She also developed FLEXTEM, a flexible skin thermometer that provides precise, continuous temperature monitoring, ideal for personalized healthcare (https://doi.org/10.1002/pc.27178).  Her touch sensors for assistive applications detect touch and bending simultaneously with high sensitivity and minimal drift, achieving 93% accuracy in gesture recognition (https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2666053925000049?via%3Dihub). Additionally, she developed a novel dataset, FEAD, to model PVDF nanofiber properties using machine learning, bridging experimental and computational insights  (https://doi.org/10.1016/j.commatsci.2022.111661).  Supported by over 1.9 crore INR funding, including Indo-German TESSLA and ANRF-FRAMME projects, her work in sensor technology promises transformative advances in healthcare, sustainability, and engineering. Besides Academics, she is also an excellent performer of music and had represented India in Japan through Jenesys 2.0 program.

 Mechanical Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Sudipto Mukhopadhyay 
 
 
 
 
 
 Sudipto Mukhopadhyay 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Sudipto Mukhopadhyay
                         
 
                                Associate professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD. Eindhoven University of Technology 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        smukhopadhyay[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Experimental and Numerical Combustion, Clean Energy Technologies, Droplets and Sprays, Turbulent Flows 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1511 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr Sudipto Mukhopadhyay currently serves as an Associate Professor in the Department of Mechanical Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur (IITJ). He is also affiliated with the Rishabh Centre for Research and Innovation in Clean Energy. His research interests are a blend of theory and practice, focusing on the design and development of Clean Energy Technologies using experimental and numerical techniques. His investigations span a wide range of areas, including micro combustors, Scramjets, Microgas Turbines, Spray diagnostics, Gasturbines, and fundamental droplet studies. He has been awarded multiple research grants, including those from the Royal Academy of Engineering (UK), the Ministry of Power (MoP) India, the Ministry of Education (MoE) India,  Centre for High Technology (CHT) India, CESC Ltd., Rajasthan Renewable Energy Corporation Ltd., and GE Vernova. He was also awarded the Leaders in Innovation Fellowship, RAE, UK (2025) and Leeds Institute of Fluid Dynamics Visiting Fellow,UK (2024). Before joining IITJ, he was a postdoctoral researcher at Eindhoven University of Technology and Delft University of Technology in the Netherlands. He earned his PhD in turbulent combustion modelling at Eindhoven University of Technology (TU/e). He also holds an MTech from IIT Madras and a BTech from Kalyani Government Engineering College. Before his doctoral studies, Sudipto gained valuable experience working for over seven years in the Engineering and Industrial Services division of Tata Consultancy Services, a leading Indian multinational corporation.

 People 
 
 
 Barun Pratiher 
 
 
 
 
 
 Barun Pratiher 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Barun Pratiher
                         
 
                                Associate Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD. Indian Institute of Technology Guwahati 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        barun[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Dynamics of Machines and Structures, Flexible Robots, MEMS, Rotor Dynamics, Nonlinear Oscillations 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1505 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Barun Pratiher is an Associate Professor in the Department of Mechanical Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jodhpur. He earned his Ph.D. from the Indian Institute of Technology Guwahati in 2019, following a Master of Engineering in Engineering Mechanics from the Indian Institute of Engineering Science and Technology (IIEST), Shibpur (formerly Bengal Engineering College) in 2004. He began his academic career as a Lecturer at BITS Pilani and later joined IIT-ISM Dhanbad (formerly ISM Dhanbad) as an Assistant Professor in May 2009. In July 2011, he moved to IIT Jodhpur as an Assistant Professor. His research interests span vibration and control, rotor dynamics, flexible robotic systems, MEMS, and nonlinear dynamics. Dr. Pratiher has published several research articles (60+) in leading international journals and conferences, contributing significantly to these areas. He has successfully led and completed multiple research projects and continues to be actively engaged in ongoing research endeavors.

 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Gourhari Ghosh has been working as an assistant professor in the Mechanical Engineering department of IIT Jodhpur since September 2022. Prior joining to IIT Jodhpur, he worked as a National post-doctoral fellow (NPDF) in the Mechanical Engineering department of IIT Bombay. His post-doctoral research work was focused on the Laser directed energy deposition (LDED) of metal matrix composite coatings. He completed Ph.D. in Mechanical Engineering from IIT Kharagpur. His Ph.D. research work was focused on the ultra-high finishing of thermally sprayed WC-Co coating. His present research work is focused on Ultra-precision Finishing, LASER baser Additive Manufacturing, Thermal Spray Coating, Bio-compatible materials and coatings, and Tribology. He has been awarded the DST Inspire faculty fellowship award. He has also been awarded the Seal of Excellence award from European Commission, European Union (EU), and the National post-doctoral fellowship (NPDF) award from the Science and Engineering Research Board (SERB). During his B.Tech, he secured the departmental topper position and received a scholarship under UGC scheme from the Ministry of Human Resource Development (MHRD). Currently, he has been actively working as a reviewer of a few journals of Elsevier, Springer, and Sage publications.

 Mechanical Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Shobhana Singh 
 
 
 
 
 
 Shobhana Singh 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Shobhana Singh
                         
 
                                Associate Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD. Indian Institute of Technology Delhi 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        shobhana[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Thermal energy systems: Thermal energy storage, Solar dryers, Heat pumps, Heat exchangers; Heat and mass transfer analysis; Dynamic and Multiphysics modeling; Computational fluid dynamic modeling; System and design optimization; Renewable energy integration; Electrochemical carbon capture. 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1520 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Shobhana Singh is an experienced researcher and Associate professor in the Department of Mechanical Engineering at IIT Jodhpur. She received her Ph.D. from IIT Delhi and spent six years abroad working as postdoc and Assistant Professor at Arizona State University, USA, and Aalborg University, Denmark. She has over twelve years of research and teaching experience, focusing on energy systems that facilitates the transition towards sustainability by designing and optimizing thermal systems as critical units for energy generation, management, and storage. At the Energy Conversion and Storage Research (ECSR) Lab, her team develops computational numerical models of energy systems and components, as well as their applications. Another focus area is transitioning to smarter and sustainable solutions to water supply. She works on various aspects of water management, such as real-time monitoring and analysis of water distribution, hydraulic modeling to simulate critical scenarios, and developing Digital Twins that replicate real system in virtual models that serve as the basis for experimentation on drinking water distribution networks. Experimental research at ECSR includes facilities for performance tests on thermoelectric generator for waste heat recovery, research on heat transfer in energy storage systems and heat exchangers. Beyond scholarly activities, she is also committed to contributing to society and serves at multiple positions, including Editorial Board Member at Nature-Scientific Reports, Treasurer of ASME's Women in Engineering Group India, Principal Member in the Mechanical Engineering Division at the Bureau of Indian Standards, Executive Director at spin-off Medvocare Pvt. Ltd. India, and Research Expert at ITVersum KG, Austria.

 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Mrityunjay Doddamani 
 
 
 
 
 
 Mrityunjay Doddamani 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Mrityunjay Doddamani
                         
 
                                Associate Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph. D.: NITK Surathkal 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        mrityunjay[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Additive Manufacturing, Composites, Foams, Secured Manufacturing 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1533 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Mrityunjay Doddamani is an Associate Professor in the Department of Mechanical Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jodhpur, and an affiliated faculty member at the Department of Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering, New York University (NYU), USA. He leads the Advanced Manufacturing group and collaborates with premier institutions including National University of Singapore, University of Wisconsin-Madison, New York University, USA. His research spans Composites, Additive Manufacturing, Smart Manufacturing, Design and development of materials for specified applications with a strong focus on product design and security assurance in manufacturing systems.  Dr. Doddamani has authored over 85 journal publications, holds 5 patents and 12 design registrations with patent office of India. He is a recipient of the Prof. Satish Dhawan Young Engineer State Award and is listed among the top 2% of scientists globally. His research has been supported by national and international agencies, securing over ₹4000 lakhs in funding as PI/Co-PI. He is an active member of multiple professional societies and serves on editorial boards and conference organizing committees.

 Roshan Kumar Hota 
 
 
 
 
 
 Roshan Kumar Hota

 school 
 
 Ph. D.: Indian Institute of Technology Kharagpur

                                        rkhota[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Robotics, Robotic Manipulation, Grasping, Design of Robotic Hands, Mechanisms, Reinforcement Learning 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1529 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 I am Dr. Roshan Kumar Hota, an Assistant Professor at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jodhpur, with a Ph.D. in Mechanical Engineering from IIT Kharagpur. My main research interests are in the design of multi-fingered robotic hands, grasping and manipulation. I apply model-based and Reinforcement Learning based control methods for solving problems in robotic grasping and manipulation. This has applications in industrial automation and manipulation of objects in unstructured environments.

 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Anurag Sharma 
 
 
 
 
 
 Anurag Sharma 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Anurag Sharma
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D. : Nanyang Technological University (NTU) Singapore 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        anuragsharma[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Advanced Composite Materials; Thermoplastic composites; Fatigue and Impact behavior of Composites; Multifunctional Composite Design; Composite Manufacturing & Processing; Structural testing; Damage mechanics & Failure analysis. 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1569 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Anurag Sharma is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Materials Engineering (jointly with Mechanical Engineering) at IIT Jodhpur. He obtained his Ph.D. in Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering from Nanyang Technological University (NTU), Singapore (2021–2025). He earned his M.Tech. in Materials Science and Engineering from IIT Gandhinagar (2019–2021) and holds a B.Tech. (Honors) in Mechanical Engineering from Government Engineering College Ajmer (2015–2019). His Ph.D. research primarily focused on enhancing the cyclic and impact performance of thermoplastic composites using filler reinforcement. During his doctoral studies, he gained collaborative research experience with Leonardo Helicopters and the Agency for Science, Technology and Research (A*STAR), Singapore. He has received several notable accolades, including the NTU Research Scholarship and the IITGN Institute Fellowship. At IIT Jodhpur, his research focuses on advancing composite materials for next-generation structural components. His research interests include fiber-reinforced thermoplastic composites; fatigue and impact behavior of composites; multifunctional composite design; composite manufacturing and processing; structural testing; and damage mechanics and failure analysis. His work aims to develop high-performance, lightweight, and sustainable composite materials through innovative design, manufacturing, and performance evaluation.

 Mechanical Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Amrita Puri 
 
 
 
 
 
 Amrita Puri 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Amrita Puri
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD. Indian Institute of Technology Delhi 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        amritapuri[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Active noise control; Active vibration control; Experimental modal analysis; Acoustics 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1518 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Amrita Puri has been an assistant professor in Department of Mechanical Engineering, Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur since July 2019. She completed her PhD in the field of active control of noise in vibro-acoustic cavities from Indian Institute of Technology, Delhi. Her PhD thesis work has been published in Mechanical Systems and Signal Processing, Journal of the Acoustical Society of America, Applied Acoustics, Journal of Sound and Vibration etc. She was awarded ‘Distinction in Doctoral Research for the year 2019’ by Indian Institute of Technology, Delhi. Prior to that, she did Master of Technology in Solid Mechanics and Design from Indian Institute of Technology, Kanpur. Her first PhD student is working in the field of spatial sound field synthesis techniques to render focused and non-focused moving sound sources. The work has been published in Journal of Audio Engineering Society. Currently, she is supervising 3 full time PhD students and 1 part time PhD student. She has taught undergraduate course of ‘Engineering Mechanics’ and postgraduate courses of ‘Introduction to Acoustics’, ‘Modeling and control of Mechanical systems’ and ‘Geometric modeling and CAD’. Currently, she is principal investigator of three sponsored research projects broadly in the field of sound control.

 People 
 
 
 Nitesh Arora 
 
 
 
 
 
 Nitesh Arora 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Nitesh Arora
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: University of Wisconsin-Madison 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        nitesharora[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Solid & Structural Mechanics; Metamaterials; Instabilities; Soft Materials; Magnetoactive Materials 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1536 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Nitesh Arora is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Mechanical Engineering at IIT Jodhpur. Prior to joining IIT Jodhpur, he was a Postdoctoral Fellow at the Georgia Institute of Technology. He received his Ph.D. in Mechanical Engineering from the University of Wisconsin–Madison in 2023 and his bachelor’s degree from IIT Roorkee in 2018.
 
Prof. Arora leads the  Structured & Active Materials Lab , where his group integrates theory, computation, and experiments to investigate how structure, geometry, and activity can be harnessed to design metamaterials with programmable and adaptive mechanical responses.
 
His research has been recognized with several honors, including the Thomas J. R. Hughes Fellowship, the APS Distinguished Student Award, and the NSF Presenter Fellowship. Beyond research, he is actively involved in science outreach and has served as an Outreach Fellow for the U.S. National Committee for Theoretical and Applied Mechanics.

 Mechanical Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Anand Krishnan Plappally 
 
 
 
 
 
 Anand Krishnan Plappally 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Anand Krishnan Plappally
                         
 
                                Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD. The Ohio State University 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        anandk[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Water, Water Management and Characterization of Engineered Materials 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1507 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 I am a faculty in Engineering at IIT, Jodhpur (Indian Institute of Technology). I did a stint from March 2012-March 2014 as Assistant Professor at Center of Excellence in Energy, IITJ. I headed the focus group of Mechanical Engineering in 2014-15 to perform transition from COE Energy to Department of Mechanical Engineering. From 2015 to March 2019 as an Assistant Professor (7 years in total) in Mechanical Engineering. From March 2019 to June 2023 (4.2 years), Anand was an Associate Professor at the department and headed the Center for Emerging Technologies for Sustainable Development (CETSD) till August 31, 2023. I am also affiliated to the Space Science and Technology Program at IITJ since 2019. Presently, I am a Professor in Mechanical Engineering.  My teams work did focus in areas of  water-energy nexus, water filtration, wetland hydrodynamics, rural technologies, engineering education, micro-turbo designs, irrigation and horticulture, materials and probabilistic modeling in engineering design. I was a Post Doctoral Associate at Rohsenow Kendall Heat Transfer Laboratory , in Mechanical Engineering Department at MIT, Cambridge, MA. My Advisor was Prof. John Lienhard V. I worked in the area of water-energy nexus and contributed mainly to energy for water and economics of water use.   Enjoy and Read: Cost of Water Curious Read: Energy for Water Before I had another Post Doctoral Fellowship from Food, Agricultural and Biological Engineering (FABE) Department at The Ohio State University at Columbus, OH. I was involved in research on agricultural constructed wetland systems and also in physics of clay ceramics. My advisors were Prof. Larry C Brown and Prof. Alfred B. O. Soboyejo.  I received the PhD (Engineering) under the guidance of Prof Alfred Soboyejo and Prof. Larry C Brown from Food, Agricultural and Biological Engineering Department and  I conducted some of my experiments at The Princeton Institute for the Science and Technology of Materials (PRISM) at Princeton University. This also helped me to work under Prof Winston Soboyejo.  Previously I received the M.S. in Engineering Science and Mechanics under the guidance of Prof. Muhammad Ali R Sharif from the Aerospace Engineering and Mechanics Department at University of Alabama, Tuscaloosa. I was also co-advised by Prof. Richard C Bradt, Department of Metallurgical and Materials Engineering, University of Alabama. Here I worked on numerical modeling of molten steel flows in an industrial tundish. Prior to this I have experience as a design engineer for irrigation projects with CIMMYT (a part of the CGIAR) India. Here I had the honor to work under Dr Raj Kishan Gupta.  Prior to that, I was at IIT Delhi and part of UN FAO projects in the mentorship of Prof P M V Subbarao.  My Bachelors in Mechanical Engineering is from Madurai Kamaraj University in 2001. I am an alumnus of the Kerala School at Vikas Puri in New Delhi run under the aegis of Kerala Education Society and Government of Delhi.

 Mechanical Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Prodyut Ranjan Chakraborty  
 
 
 
 
 
 Prodyut Ranjan Chakraborty  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Prodyut Ranjan Chakraborty 
                         
 
                                Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD. Indian Institute of Science Bangalore 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        pchakraborty[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in  
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Heat and mass transfer, Latent heat based storage device for high temperature applications, Alloy solidification process, Active and passive solar cooling systems, Electronic cooling 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1508 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Prodyut Ranjan Chakraborty is a Professor in the Mechanical Engineering, IIT Jodhpur. He received his Bachelor degree of Mechanical Engineering from the North Bengal University in 2000, and his M.Sc Engineering in 2004, and PhD in 2011 both from the Department of Mechanical Engineering, Indian Institute of Science Bangalore. Prior to his joining at IIT Jodhpur, he served two years at the Department of Material Physics in Space in German Aerospace Center (DLR) Cologne as a postdoctoral research fellow. He also worked as a Research Analyst at the Applied CFD Lab, G.E. Global Research Center Bangalore during the years 2004 and 2005. His primary area of research is numerical modeling of alloy solidification, latent heat based energy storage systems for high temperature applications, Thermal management and thermal comfort, and sorption cooling.

 Mechanical Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Rahul Chhibber 
 
 
 
 
 
 Rahul Chhibber 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Rahul Chhibber
                         
 
                                Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD. Indian Institute of Technology Roorkee 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        rahul_chhibber[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Welding and joining, Manufacturing and materials processing, Mechanical behavior of materials 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1506 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Rahul Chhibber is a Professor in the Department of Mechanical Engineering at IIT Jodhpur, where he has been a faculty member since 2012. He earned his Ph.D. in Mechanical Engineering from IIT Roorkee in 2008. His research interests encompass welding and joining, manufacturing and materials processing, and the mechanical behavior of materials. He has contributed significantly to the development of welding consumables for shielded metal arc and submerged arc welding processes. His work also includes the fabrication and structural integrity assessment of dissimilar material joints. Dr. Chhibber has been involved in hygrothermal investigations on fiber-reinforced polymer nanocomposites. He has published over 90 research articles and has an h-index of 27. His work is widely cited, with over 2,400 citations. His student recently received the C.V. Raman Gold Medal for the best thesis in 2024. He was also awarded the W.H. Hobart Memorial Award from the American Welding Society in 2024. His research group has been recognized for excellence in presentations and research experimentation, including awards at international conferences and access to research facilities in Switzerland.

                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD. TU Delft, Netherlands 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        rajitranjan[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Topology optimization, Additive Manufacturing, Computational modelling 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1532 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Rajit Ranjan is currently serving as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Mechanical Engineering at IIT Jodhpur, India (since March 2025). He holds a Ph.D. from Delft University of Technology, Netherlands, where he was a Marie-Curie Fellow under the European Union’s project titled 'Precision Metal Additive Manufacturing'. He holds an M.S. from the University of Cincinnati, USA, and a B.Tech. from NIT Allahabad, India. Prior to joining IIT Jodhpur, he briefly held a faculty position at BITS Pilani, where he taught Additive Manufacturing. He also worked as a Computational Engineer in Simulation & Design at Alloyed Ltd., Oxford, UK, contributing to cutting-edge industrial R&D in metal additive manufacturing. Dr. Ranjan is a self-motivated multidisciplinary researcher with over 7 years of research experience in multi-physics modeling and topology optimization. His research interests lie in part-scale simulation of additive manufacturing processes and integrating physical models with optimization frameworks. He has total 5 years of industry experience and has worked across five countries, collaborating effectively with international teams.

 biotech 
 
 Robotics; Machine Vision; Robot calibration; Automated Visual Inspection; Industrial robots; Human-Machine Interface; 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1504 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Riby Abraham Boby did his BE, MS and PhD from National Institute of Technology Surathkal, Indian institute of Technology Madras and Indian Institute of Technology Delhi respectively all in Mechanical Engineering. He has worked at Sandvik Coromant as Engineer, Indian Institute of Technology Delhi as Senior Research Fellow/Project and Consultant, Innopolis University, Russia as Senior Researcher, Indian Institute of Technology Madras as Post Doctoral Researcher and Indian Institute of Information Technology, Kottayam as Assistant Professor. His research interests are in Robotics and Automation, Computer Vision, Human Machine Interface, etc., and details of scientific articles published in these areas can be seen at https://scholar.google.co.in/citations?user=W6SLYskAAAAJ&hl=en. Further information about sponsored projects undertaken, patents, etc. are available at https://sites.google.com/view/ribyabrahamboby/home. He is with the Department of Mechanical Engineering and also associated with Inter Disciplinary Research Platform, Robotics and Mobility Systems, Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur from 2022.

 Mechanical Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Nipun Arora 
 
 
 
 
 
 Nipun Arora 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Nipun Arora
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD. Indian Institute of Technology Delhi 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        nipun[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Flapping wing aerodynamics; Fluid-structure interaction; CFD with Lattice Boltzmann method; Turbulence and moving boundary simulations; High performance computing; Electrorheological Fluids. 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1519 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Nipun Arora completed his Ph.D. in Mechanical Engineering from the Indian Institute of Technology Delhi in 2017. Prior to that, he earned his M.Tech in Thermal Engineering from IIT Delhi in 2013, and his B.E. in Mechanical Engineering from Delhi College of Engineering (now DTU) in 2011. He pursued postdoctoral research at the Technion – Israel Institute of Technology from 2017 to 2018. Dr. Arora’s research focuses on bio-inspired aerodynamics, unsteady flow physics, and flapping-wing micro air vehicles (MAVs). At IIT Jodhpur, he leads research in experimental and computational aerodynamics, including force/torque measurements, flow visualization, and kinematic optimization. He has developed in-house high-fidelity LBM-based CFD solvers with dynamic mesh refinement, alongside low-fidelity methods like unsteady vortex panels. His solvers are parallelized using open-MPI and CUDA, and integrated with FSI coupling techniques. His broader goal is to enable efficient design and development of next-generation flapping MAV prototypes.

 Microsystems Fabrication

 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Ankur Gupta is an Associate Professor in the Department of Mechanical Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jodhpur. His research focuses on developing innovative and inexpensive fabrication methods for MEMS and microsystems across diverse application domains. He has gained foundational expertise in multi-scale fabrication/characterization techniques, and surface engineering.

 Mechanical Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Pratyaksh Karan 
 
 
 
 
 
 Pratyaksh Karan 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Pratyaksh Karan
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD: Indian Institute of Technology Kharagpur 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        pratyakshkaran[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
  Modelling and applications of fluids and soft materials in micro and nano scale systems, Mathematical methods for applied mechanics, Characterization of mechanical behavior of materials, Transport and Mixing of Solutes 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1537 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Pratyaksh Karan is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Mechanical Engineering at IIT Jodhpur. His research lies at the intersection of fluid mechanics, solid mechanics, and transport phenomena, with a focus on elastohydrodynamics, soft matter mechanics and applications and transport phenomena in porous media. His work combines analytical modelling, numerical simulations, and dedicated in-vitro experiments to investigate how flow, deformation, and transport interact across micro- to macro- length scales. Dr. Karan’s research spans a range of applications, including microfluidics, biological transport processes, and environmental flows. He is particularly interested in understanding transport in deformable and heterogeneous systems such as soft tissues, hydrogels, and porous geological formations. His work addresses fundamental questions related to mixing, dispersion, and irreversibility in coupled flow–deformation systems, with implications for drug delivery, contaminant transport, and emerging soft-material technologies. Prior to joining IIT Jodhpur, Dr. Karan completed his dual-degree (B.Tech. and M.Tech.) in Mechanical Engineering with specialization in Thermal Science and Engineering and his PhD from IIT Kharagpur, followed by postdoctoral research at IIT Gandhinagar and at CNRS Geosciences Rennes, France. He was awarded the ANRF Ramanujan Fellowship (2025) and the Région Bretagne Bienvenue Postdoctoral Fellowship (2024), and received the Horizon Europe MSCA Postdoctoral Fellowship Seal of Excellence (2022) and the Early Career Postdoctoral Fellowship and Research Grant at IIT Gandhinagar (2021). Over the course of his doctoral and postdoctoral work, he has collaborated with researchers at international institutions such as Purdue University, CNRS Geosciences Rennes and University of Brussels, contributing to interdisciplinary efforts at the interface of fluid mechanics, transport phenomena, and soft matter physics and applications.

 call 
 
 0291 244 1514 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Chandan Pandey completed his M.Tech. in Mechanical and Industrial Engineering from the IIT Roorkee Roorkee in 2012. He began his academic career as a faculty member at HCST, Farah, Mathura, where he served for approximately one year. He has completed Ph.D. in the area of welding from IIT Roorkee in 2018. Following his Ph.D., Dr. Pandey joined SRM University as a faculty member in July 2018. Shortly thereafter, he joined to the IIT Jodhpur in April 2018, where he currently holds the position of Associate Professor. During his academic tenure, Dr. Pandey contributed to research activities at IIT Jodhpur, broadening his expertise in welding, weld testing, materials characterization, and residual stress analysis. Over the past six years, Dr. Pandey has successfully supervised 06 PhDs and over 12 M.Tech. theses. His contributions to the field have been recognized through several prestigious honors, including the INAE Young Associateship (2024) and the Institution of Engineers (India) Young Engineers Award (2023–24).

 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Himanshu Dave 
 
 
 
 
 
 Himanshu Dave 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Himanshu Dave
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D Indian Institute of Science, Bangalore 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        himanshudave[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Clean combustion for power and propulsion | LES and DNS of turbulent combustion | Scientific machine learning  
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1534 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Himanshu Dave is currently an Assistant Professor in the Mechanical Engineering Department at IIT-Jodhpur. His research interests primarily are simulating turbulent flows and developing novel and green technologies, such as MILD combustion or hydrogen-based combustion. He is a skilled researcher on numerical simulations of reacting flows and its analysis using domain-specific and machine learning (ML) techniques. Prior to joining IIT-Jodhpur, he has worked for a year as a Lead Engineer – Combustion Modelling in GE Vernova, Bangalore. Between 2019 and 2023, he worked as an IF@ULB (Marie-Curie COFUND) postdoctoral fellow in ULB, Belgium, working on application of ML on combustion datasets. Between 2014 and 2019, he completed his PhD from the Indian Institute of Science (IISc) Bangalore in Aerospace Engineering working on direct numerical simulations (DNS) of turbulent premixed hydrogen-air flames. Between 2010 and 2014, he worked on liquid rocket engines as a Scientist/Engineer at Liquid Propulsions Systems Center (LPSC), ISRO. He holds a BE(Hons.) in Mechanical Engineering from BITS-Pilani, Goa Campus.

 Mechanical Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Ashish Pathak 
 
 
 
 
 
 Ashish Pathak 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Ashish Pathak
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD. University of Massachusetts Dartmouth 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        apathak[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Fluid-Structure Interaction; Ocean Energy; Phase-Change Problems; Multiphase flows; High Performance Computing 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1525 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 I joined IIT Jodhpur as an Assistant Professor in Mechanical Engineering in May 2021. I completed my B.Tech and M.Tech in Mechanical Engineering from IIT Kharagpur. During my undergraduate research with Prof. Ghosh Moulic, I became interested in numerical methods for modeling fluid flows, particularly those with discontinuities such as shocks and multiphase phenomena. For my B.Tech thesis, I evaluated numerical schemes for shock modeling in an adaptive mesh framework. My M.Tech thesis involved developing an in-house code for simulating mixed convection film condensation, using coordinate transformation and a body-fitted mesh to address matrix stiffness, solved via perturbation methods. I pursued my Ph.D. at the University of Massachusetts Dartmouth under Prof. Mehdi Raessi, focusing on two-phase flows interacting with moving rigid bodies. Due to significant interface deformation, I used the Volume-of-Fluid method on a fixed grid, avoiding remeshing and enabling large topological changes. This solver was applied to Ocean Wave Energy Converters, yielding key insights into dynamic similarity. As a Postdoctoral Fellow at UMass Dartmouth, I studied droplet dynamics from spray-wall interactions and developed a phase-change solver for evaporating, deforming interfaces.

 People 
 
 
 Gagan Kewalramani 
 
 
 
 
 
 Gagan Kewalramani 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Gagan Kewalramani
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: Universite de Lorraine  
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        gagank[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Turbulent flows, Fluid Mechanics, Heat transfer. 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1535 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Gagan Kewalramani joined the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur in April 2025. He completed his Ph.D. in March 2023 from the Laboratoire d'Énergétique et de Mécanique Théorique et Appliquée, affiliated with the University of Lorraine, France. Prior to his appointment at IIT Jodhpur, he worked as a postdoctoral researcher at the Laboratoire de Mécanique des Fluides de Lille, under the Centre National de la Recherche Scientifique, France. At IIT Jodhpur, Dr. Kewalramani's work focuses on experimental and theoretical investigations of fluid flows. His areas of research include non-stationary effects in turbulent flow modelling, two-phase turbulence and flows in turbomachinery.

 biotech 
 
 Computational Fracture Mechanics, Computational Solid Mechanics, Adaptive re-meshing strategy, Phase-field method 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1528 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Hirshikesh is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Mechanical Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur. Prior to joining IIT Jodhpur, he was a scientist at the IAM-Microstructure Modelling and Simulation Lab, Karlsruhe Institute of Technology (KIT) Germany.  He has completed his M.S and PhD degree from the Department of Mechanical Engineering at IIT Madras, India, where he was affiliated with the Integrated Modeling and Simulation Laboratory (iMSL) and the Mechanics of Materials Laboratory.  His research interests include computational fracture mechanics, computational solid mechanics, phase-field method for fracture, fracture due to multi-physical interactions, and adaptive re-meshing strategies.

 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Harshal Akolekar 
 
 
 
 
 
 Harshal Akolekar 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Harshal Akolekar
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD. University of Melbourne, Australia 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        harshal[dot]akolekar[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Aerodynamics; Computational Fluid Dynamics; Machine Learning; Submarine Hydrodynamics; Turbomachinery 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1527 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Harshal Akolekar  is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Mechanical Engineering and the School of AI and Data Science at the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur. His research focuses on the development of turbulence and transition models using advanced machine learning approaches, including evolutionary algorithms and deep learning, to enhance the accuracy of low-fidelity computational fluid dynamics (CFD) simulations for gas turbine applications. He is also actively involved in developing coursework at the intersection of machine learning and fluid mechanics. Prior to joining IIT Jodhpur, he was a postdoctoral researcher at the University of Melbourne, Australia. Before this, he worked on industry-driven computational fluid dynamics problems related to underwater vehicles. He received his M.E. and Ph.D. in machine-learning-based computational fluid dynamics from the University of Melbourne in collaboration with GE Aerospace. He completed his undergraduate studies in Mechanical Engineering at BITS Pilani.

                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr.  Atul Kumar Sharma is an Assistant Professor at the Department of Mechanical Engineering, Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur, India. Dr. Sharma received his Ph.D. in Mechanical Engineering from IIT Roorkee in 2019 and joined as a postdoctoral fellow at Technion-Israel Institute of Technology. In 2020, he joined the Department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Jodhpur. His research is concerned with mechanics of soft active materials.

 Mechanical Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 B Ravindra 
 
 
 
 
 
 B Ravindra 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            B Ravindra
                         
 
                                Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD. Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        ravib[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Design, Dynamics, Vibration and Control 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1503 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 B. Ravindra joined IIT Jodhpur in 2010. He obtained his BTech from NIT Warangal in 1987 and M.Tech from IIT Kanpur in 1989 both in the area of Mechanical engineering. He obtained his PhD in 1994 from IIT Kanpur in the area of Nonlinear Vibrations. His main areas of research are Nonlinear Vibrations, Mechatronics, Vehicle dynamics and Renewable energy. Prior to joining IIT Jodhpur, he did his postdoctoral work in HongKong in the year 1995-1996. Subsequently he spent two years as an Alexander von Humboldt fellow at Technical university of Darmstadt, Germany. During 1998-2000 he worked in gas turbine industry with Alstom power. He worked on shape memory alloy actuators for automotive applications at the General Motors R&D center in Bangalore. At the IIT Jodhpur, he was involved in establishing a rooftop solr PV plant and Mechanical engineering laboratories. He was also a member of Building works committee and was actively involved in the campus construction and sustainable buildings research. He conducted a GIAN program on Vehicle dynamics. He is also involved in "Bridging the Innovation gap in schools" project to promote establishment of tinkering labs in rural schools.

 Mechanical Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Srijit Biswas 
 
 
 
 
 
 Srijit Biswas 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Srijit Biswas
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD. National Institute of Technology, Agartala 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        sbiswas[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 IC engines, alternative fuels, combustion, performance, emissions (Experimental and Simulation), UAV propulsion, and sustainability assessment through Life Cycle Assessment (LCA), Techno-Economic Assessment (TEA), and Socio-Economic Assessment (SEA).  
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1539 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr Srijit Biswas is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Mechanical Engineering and Associate Centre Head of the Manekshaw Centre for National Security Studies and Research at IIT Jodhpur. He holds a PhD in Mechanical Engineering from NIT Agartala. His expertise spans IC engines, alternative fuels, combustion, performance, emissions (Experimental and Simulation), UAV propulsion, and sustainability assessment through Life Cycle Assessment (LCA), Techno-Economic Assessment (TEA), and Socio-Economic Assessment (SEA). Prior to joining IIT Jodhpur, he served as a CSIR-Pool Scientist at IIT Kanpur, where he worked extensively on LCA and TCO analysis of Indian transportation powertrains, as well as the design & development of UAV engines. Dr Biswas has authored over 22 SCIE journal papers, nine conference papers, and one edited book with Springer. He is a recipient of the Prof. P. K. Bose Young Scientist Award (2024) by ISEES and frequently serves as an invited speaker in various OEM forums.

 school 
 
 PhD. Indian Institute of Technology Bombay 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        hardikkothadia[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Multiphase Flow, Boiling and Condensation, Heat Transfer, Fluid Mechanics, Gasification 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1512 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Hardik Kothadia is
an Associate Professor in the Department of Mechanical Engineering. He is also
affiliated with the Rishab Center for Research and Innovation in Clean Energy
and the IITJ-AIIMSJ Medical Technology Center. He received his Ph.D. from IIT
Bombay in the area of multiphase flows and heat transfer. He is the recipient
of the National Award for Best M.Tech Thesis by the Indian Society for
Technical Education (ISTE) for his research on lignite coal gasification. He
received the Excellence in Thesis Work Award from IIT Bombay for his
outstanding research contributions during his Ph.D. At IIT Jodhpur, Dr.
Kothadia has been honoured with the Teaching Excellence Award (2021) and the
Institute Award for Research Excellence (2025) in the Young Researcher
category.   
 With a prolific
academic record, he has authored over 80 research papers published in reputed
international journals and conferences. He is the PI/Co-PI in 14 sponsored and
consultancy projects, securing total funding exceeding ₹20 crore. His
collaborations span major industrial and government bodies, including Thermax
Limited, DRDO, DST, ANRF, ARDB, ISRO, DAE, ICMR, the Ministry of Defence and
the Ministry of MSME. In the domain of innovation, Dr. Kothadia has been
granted one patent and has filed four provisional patents.

 Mechanical Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Arun Kumar R 
 
 
 
 
 
 Arun Kumar R 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Arun Kumar R
                         
 
                                Associate Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD. Indian Institute of Technology Madras 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        arunkr[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Experimental Aerodynamics - Confined Jets, Shock Wave Reflection and Transitions, Ejector Flows. 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1516 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Arun Kumar R  is an  Associate Professor  in the  Department of Mechanical Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jodhpur . His area of expertise lies in  high-speed flows and shock waves . He has established the  Shock Waves and High-Speed Flow Laboratory (SWAHS Lab)  at IIT Jodhpur, which focuses on experimental and computational research related to supersonic and hypersonic flow phenomena, including fuel injection, mixing enhancement, and blast wave interactions. The  Shock Waves and High-Speed Flow Laboratory (SWAHS Lab)  is dedicated to advancing the frontiers of research and technology development in the  aerospace and defense sectors . In addition to traditional high-speed aerodynamic applications, the lab actively explores  multidisciplinary extensions  of shock wave and high-speed flow research into fields such as  refrigeration systems, biomedical engineering, industrial processing, and water purification . Ongoing research includes projects on  supersonic intakes ,  supersonic air-fuel mixing in scramjet combustors ,  flow control and actuators ,  gas turbine cooling ,  electric propulsion systems ,  shock and blast wave attenuation , as well as  novel applications  like  solar ejector refrigeration ,  steam ejector-based water purification , and  shock wave-assisted biomedical techniques .

 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Ram Prakash 
 
 
 
 
 
 Ram Prakash 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Ram Prakash
                         
 
                                Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD: Centre of Plasma Physics-Institute for Plasma Research Guwahati 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        ramprakash[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Plasma Science & Technology: Low temperature plasma applications 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1615 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        Biography
                     
 
 
 
		Prof. Ram Prakash is a plasma physicist and currently serving as
		a Professor in the Department of Physics at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jodhpur. He
		joined IIT jodhpur as an Associate Professor in the year 2019. He
		earned his Ph.D. in Plasma Physics from the Centre of Plasma
		Physics–Institute for Plasma Research (CPP-IPR), Guwahati (a DAE, GoI
		Unit) in the year 2002. He worked on ADITYA Tokamak as Post-Doctoral
		Fellow at Institute for Plasma Research, Gandhinagar during 2002-2004.
		Later worked as Faculty at Birla Institute of Technology, Mesra during
		2004-2011. During 2006-2007, he also worked at Max-Planck Institute for Plasmaphysik, Garching, Germany on ASDEX Upgrade Tokamak as Guest Scientist under BOYSCAST Fellowship scheme of Govt. of India. Prior to his current assignment, he worked as Senior
		Scientist and Principal Scientist at CSIR-Central Electronics
		Engineering Research Institute (CSIR-CEERI), Pilani, and was group
		leader in the Plasma Devices Lab during 2011-2017. He established
		CSIR-CEERI's Incubation-cum-Innovation Hub, Jaipur Centre and IIT
		Jodhpur’s Technology Innovation and Start-up Centre, and was the first
		Scientist-in-charge and Faculty-in-charge during 2017-2019 and
		2019-2021, respectively. He was also the first faculty-in-charge of
		IIT Jodhpur Technology Park during 2021-2023 and has mentored three
		start-ups. Further, he has served as Dean of Infrastructure Planning(DoIP) at IIT Jodhpur during Sept 2023 - Aug 2025.    He has been honoured with several
		prestigious awards including the PSSI Jaidutt Saraswati Sodha Award
		(2022), SERC Fast Track Young Scientist Award (2003), BOYSCAST
		Fellowship (2005), and CSIR-CEERI’s Foundation Day Award for
		Excellence in Research (2018). He is one of the editors of Nature:
		Scientific Reports Journal, was a Vice-President of the Plasma Science
		Society of India during 2022–2024, and was also a scientific
		consultant to the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) in Vienna
		in the year 2009. His contributions to science communication in Hindi
		are well-known and he received a Vishesh Vaigyanik Rajbhasha
		Vishishtha Samman in the year 2012.
	 
 
 
 
 
                        Research
                     
 
 
 Prof. Ram Prakash is currently
		working on low temperature plasma applications and spearheading the
		development of a state-of-the-art Cold Plasma Laboratory aimed at
		delivering innovative, plasma-based solutions in the fields of health,
		food, energy, and agriculture. His team has made significant research
		contributions using cold plasmas for clean air, water purity,
		environmental safety, and efficient energy systems. The unique aspects
		of his work are related to innovations that have involved design,
		development, and translation of fundamental research into devices and
		systems that can be used in the field by common man and industries. He
		has successfully developed six technologies -Four technologies for
		commercialization by industries and two technologies for strategic
		sectors. The work done on novel Cold-plasma Detergent in Environment
		(CODE) Device for better indoor air quality, Advanced Photocatalytic
		Oxidation Systems to fight COVID-19 pandemic, Mercury-Free-Plasma
		UV-lamps for efficient water disinfection, VUV-spectrometer-detector
		system calibration source useful for tokamak machines, Radial
		Multi-channel High-current Pseudospark Switch useful for high pulse
		power applications, Sheet-beam Plasma Cathode Electron Gun useful for
		THz microwave generation are a few of them to mention.

 People 
 
 
 Naveen Kumar Tailor 
 
 
 
 
 
 Naveen Kumar Tailor 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Naveen Kumar Tailor
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD: Indian Institute of Technology Roorkee 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        nktailor[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Solar Cells, Photodetectors and X-ray detectors, CO 2 Capture and Conversion, Green Hydrogen, Fundamental properties investigation in emerging materials 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1643 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Naveen Kumar Tailor is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Physics at the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur (IITJ), where he joined in October 2025. Before taking up this position, he served as a Fulbright-Nehru Postdoctoral Fellow at Northwestern University, Evanston, USA, working with renowned materials scientist Prof. Mercouri Kanatzidis on cutting-edge research in energy materials and semiconductor devices.  Dr. Tailor earned his Ph.D. in Physics from the Indian Institute of Technology Roorkee, where his research was recognized with the Excellence in Doctoral Research Award in 2023. His academic journey began with an M.Sc. in Physics from the University of Rajasthan, Jaipur, in 2017.  Dr. Tailor has authored over 55 journal publications and holds two granted patents. One of his notable research outcomes has been highlighted by Nature India, reflecting the societal and scientific relevance of his work. He has been honored with several prestigious international fellowships, including the Fulbright-Nehru Postdoctoral Fellowship, the JSPS Fellowship (Japan), and the Humboldt Fellowship (Germany). He is serving as a reviewer in various scientific journals.  He is widely recognized for his collaborative research approach, academic excellence, and active engagement in leadership and extracurricular activities, reflecting his commitment to impactful science and societal development. 
 
 
 
 
                        Research
                     
 
 
 We are a young and dynamic research team working at the forefront of experimental physics. Driven by curiosity and sustained by creativity, our group is committed to advancing scientific knowledge while developing novel technologies that address societal needs. We believe that research is most impactful when it bridges fundamental science with practical applications, especially in areas that contribute to sustainability and a cleaner energy future.  Our work combines laboratory experimentation, device engineering, spectroscopy, and computational tools to explore materials, interfaces, and energy conversion mechanisms. With a strong interdisciplinary approach, we aim to create solutions that support global energy demands, healthcare advancements, environmental protection, and next-generation optoelectronic technologies.   Our Major Research Areas  Solar Energy and Photovoltaics  Perovskite solar cells (3D, 2D–3D, and tandem architectures)  Tandem solar cells combining perovskite–silicon and perovskite–CIGS technologies  Emerging concepts in silicon solar cells  Building-integrated photovoltaics (BIPV) and Agrivoltaics  Space photovoltaics for extreme radiation environments  Solar thermal systems and energy storage integration  X-ray Detectors & Imaging  Medical diagnostics  Security scanning and border inspection  High-energy and hard radiation detection using lead-free halide crystals and semiconductor materials  Photodetectors & IR Imaging  Broadband photodetectors  Infrared detectors for imaging, surveillance, and night-vision applications  Device engineering for high sensitivity, low noise, and faster response  Carbon Capture & CO₂ Conversion  Photo-catalytic and photo-electrochemical CO₂ reduction  Conversion of CO₂ into value-added fuels and chemicals  Development of catalysts and interface engineering for higher efficiency  Green Hydrogen Technologies  Water electrolysis and hydrogen evolution reactions (HER)  Design of high-performance electrode and catalyst materials  Scalable electrolyzer development for green hydrogen production  Ultrafast Transient Spectroscopy  Light–matter interaction dynamics in emerging semiconductor and quantum materials  Carrier cooling, phonon interactions, defect states, and interfacial kinetics  Fundamental Materials Physics:  Using electrical and optical spectroscopy along with structural correlation studies, we probe:  Band structure and carrier transport  Defect dynamics, exciton behavior, and electron-phonon coupling  Charge recombination and ion migration in halide perovskites and related materials  Computational & Device Simulations  Solar and optoelectronic device optimization  Optical modeling, charge transport simulations, and interface engineering  Predicting material properties and device behavior  We warmly welcome dedicated, motivated, and enthusiastic students to become part of our research group. Our group offers a collaborative environment where students can develop strong scientific skills, gain hands-on laboratory experience, and contribute to impactful research aimed at solving real-world challenges. If you are interested in joining us, please reach out with your latest CV/resume, along with a brief statement of your research interests.  We look forward to working with passionate minds and nurturing future researchers!

 People 
 
 
 Dushyant Kumar 
 
 
 
 
 
 Dushyant Kumar 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Dushyant Kumar
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD: Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        dushyantkumar[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Topological Quantum Materials; Energy Harvesting (3G, 4G, 5G, upto THz); Sensing; Non-linear Spintronics; Quantum Engineering; Mapping 3D Spin Texture. 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1620 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        Biography
                     
 
 
 Dr. Dushyant Kumar is currently an Assistant Professor in the Department of Physics at the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur (IITJ), having joined in March 2025. Previously, he held the position of Assistant Professor at Netaji Subhas University of Technology (NSUT) in New Delhi from November 2022 to March 2025. Prior to that, Dr. Dushyant worked as a Postdoctoral Fellow at the National University of Singapore (NUS) between 2016 and 2022. He was awarded his Ph.D. in Physics from the Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur (IITK) in 2016. Between thesis submission to degree award, he contributed his expertise as SRF at the National Physical Laboratory (NPL) New Delhi. His academic foundation was laid in 2009 with M.Sc. in Physics as a Gold Medalist from Banaras Hindu University (BHU), Varanasi. 
 
 
 
 
                        Research
                     
 
 
 Dr. Dushyant Kumar's research investigates the fundamental properties and applications of novel quantum materials, with a significant emphasis on two-dimensional topological quantum materials. 
His work explores their intrinsic quantum properties, including nonlinearity, for applications in spintronics, energy harvesting, and sensing. 
His research group employs advanced device fabrication techniques, such as e-beam lithography, for the development of components relevant to quantum communication and quantum computing. 
The group's activities encompass thin film growth, device fabrication, and comprehensive electrical transport and optical measurements.
 His key research interests include 
 
 Topological quantum materials for next-generation spintronic devices.  
 Spin texture characterization in topological materials and perovskites. 
 Nonlinear quantum properties for sensing and energy harvesting applications. 
 Quantum rectification at high frequencies, spanning 4G, 5G, and up to THz. 
 Device development for quantum communication and quantum computing technologies.
