 Jitendra Kumar 
 
 
 
 
 
 Jitendra Kumar 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Jitendra Kumar
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD: Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        jitendrak[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Thin-Film Photovoltaic Devices, Nanomaterials, Semiconductor Devices, Micro-fabrication Technologies, Materials for Energy Applications, and Flexible Electronic Devices. 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1609 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        Biography
                     
 
 
 Dr. Jitendra Kumar is Joint Faculty in the Department of Physics and the Department of Metallurgical & Materials Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur. He completed his B.Tech. in Electronics & Communication Engineering from Uttar Pradesh Technical University, Lucknow, in 2009, followed by an M.Tech. in Nanotechnology from the Indian Institute of Technology Roorkee in 2011. Dr. Kumar earned his Ph.D. from the Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur in 2019, where his research focused on the synthesis and characterization of solution-processed Cu2ZnSnS4 thin films for solar cell applications.
After completing his Ph.D., Dr. Kumar worked as an Institute Postdoctoral Fellow at the Indian Institute of Technology Delhi until 2020. During this tenure, his research primarily focused on the synthesis of inorganic perovskite nanocubes (CsPbIxBr3-x) using a hot-injection technique for solar cell applications. From 2020 to 2023, he held a postdoctoral position at Ben-Gurion University of the Negev, Israel, where his research centered on (BixSb1-x)2Se3-based solar cells. Between August 2023 and April 2025, he served as an INSPIRE Faculty at IIT Dhanbad, leading research on BaZrS3 thin-film solar cells. In February and March 2025, he was Marie Curie Fellow at INL – International Iberian Nanotechnology Laboratory.  
Dr. Kumar has received several prestigious honors, including the Marie Sklodowska-Curie Award in 2024 and the Marie Sklodowska-Curie Seal of Excellence in 2023. He was also awarded the INSPIRE Faculty Fellowship in 2023. Additionally he received international travel grants from EPFL and DST, which enabled him to present his research in Switzerland in 2021 and in the USA in 2018. He won the Best Poster Presentation Award at the IEEE Conference in Bangalore in 2018. Dr. Kumar has actively participated in and presented his work at numerous international conferences. Furthermore, he has been a visiting researcher at Martin Luther University, Halle-Wittenberg, Germany, in 2015 and at the Centre for Nanoscience & Engineering at the Indian Institute of Science Bangalore in 2014. 
 
 
 
 
                        Research
                     
 
 
 Our research group is dedicated to the exploring advanced materials for energy applications, with a special emphasis on the development of inorganic thin-film solar cells. We strive to contribute to a sustainable future by advancing the innovation in materials design, fabrication and device engineering.
Feel free to explore our work, meet our team, and join us in building a cleaner and greener future. To learn more about our research, please visit research group  webpage

 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Jitendra Kumar 
 
 
 
 
 
 Jitendra Kumar 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Jitendra Kumar
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD: Indian Institute of Technology, Bombay 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        jkumar[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Experimental High Energy Physics, Particle Detection Techniques, and ML in HEP 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1622 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        Biography
                     
 
 
 Dr. Jitendra Kumar is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Physics at the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur. He joined the institute in February 2023. Prior to this, he was a postdoctoral fellow at Carnegie Mellon University (CMU), Pittsburgh, USA (from 2018), and he continued at CMU as a Special Faculty Researcher until early 2023. At CMU, he worked on the Belle II experiment at SuperKEKB, Japan, where he contributed to the development and analysis of the Central Drift Chamber and conducted studies in flavor physics.  Belle II is one of the world’s leading experiments in precision flavor physics, aiming to test the Standard Model and search for signs of new physics through rare decays and CP violation measurements. Dr. Jitendra is part of the Belle II Collaboration from IIT Jodhpur.
 
His doctoral research, conducted in collaboration with the ALICE (A Large Ion Collider Experiment) experiment at the Large Hadron Collider (LHC), CERN, Switzerland, focused on heavy-flavor azimuthal correlations in high-energy proton-proton collisions. ALICE is a major experiment at the LHC dedicated to studying the quark-gluon plasma and the properties of strongly interacting matter under extreme conditions. Following his Ph.D., degree Dr. Jitendra served as a Research Associate and Project Scientist at IIT Bombay. 
 
 
 
 
                        Research
                     
 
 
 Key areas; Flavour Physics Hadron spectroscopy and QCD AI/ML in High-Energy Physics Detector studies  
Dr. Jitendra’s research group at IIT Jodhpur is working on experimental high-energy particle physics, with a primary focus on flavor physics (e.g., LFUV, CP violation, rare decays) at the Belle II experiment. He also works on hadron spectroscopy (e.g., pentaquarks) to investigate the dynamics of Quantum Chromodynamics (QCD) in the non-perturbative regime. In addition, the group investigates the application of AI/ML techniques to enhance data analysis and search for rare processes or beyond-the-standard-model (BSM) physics. His group is also exploring quantum measurement concepts for precision measurements and foundational aspects of particle physics.

 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Shahab Ahmad 
 
 
 
 
 
 Shahab Ahmad 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Shahab Ahmad
                         
 
                                Associate Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD: Indian Institute of Technology Delhi 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        shahab[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Condensed Matter Physics, Nanomaterials, Optoelectronics, Energy Storage Devices, Solar Cells, Photo-detectors, Light Emitting Diodes 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1617 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        Biography
                     
 
 
 Dr. Shahab Ahmad is currently an Associate Professor at the Department of Physics, IIT Jodhpur. He has joined IIT Jodhpur as an Assistant Professor in 2019. He has received PhD in Physics from IIT Delhi in 2014 and won the Distinction in Doctoral Research Award. During PhD he has also worked as a visiting researcher at the NanoPhotonics Centre, Cavendish Laboratory at the University of Cambridge, UK (2011 and 2014). Subsequently he has gained post-doctoral research experience of 3 years (2014-2017) at the NanoManufacturing Centre, University of Cambridge, UK. Prior to joining IIT Jodhpur, he has worked as an Assistant Professor at the Centre for Nanoscience and Nanotechnology, Jamia Millia Islamia (Central University), New Delhi for 2 years (2017-2019). So far he has authored more than 55 research articles in internationally reputed journals including Nature, Advanced Materials, Advanced Energy Materials, Advanced Functional Materials, Nano Letters, ACS Nano, JACS, Small etc. He is recipient of several awards and fellowships, and known in the scientific community for the development of first single active material based Photo-rechargeable Li-Ion battery in a two-electrode configuration.   
 
 
 
 
                        Research
                     
 
 
 Dr. Shahab Ahmad has formed and leading the ‘Advanced Energy Materials Group’ at the Department of Physics, IIT Jodhpur. His research group is focused on experimental investigations of the fundamental properties of low-dimensional materials by using various types of Optical, Electronic and Electrochemical probes to explore and demonstrate the potential of these materials for Energy Applications ranging from Energy Storage to Optoelectronic and Photonic devices. In view of interdisciplinary nature of his research work, the ‘Advanced Energy Materials Group’ extensively work in the research domains of ‘Experimental Condensed Matter Physics’ and ‘Optics & Photonics’. The current research interest of the group includes: 
 
 Photo-rechargeable and Photo-enhanced Energy Storage Devices  
 Lithium-Ion, Lithium-Sulphur and Emerging Metal-Ion Batteries 
 Nanostructured and Hierarchically Engineered Battery Electrodes 
 Scalable and cost-effective Production of Batteries 
 Optoelectronic Investigations of 2D and Quasi-2D Metal Halide Perovskites 
 Band-gap Engineering and Photonic studies of Metal Halide Perovskites 
 Solar Assisted Water Splitting for Hydrogen Production

 People 
 
 
 Sunita 
 
 
 
 
 
 Sunita 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Sunita
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD: Banaras Hindu University, Varanasi 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        sunita[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Theoretical soft matter and physics of living organisms (active matter; intracellular phase transitions; properties of soft elastic materials; charge regulation effects on biomolecules; liquid crystals) 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1618 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        Biography
                     
 
 
 I have joined Dept. of Physics, IIT
		Jodhpur in September 2022 as an Assistant Professor. Prior to that, I
		worked as a postdoctoral fellow at the University of Chinese Academy
		of Sciences, China, and the University of Lisbon, Portugal with Prof.
		Nuno Araujo and Prof. Margarida Telo Gamma. In JNCASR, Bangalore, I
		worked as a research associate with Prof. Subir K Das. I received
		doctoral degree in Physics ​from Banaras Hindu University. During my
		PhD, I have constructed the first thermodynamics model to analyze the
		nonlinear dielectric effect and the effect of pressure on the
		structure and properties of the achiral, bent-core, and ferroelectric
		liquid crystals. 
 
 
 
 
                        Research
                     
 
 
 Our group focus on understanding
		the physical mechanisms underlying soft materials in biological
		perspectives and active matter systems. We aim to develop analytical,
		statistical mechanics, electrodynamics, and computational techniques
		to address the most exciting unsolved scientific challenges in these
		highly interdisciplinary fields. Current research topics include the
		following problems- 
 
 Theoretical modeling of nature-inspired soft robots 
 Electrostatics in biophysics and technical applications 
 Liquid-liquid phase separation in biology 
 Physics of Active(self propelled) particles 
 Flow physics of micro-organisms 
 Phase transitions in liquid crystals

 Yogeshwar Nath Mishra | Physics | IIT Jodhpur 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 ###147852369$$$_RedirectToLoginPage_%%%963258741!!!

 Physics 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Yogeshwar Nath Mishra 
 
 
 
 
 
 Yogeshwar Nath Mishra 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Yogeshwar Nath Mishra
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD: Lund University, Sweden 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        mishrayn[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Ultrafast Imaging, Laser Diagnostics, Photonics,  Energy Technology, Biomedical Imaging 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1624 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        Biography
                     
 
 
 Dr. Yogeshwar Nath Mishra is an Assistant Professor at IIT Jodhpur. He earned his Ph.D. in Optics and Engineering Physics from Lund University, Sweden, in 2018, under the supervision of Prof. 
Edouard Berrocal, where he developed advanced laser diagnostics for probing optically dense media. He subsequently held postdoctoral and research scientist positions at FAU Erlangen in Germany, 
and jointly at the University of Gothenburg in Sweden and NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory at the California Institute of Technology (Caltech) in the USA. 
 He has been mentored by several globally renowned scientists and pioneers in their fields, including Prof. Lihong Wang (Caltech), 
Dr. Murthy Gudipati (NASA), and Prof. Wolfgang Heidrich (KAUST).  
 Dr. Mishra has received numerous accolades, including the Indian Academy of Sciences Summer Research Fellowship (2010), Visiting Student Fellowship from Raman Research Institute (2011), SPIE Student Author Travel Award (2012), PETRA Awards for Young Researchers - ILASS Europe (2013), ÅForsk Foundation Scholarship (2015), Invited Speaker, Gordon Research Conference (2017), Swedish Research Council International Postdoctoral Fellowship (2018), Adlerbert Foundation Sustainable Research Fellowship – Gothenburg (2022), Dr. K. Radhakrishnan Young Scientist Award (2023) from ISEES–IIT Kanpur, and the Young Researcher Award (2025, Runner-up) from Erlangen Graduate School in Advanced Optical Technologies, FAU Erlangen, Germany.  
 While at Caltech, he co-invented laser sheet compressed ultrafast photography (LS-CUP), a groundbreaking technique recognized as the World’s Fastest single-shot planar imaging method. 
This work, conducted under Prof. Lihong Wang, was among the top-downloaded articles in the prestigious Light: Science and Applications journal in 2023. 
More recently, he reported CUP2AI, recognized as the world’s fastest single-shot wide-field molecular mapping technique. 
His research has been featured in Physics World, Phys.org, EurekaAlert! (AAAS), Nature India, Swedish National Radio, Caltech,  The Hindu, The Indian Express, The Lallantop, Republic TV, among other national and international news outlets. 
Dr. Mishra has served as an Adjunct Faculty at IIT Indore, where he founded Fourier Energy Solutions Pvt. Ltd. along with his colleagues, 
focusing on developing smart optical solutions for energy technology and biomedical research.  
 
 
 
 
                        Research
                     
 
 
 We are young and dynamic team engaged in cutting-edge research in Optics and Photonics. Our group thrives on curiosity, creativity, and collaboration as we explore both fundamental science and real-world applications of optical technologies for a sustainable future and the benefit of society. Our research includes:
 
 
 Laser Diagnostics  in engines, fuel cells, and photovoltaic systems. 
 Ultrafast Science , focusing on light-matter interaction dynamics. 
 Space Science , including adaptive optics and thermal/hyperspectral imaging. 
 Quantum Technologies , with a focus quantum-enhanced imaging techniques. 
 Biomedical Imaging and Artificial Intelligence:  lab-on-chip diagnostic platforms for biomedial applications. 
 We actively welcome enthusiastic students and researchers to join us. For internships, Ph.D. admissions, or self-sponsored positions, please contact with your CV.

                                            ऑप्टिक्स और सौर क्षेत्र डिज़ाइन, प्लास्मोनिक्स, लेज़र द्वारा उत्पादित प्लाज्मा (एलपीपी), पल्स्ड लेज़र निक्षेपण (पीएलडी), प्लाज्मा डायग्नोस्टिक्स (इंटरफेरोमेट्री और ऑप्टिकल एमिशन स्पेक्ट्रोस्कोपी (ओईएस)), लेज़र-द्रव्य अन्तःक्रिया और लेज़र-क्लस्टर अन्तःक्रिया।

 People 
 
 
 Lata Panwar 
 
 
 
 
 
 Lata Panwar 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Lata Panwar
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD: Indian Institute of Science, Bangalore, Karnataka 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        lata[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Experimental High Energy Physics. 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1625 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        Biography
                     
 
 
 Dr. Lata Panwar is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Physics at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jodhpur. She joined the institute in April 2025.
She earned her B.Sc. (Hons.) and M.Sc. degrees in Physics from the University of Rajasthan (2011 - 2014, 2014 - 2016), and completed her Ph.D. in Experimental High Energy Particle Physics at the Indian Institute of Science (IISc), Bangalore, in 2022. Her doctoral research, under the supervision of Dr. Jyothsna Rani Komaragiri, focused on searches for nonresonant and resonant Di-Higgs production at 13 TeV using proton-proton collision data from the CMS detector at the Large Hadron Collider (LHC), CERN.
 Following her Ph.D., Dr. Panwar pursued postdoctoral research at the Laboratoire de Physique Nucléaire et des Hautes Énergies (LPNHE)-CNRS-Sorbonne Université / Université Paris Cité, working with the ATLAS collaboration. She collaborated with Dr. Bogdan Malaescu, Dr. Luc Poggioli and Dr. Reina Camacho Toro on deriving in situ Jet Energy Scale (JES) calibrations for ATLAS and on Quantum Chromodynamics (QCD) and Lund Jet Plane studies at ATLAS and at the proposed Future Circular Collider-electron-positron (FCC-ee) experiment.
At IIT Jodhpur, Dr. Panwar is building a dynamic research group in Experimental High Energy Physics, focusing on Higgs and Jet Physics at the LHC and FCC. 
 
 
 
 
                        Research
                     
 
 
 Our research group at IIT Jodhpur is a growing team engaged in cutting-edge research in the field of Experimental High Energy Physics (EHEP). We are actively involved in exploring fundamental aspects of particle physics through both current and future collider experiments.
Our primary research interests include:
 Jet performance studies  Data analyses using LHC datasets, with a focus on Higgs and QCD physics, including the application of modern techniques such as machine learning  Projection and feasibility studies for the proposed Future Circular Collider (FCC)
 
We invite enthusiastic students and researchers to collaborate with us and contribute to the growing landscape of experimental high energy physics.

 biotech

 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        Biography
                     
 
 
 I am currently an associate
		professor in the Department of Physics, IIT Jodhpur. I have joined IIT
		Jodhpur as an assistant professor in 2011. Before joining IIT jodhpur,
		I worked as an Alexander von Humboldt fellow in Institute of
		Theoretical Physics, J. W. Goethe University, Frankfurt, Germany.
		Prior to that I worked as Centenary Fellow in department of Physics,
		Indian Institute of Science (IISc.), Bangalore, India for one year. I
		was a research associate in Theory Division of Saha Institute of
		Nuclear Physics, Kolkata India for three years. I obtained my Ph. D.
		degree from Jadavpur University in 2006. 
 
 
 
 
                        Research
                     
 
 
 I work on interface of
		nuclear-particle physics and astrophysics. My interests in in compact
		stellar objects composed of highly dense matter. It focusses on the
		application of dense matter model to the astrophysical observables.
		The research field covers Pulsars, Magentars, X-ray binaries

 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Sagar Kumar Verma 
 
 
 
 
 
 Sagar Kumar Verma 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Sagar Kumar Verma
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD: Indian Institute of Technology, Roorkee 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        sagarkv[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Nanophotonics, Plasmonic, Metasurfaces,
Metamaterials, Laser Sintering, Ultrafast Time-Resolved Spectroscopies. 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1642 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Sagar Kumar Verma joined the Department of Physics at the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur (IITJ) as an Assistant Professor in October 2025. Before joining IITJ, he served as a Postdoctoral Research Faculty in the School of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science at Oregon State University (OSU), Corvallis, USA.  Dr. Verma obtained his Ph.D. and M.Tech. degrees in Photonics from the Indian Institute of Technology Roorkee (IITR) in January 2024 and June 2019, respectively. He began his academic journey with a B.Sc. (2013) and an M.Sc. (2015) in Physics from Chaudhary Charan Singh University, Meerut, India.  In recognition of his research excellence, Dr. Verma was awarded the  Japan Society for the Promotion of Science (JSPS)  Postdoctoral Fellowship in August 2025 — one of the most prestigious and competitive international fellowships. Earlier, in July 2025, he also received the Institute Postdoctoral Fellowship offer from the  University of California, Irvine, USA , for his significant contributions to the field of Nanophotonics. He is serving as a reviewer in various scientific journals, including Plasmonics, Optics Letters, Applied Optics, Journal of the Optical Society of America: A (JOSA A), Journal of the Optical Society of America: B (JOSA B), Optics Express, Optical Material Express, OSA Continuum, Nanotechnology, IEEE Sensors Journal, International Journal of Optics, Optics Communications, Optical and Quantum Electronics and Scientific Reports. He is also contributing as a Reviewing Editor in the  Springer Nature Publishing  group. 
 
 
 
 
                        Research
                     
 
 
 Dr. Verma’s research focuses on the frontier areas of Applied Meta-Optics, Advanced Nanophotonics, and Adaptive Manufacturing. His research group at IIT Jodhpur is committed not only to advancing the fundamental understanding of light–matter interaction at the nanoscale but also to translating these innovations into real-world technologies for societal benefit. His group’s research interests include:  Advanced Nanophotonics:  Dr. Verma’s work in nanophotonics aims to design and fabricate plasmonic and dielectric metasurfaces that uncover the intricate physics of light manipulation at subwavelength scales. His research explores several advanced optical phenomena, including:  Surface Lattice Resonances (SLRs)  Gap Plasmons (GPs)  Extraordinary Optical Transmission (EOT)  Electromagnetically Induced Transparency (EIT)  Bound States in the Continuum (BIC)  Fano Resonances  Surface-Enhanced Infrared Absorption (SEIRA)  Surface-Enhanced Raman Scattering (SERS)  Surface-Enhanced Fluorescence (SEF)  His group applies these nanophotonic principles across diverse applications such as chemical and biosensing, spectral filtering, photodetection, smart thermal coatings for heat protection, tunable light emission, and infrared up-conversion for efficient energy harvesting.  Ultrafast Optics:  Dr. Verma also investigates ultrafast optical phenomena to probe time-resolved dynamics in novel materials and nanostructures. By employing advanced techniques such as Time-Domain Thermoreflectance (TDTR), Transient Absorption, and Transient Fluorescence Spectroscopy, his research provides insights into thermal transport and charge-carrier dynamics in emerging 2D materials, integrated with nanophotonic architectures to exploit enhanced electromagnetic field interactions.  Adaptive Manufacturing:  In the realm of adaptive manufacturing, Dr. Verma’s group focuses on optimizing Laser Sintering Techniques for the fabrication of printed electronic devices, particularly aimed at In-space applications. This work bridges photonics, materials science, and manufacturing technology to realize scalable and high-performance optoelectronic systems.  Vision:  Through a multidisciplinary approach that unites fundamental optics, nanoscale engineering, and advanced fabrication, Dr. Verma’s group aspires to develop next-generation meta-optical devices and nanophotonic technologies that push the boundaries of light manipulation and enable transformative innovations in sensing, energy, and quantum photonics.  Note:  Dr. Verma’s group welcomes passionate students and researchers with innovative ideas to pursue their research aspirations and contribute to advancing the future of Optics and Photonics. Interested candidates are encouraged to reach out for short-term internships, Ph.D. opportunities, or self-sponsored positions by sharing their CVs.

 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Monika Tanwar 
 
 
 
 
 
 Monika Tanwar 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Monika Tanwar
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor  
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD:  Indian Institute of Technology, Delhi 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        monikatanwar[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Operations & Maintenance Management, Prognostics and Health Management, Failure Analytics, Reliability Engineering, Digital Twin 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1808 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Monika Tanwar is an Assistant Professor at the School of Management and Entrepreneurship at Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jodhpur, specializing in Prognostics and Health Management, Industry 4.0, Reliability and Risk Management, Digital Twin, and Digital Transformation. Prior to joining IIT Jodhpur, Dr. Tanwar gained extensive research experience as a Postdoctoral Research Fellow at the Singapore University of Technology and Design (SUTD) and as a Researcher at Leibniz University, Germany. Dr. Tanwar is actively engaged in various international research collaborations, working with leading institutions and researchers across the globe in the field of PHM  & Digital Twin. She also serves on the review panels of more than 10 internationally reputed journals and conferences.

 People 
 
 
 Deepak Srivastav 
 
 
 
 
 
 Deepak Srivastav 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Deepak Srivastav
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD: Indian Institute of Management, Kozhikode 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        deepaksrivastav[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Humanitarian Supply Chain, Disaster Operations Management, Sports Analytics, Business Applications of Analytics 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1809 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Deepak Srivastav is an Assistant Professor at the School of Management & Entrepreneurship, Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur. He holds a PhD in Quantitative Methods and Operations Management from the Indian Institute of Management Kozhikode and a B.Tech. in Mechanical Engineering from the Maulana Azad National Institute of Technology Bhopal.  Prior to joining IIT Jodhpur, Dr. Srivastav served as an Assistant Professor at FLAME University, Pune. He has also held visiting faculty positions at IIM Kashipur, IIM Amritsar, IIM Rohtak, and IIM Sirmaur. His teaching spans operations management, supply chain management, and operations research, among others. Dr. Srivastav brings over five years of industry experience from Applied Materials India Private Ltd. and Lam Research India Private Ltd..  His research interests include humanitarian supply chain management, business applications of analytics, and sports analytics. His work has been published in reputed journals such as Annals of Operations Research and IIMB Management Review, and has been presented at leading international and national conferences, including POMS, EURO, and DSI.

 Seema Saini 
 
 
 
 
 
 Seema Saini 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Seema Saini

 school 
 
 PhD: Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        seemasaini[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Macro-Finance, Firm Dynamics and Applied Econometrics 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1803 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Seema Saini  is an Assistant Professor at the School of Management and Entrepreneurship (SME), Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jodhpur. Prior to joining IIT Jodhpur, she was an Assistant Professor at  Dr. B. R. Ambedkar School of Economics University, Bengaluru .  She holds a Ph.D. in Economics from the  Department of Economic Sciences ,   Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur .  Her research interests lie in macro-finance, firm dynamics, and applied econometrics. Her current research focuses on the intricate relationships between firm-level dynamics and their broader impact on the macroeconomy. She is also actively exploring the macroeconomics of climate change and key themes within development economics. Personal Website:  https://sites.google.com/view/seemasaini/home

 Venkat Ram Reddy Ganuthula 
 
 
 
 
 
 Venkat Ram Reddy Ganuthula 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Venkat Ram Reddy Ganuthula
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor  
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD:  IIT Madras 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        ram[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Human Capital & Organizational Dynamics 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1806 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        Bio:
                     
 
 
 Venkat Ram Reddy Ganuthula is an Assistant Professor at the School of Management and Entrepreneurship (SME) at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jodhpur, specializing in Human Capital and Organizational Dynamics. His research interests span behavioral science, judgment and decision-making, intelligence, rationality, and the implications of artificial intelligence (AI) on human behavior and organizational systems. Prior to IIT Jodhpur, he served as a faculty member in the Organization Management area at IIT Delhi. He holds a Master of Science and a Ph.D. in Management Science, and his work integrates insights from disciplines such as economics, neuroscience, and organizational theory.

 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Magesh Nagarajan is an Associate Professor in IIT Jodhpur's School of Management and Entrepreneurship. He has a background in  Engineering , a master’s degree from  Indian Institute of Technology Madras  and a PhD in Operations and Information Management ( Aston Business School , UK). As an interdisciplinary researcher, his primary interests are in applying Analytics, Operational research, Simulation, Agent-based modelling, Management science in enterprise and public services.

 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Manisha Yadav 
 
 
 
 
 
 Manisha Yadav 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Manisha Yadav
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD: Indian Institute of Technology, Roorkee 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        manishayadav[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Behavioral finance, asset pricing, and cryptocurrencies 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1814 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Manisha is an assistant professor of finance and accounting at the School of Management and Entrepreneurship, Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur. Before moving to IIT Jodhpur, she worked with Jindal School of Banking & Finance, OP Jindal Global University, Sonipat. Her doctoral research is in the domain of finance from the Department of Management Studies (DoMS), Indian Institute of Technology, Roorkee.  Her research expertise lies in behavioral finance, asset pricing, and cryptocurrencies. She has published three research articles in reputed journals like “Review of Behavioral Finance”, “Managerial Finance”, and “Studies in Economics and Finance”. She has also presented her work at various national and international conferences of repute, including the World Finance Conference, Pan IIT conference (ICFMCF), and the Annual International Capital Markets Conference organized by NISM.  Publications:  Yadav, M. (2024). Behavioral biases of cryptocurrency investors: a prospect theory model to explain cryptocurrency returns. Review of Behavioral Finance (ABDC-B), 16(4), 643–667. https://doi.org/10.1108/RBF-07-2023-0172  Yadav, M., & Dixit, G. (2024). Asymmetric dependence between the prospect theory value and stock returns in India: a quantile regression approach. Managerial Finance, (ABDC-B), 51(2), 236–261. https://doi.org/10.1108/MF-10-2023-0628/FULL/PDF  Yadav, M. (2025). Intraday lottery demands in the cryptocurrency market. Studies in Economics and Finance, ahead-of-print(ahead-of-print). https://doi.org/10.1108/SEF- 07-2024-0461  Conferences:  Yadav M. (2023) “Asymmetric Dependence Between the Prospect Theory Value and Stock Returns: A Quantile Regression Approach” presented at 4th Capital Markets Conference 2023, organised by NISM and LSE.  Yadav M. & Jindal L. (2023) “Order Imbalance and Market Efficiency: Evidence from a Pure Order-driven Market” paper presented for the 2023 World finance conference-Norway.  Yadav M. (2023) “Behavioral Biases of Cryptocurrency Investors: A Prospect Theory Model to Explain Cryptocurrency Returns” paper presented at 2nd VSEFI 2023 held at Vietnam National University - International School, Hanoi, Vietnam.  Yadav M. (2023) “Behavioural Biases of Cryptocurrency Investors: A Prospect Theory Model to Explain Cryptocurrency Returns” paper presented at the 2023 China Finance Review International & China International Risk Forum Joint Conference held at the Antai College of Economics and Management, Shanghai Jiao Tong University  Yadav M. & Tripathi A. (2023) “System 1 and Cryptocurrency Investors: A Prospect Theory Model to Explain Cryptocurrency Returns” won best paper at the Research Conclave, YES SECURITIES Benchmark 2023, held on 29 July at SPJIMR SP Jain Institute of Management & Research, Mumbai, India.  Yadav M. (2023) “Behavioural Biases of Cryptocurrency Investors: A Prospect Theory Model to Explain Cryptocurrency Returns” paper presented at 5th International Conference on Financial Markets & Corporate Finance (ICFMCF 2023) held at IIT Delhi.  Yadav M. & Tripathi A. (2023) “System 1 and Cryptocurrency Investors: A Prospect Theory Model to Explain Cryptocurrency Returns” paper presented for the International Conference on Sustainable Business Management (SBM 2023) held at IIT Roorkee.  Yadav M. (2023) “Behavioural Biases of Cryptocurrency Investors: A Prospect Theory Model to Explain Cryptocurrency Returns” paper presented for 1 st Management Doctoral Colloquium held at IIT Jodhpur.

                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Neha is an Assistant Professor in the School of Management & Entrepreneurship (SME) at IIT Jodhpur. She holds a B.Tech in Information Technology and an MBA (Gold Medal). She earned her Ph.D. from the Indian Institute of Technology Roorkee. A scholar of both technology and management, her interests lie at the intersection of innovation and marketing. She has pursued her research and academic work in this domain and continues to contribute actively through teaching, research, and thought leadership.

 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Deepak Kumar Saxena 
 
 
 
 
 
 Deepak Kumar Saxena 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Deepak Kumar Saxena
                         
 
                                Associate Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD: Trinity College Dublin 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Management Information Systems 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        saxenad[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1821 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Deepak Kumar Saxena is currently Associate Professor and Head of School of Management & Entrepreneurship. He holds a PhD in Management Information Systems from Trinity College Dublin (Ireland) and an M. Tech. (Industrial and Management Engineering) from IIT Kanpur. Before moving to IIT Jodhpur, he worked with Trinity College Dublin (Postdoctoral Research Fellow), Dublin Institute of Technology (Lecturer), and Birla Institute of Technology & Science Pilani (Assistant Professor). Prior to his PhD, he worked with the Indian Space Research Centre, Council for Scientific and Industrial Research, and Defence Research and Development Organisation in different capacities.
 
His current areas of interest include Enterprise Systems (on-premise and cloud-based), Digital Transformation, and AI Ethics. He has published his works, more than 70 to his credit, in ABS, ABDC, SCI, and Scopus-listed journals. He is currently serving as an Associate Editor for two Scopus-indexed journals and serving on the review panel of more than 20 internationally reputed Information Systems journals. He has co-authored a textbook on  Digital Business Models  and co-edited two volumes -   IoT and Cloud Computing for Societal Good  (Springer Nature) and  Cloud IoT: Concepts, Paradigms, and Applications.

 School of Design 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Kedar Dicholkar 
 
 
 
 
 
 Kedar Dicholkar 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Kedar Dicholkar
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: Indian Institute of Technology, Kanpur 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        kedard[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Interaction design; User experience design; visual culture and narrative systems; interactive storytelling; game design; experimental and new media art; design aesthetics; art, craft, practice-based research. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Kedar Dicholkar is an Assistant Professor at the School of Design, IIT Jodhpur. He holds an Integrated Master of Design–PhD in Design from IIT Kanpur, where his research focused on interaction-based interventions in Indian Classical Music through visualization, systems thinking, and performance-based exploration. Dr. Dicholkar’s academic and research interests span UX research, interaction design, embodied cognition, design methodology, and the use of emerging media to support performance-based and cultural practices. Prior to joining IIT Jodhpur, he was associated with the Symbiosis Institute of Design, where he contributed to teaching, curriculum development, and mentoring for BDes and MDes students. Alongside his ongoing research and creative practice of art and Indian Classical Music, Dr. Kedar has been awarded scholarship from the Centre for Cultural Resources and Training (CCRT).

 School of Design 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Shiv Kumar Verma 
 
 
 
 
 
 Shiv Kumar Verma 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Shiv Kumar Verma
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: Department of Design, Indian Institute of Technology Guwahati 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 M.Des.: Indian Institute of Information Technology Design & Manufacturing, Jabalpur 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        skv[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Industrial Design, Design Methodology, Form Studies, Design Education and Pedagogy, Product Semantics, Design & Nature, and Organic/Biomorphic Design. 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1788 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Shiv is an Assistant Professor at the School of Design, IIT Jodhpur. He holds a PhD in Design from IIT Guwahati and an M.Des. from IIITDM Jabalpur. His academic and research interests lie in the areas of Industrial Design, Design Methodology, Form Studies, Design Education and Pedagogy, Product Semantics, Design & Nature, and Organic/Biomorphic Design. Before joining IIT Jodhpur, Dr. Shiv was the first faculty member and a founding member of the Department of Design at Tezpur University. There, he played a central role in building the department from the ground up and initiating its M.Des., B.Des., and PhD programs. In addition to teaching and research, he is actively engaged in design practice as an Industrial Designer, contributing to consultancy projects and collaborative research that connect academic inquiry with real-world design challenges.

 People 
 
 
 Sumana Som 
 
 
 
 
 
 Sumana Som 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Sumana Som
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor 
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Masters in Fine Arts 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: Indian Institute of Technology Hyderabad, Joint PhD: Swinburne University of Technology 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        sumanasom[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Content design in XR technology, User interface design in XR, Visual culture, Storytelling and interactivity in visual narratives, Experimental art practice, Design aesthetics, New media art, Art craft and toy design, Game design, Research design in creative practice, Practice-based research 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1785 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Sumana Som is an art practitioner in the realm of art and design research, specializing in the creation of virtual reality environments, educational content, and the development of innovative tools. Her academic journey includes the completion of a PhD (2022) from the esteemed Indian Institute of Technology Hyderabad, during which she also had the opportunity to contribute as a partner PhD scholar with Swinburne University of Technology, Australia.  Dr. Sumana's  educational background comprises a Bachelor in visual arts degree (BVA-2011) from the College of Art & Design, University of Burdwan. Subsequently, she completed Master's degree (MFA- 2013) from Sarojini Naidu School of Arts and Communication, University of Hyderabad. Her creative inspiration draws from a diverse array of sources, ranging from short stories and novels to Bengali literature, cinema and Hindi cinema that vividly depict different behavioural perceptions. Her creative design practics manifests in both large-scale narrative explorations and small-scale representations of instantaneous thoughts, inviting viewers to engage with the profound layers of meaning within each piece. Her practiced acknowledged with prestigious awards such as, the Inlaks Fine Arts Award in 2013, and the National Academy Award from Lalit Kala Academy, New Delhi in 2014, Elizabeth Greenshields Grant 2017 from Canada USA. Currently, she is assistant Professor in the School of Design, IIT Jodhpur and actively engaged with the contemporary art gallery, Dhi Artspace, located in Hyderabad.

 Priyabrata Rautray 
 
 
 
 
 
 Priyabrata Rautray 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Priyabrata Rautray
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Master in Architecture (Specialisation in Industrial Design), New Delhi, Joint Ph.D. 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Joint Ph.D. from Indian Institute of Technology Hyderabad & Swinburne University of Technology, AUS 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        priyabratarautray[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 User Centered Design, Sustainable architecture and design solutions, Design research and Frugal Design, Product Design and Rapid Prototyping, Installation, 3D scanning and digital documentation (Photogrammetry) 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1784 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Priyabrata Rautray, Assistant Professor at the School of Design, IIT Jodhpur, is a passionate design researcher with 16+ years of experience in architecture and design in India. He has a bachelor's degree in architecture (2007), a master's degree in industrial design (2009), and a Joint PhD degree from IIT Hyderabad and Swinburne University, Australia (2023). His research focused on understanding users’ needs, wants and concerns and how to convert these users’ insights into design parameters. He has worked on many collaborative projects and has secured funding from the Government of India and other funding agencies. He has one Indian Bio-Bricks patent and twenty design registrations for Personal Areial Vehicles and Smart Product designs.

 People 
 
 
 Sajan Pillai 
 
 
 
 
 
 Sajan Pillai 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Sajan Pillai
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 MDes: Indian Institute of Information Technology, Design and Manufacturing, Jabalpur 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD: Indian Institute of Technology Bombay 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        sajanpillai[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 User Experience, UX Strategy, Inclusive and Accessible Design, Design for Emerging Technologies 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1783 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Sajan Pillai is an Assistant Professor at the School of Design, IIT Jodhpur. He holds a Master's degree in Interaction Design and a Ph.D. in Human Factors. Before joining academia, Sajan spent over seven years in the design industry, leading projects across various sectors and user demographics. His work focused on creating user-centred solutions for complex systems, focusing on usability and accessibility. At IIT Jodhpur, he integrates his industry insights and experience into teaching and research, aiming to bridge the gap between practical design challenges and academic exploration.

 People 
 
 
 Pranjal Protim Borah 
 
 
 
 
 
 Pranjal Protim Borah 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Pranjal Protim Borah
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 M.Tech. (Computer Science and Engineering - Artificial Intelligence): Assam Don Bosco University 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D. (Human-Computer Interaction): Indian Institute of Technology Guwahati, India 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        pranjalborah[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Human-computer Interaction (HCI), Human-robot Interaction (HRI), Multi-sensory UX Design, Interaction Techniques, Haptics, Ageing and Accessibility, Game Design, Extended Reality (XR), Machine Learning 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1786 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Pranjal Protim Borah is an Assistant Professor at the School of Design, Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jodhpur, India. He holds a B.Tech. in Computer Science and Engineering and an M.Tech. with a specialization in Artificial Intelligence. He earned his Ph.D. in Design, focusing on accessible gesture-based interactions for flexible smartphones that enable users with blindness or low vision to draw primitive geometric shapes. With over ten years of academic and research experience, he has actively contributed to diverse areas, including interaction techniques for emerging technologies, tangible and haptic user interfaces, multi-sensory UX design, aging and accessibility, rehabilitation, inclusive design, interactive computing, and machine learning (ML). Beyond research, he finds joy in painting, sculpting, photography, and gardening.

 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Gaurav Vinod Vaidya 
 
 
 
 
 
 Gaurav Vinod Vaidya 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Gaurav Vinod Vaidya
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 M.Des. (Industrial Design): IDC School of Design, Indian Institute of Technology Bombay 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: Department of Design, Indian Institute of Technology Guwahati 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        gauravvaidya[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Industrial Design, Product Form and Aesthetics, Emotional Design, Craft Culture Design and Development, Toy Design, Furniture and Space Design, Product Styling and Perception 
 
 
 
 call 
 
  0291 280 1787 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Gaurav Vaidya is an Assistant Professor at the School of Design, IIT Jodhpur. He holds a bachelor's degree in Mechanical Engineering (Nagpur University), an M.Des. in Industrial Design (IDC School of Design, IIT Bombay), and a Ph.D. in Design (DoD, IIT Guwahati). Dr. Gaurav has over 12 years of academic and industry experience, having worked with Larsen & Toubro (L&T), Godrej Interio, and the National Institute of Fashion Technology (NIFT). He was a visiting faculty member at the National Institute of Design, Madhya Pradesh (NID MP); the School of Planning and Architecture (SPA), Bhopal; the Indian Institute of Information Technology, Design & Manufacturing, (IIITDM), Jabalpur; and the Gati Shakti Vishwavidyalaya (GSV), Vadodara, India. His work spans design education, emotional design, and new product development, integrating craft sensibilities and user-centered approaches in design practice.

 People 
 
 
 Suman Dhaka 
 
 
 
 
 
 Suman Dhaka 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Suman Dhaka
                         
 
                                Associate Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
  Indian Institute of Technology Guwahati 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                         dhakas[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
  Cognitive Neuroscience, Cognitive Psychology; Sleep and Cognition

 
 
 
 
 call 
 
  0291 280 1415 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                         Suman Dhaka
                     
 
 
 An Assistant Professor at the School of Liberal Arts, IIT Jodhpur. Her research explores fundamental and applied questions at the intersection of brain and behavior, focusing on cognitive processes, neural dynamics, and adaptability. She integrates EEG, brain stimulation, and computational modeling to advance understanding across cognitive neuroscience, sleep and cognitive well-being interventions, and neuromodulation strategies for diverse populations. Dr. Dhaka has successfully led R&D projects funded by ICMR, ICSSR, and DST, and collaborates actively with institutions such as the University of Pennsylvania, AIIMS, and IITs. For further details, please visit: [ https://sites.google.com/ iitj.ac.in/dhakas?usp=sharing ] . "The mind is restless and difficult to restrain, but it is subdued by practice."  — Bhagavad Gita, Chapter 6, Verse 35"

 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Anupama Mohan 
 
 
 
 
 
 Anupama Mohan 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Anupama Mohan
                         
 
                                Associate Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 University of Toronto 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        amohan[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Indian Ocean Studies; Working class literature; Transmodernity and Literature; Environmental Humanities; History of Ideas 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1419 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Anupama Mohan joined the School of Liberal Arts in Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur as Associate Professor of English in December 2022. A university first-rank-holder and gold medallist from Delhi University, she completed her PhD with distinction from the University of Toronto. She is the author of the MLA First Book Prize-nominated monograph Utopia and the Village in South Asian Literatures (Palgrave Macmillan UK, 2012) and the Editor of "Literature and Transmodernity: Of Worlds in Contact" (Brill; July 2025 & Jan 2026) and "Maritime Transmodernities: Literatures of the Indian Ocean" (Postcolonial Text, 2019). Her critical essays have featured in peer-reviewed journals such as South Asian History and Culture, Asian Review of World History, University of Toronto Quarterly, Indian Theatre Journal, and Economic and Political Weekly, among others. She was the 2021 Writer-in-Residence at the Samyukta Research Foundation, Thiruvananthapuram, and the 2024 Landhaus Fellow at the Rachel Carson Center for Environment and Society, University of Munich, Germany. Anupama is the author of the novel Where Mayflies Live Forever (Picador, 2022), the poetry collection, Twenty Odd Love Poems (The Writer's Workshop, 2008), and several short stories featured in print and online venues.
 
For more information on her research and current projects, see  www.anupamamohan.in

 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Souryabrata Mohapatra 
 
 
 
 
 
 Souryabrata Mohapatra 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Souryabrata Mohapatra
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: University of Auckland 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        smohapatra[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Economics of climate change, sustainable agriculture, green transitions, equitable migration, and rural resilience. 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 +91 (0) 291 280 1428 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        Souryabrata Mohapatra
                     
 
 
 Sourya joined the School as an economics faculty member in
Spring 2025. His empirical research lies at the intersection of environmental
and development economics, with an emphasis on climate change, sustainable
agriculture, green transitions, and equitable migration. He examines how
environmental factors interact with structural economic vulnerabilities to
inform effective policy solutions. Over the years, Sourya has held various research positions.
His work has been published in reputable ABDC-indexed journals, earning him
awards and honoraria. His invited popular writings appear in leading newspapers
and magazines such as  The Hindu ,  Business Line ,  Business Today ,
 The Indian Express ,  The Financial Express ,  Hindustan Times ,
and others. An active member of several professional associations,
Sourya regularly contributes to interdisciplinary conferences and policy
forums. Notably, he has collaborated with ADBI (Tokyo) on multiple occasions.
His academic achievements include a gold medal in his bachelor’s programme,
distinctions from the RBI Governor during his master’s studies, and an early
doctoral thesis completion award from the University of Auckland Business
School. He has also received top-tier publication recognition from CB Research Networks
(USA). To learn more, visit:  www.sites.google.com/view/souryabrata .   Graduate Supervision :
Available 
 Media Inquiries :
Available

 School of Liberal Arts 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Farhat Naz 
 
 
 
 
 
 Farhat Naz 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Farhat Naz
                         
 
                                Associate Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 University of Bonn, Germany 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        farhat[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Natural Resource Management; Water Governance; Climate Change, Disaster Risk Reduction, Gender 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1411 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        Farhat Naz   
                     
 
 
 An Associate Professor of Sociology, at the School of Liberal Arts, IIT Jodhpur, India. She is a development sociologist by academic training, and her present research is focussed on sustainability, policy and governance. She is an alumna of University of Bonn, Germany; Australian National University, Australia; Jawaharlal Nehru University, New Delhi, and University of Delhi, India. Farhat is a DAAD and Endeavour Fellow. Currently, she is a DAAD Research Ambassador for 2022-25. She has conducted development research in a diverse array of countries of South Asia, South-East Asia and Africa. Her expertise and research interest includes: Natural Resource Management, Water Governance, Disaster Risk Reduction, Climate Change Adaptation, Gender, Sanitation, and Governance Studies, to name a few. She has won many international grants and fellowships and Farhat sits on many international academic committees and boards. For more details,  on her research and work,   visit   https://farhatnaz.com/

 People 
 
 
 K J George 
 
 
 
 
 
 K J George 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            K J George
                         
 
                                Associate Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        kjg[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Philosophy: Applied Ethics, Ethics of Technology, Bioethics 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1404 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        K.J. George
                     
 
 
 An Associate Professor of Philosophy in the School of Liberal Arts,  Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur. He is an alumnus of IIT Kanpur (Ph.D.), Utrecht University, The Netherlands (Master’s), Linkoping University, Sweden (M.A.), University of Calicut (M.A.), Kannur University (B.Ed.), and Mahatma Gandhi University (B.A.).   George works in major areas of applied ethics, such as sustainability, social justice, ethics and technology, bioethics, and professional ethics, and furthermore, in philosophy of science, philosophy of religion, and Gandhian thought.

 People 
 
 
 V Hari Narayanan 
 
 
 
 
 
 V Hari Narayanan 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            V Hari Narayanan
                         
 
                                 Professor 
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        hari[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Philosophy: Cognitive Studies, Evolutionary Theory, Analytic Philosophy and Mindfulness 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1405 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        Hari Narayanan V
                     
 
 
 A Professor in Philosophy in the School of Liberal Arts at IIT Jodhpur. After completing his PhD from IIT Kanpur he taught at Shri Mata Vaishno Devi University for a brief period before joining IIT Jodhpur in 2011. He has published widely in journals such as AI and Society, Mind and Society and Balkan Journal of Philosophy, among others. At present, his research interest is centered around exploring how developments in 4E cognitive science can be used to defend a non-dualist world view.

 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Bhaskar Kumar Kakati 
 
 
 
 
 
 Bhaskar Kumar Kakati 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Bhaskar Kumar Kakati
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Tata Institute of Social Sciences, Guwahati campus 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        bhaskar[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Sociology of Rural Development, Appropriate Technology and Sustainability, Local Governance, Tribal Studies, Folk Theatre 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 02912801427 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        Bhaskar Kumar Kakati
                     
 
 
 Faculty member at the School of Liberal Arts, IIT Jodhpur. Prior to this, he served in various organisations, including IIM Shillong, IIE Guwahati, NIRD&PR (NERC), ASRLM, and PRADAN. He has led 10 research projects funded by ICSSR, NHRC, NABARD, NEDFi and others. His works have appeared in various journals such as  Area Development and Policy, Development in Practice, Sociological Bulletin ,  Local Development and Society . Bhaskar is an accredited International Peace Fellow, recognised by SAP, MGM University, Gujarat Vidyapeeth, and the Sabarmati Ashram Trust. A creative writer in Assamese, he has published over 100 poems and stories and authored two books on Assamese theatre. Actively involved in theatre as an actor and director, he has participated in five theatre workshops and six international theatre festivals. The Assam Sahitya Sabha felicitated him for his contribution to Assamese literature and theatre in 2020, and he received the 'Social Service Award 2024' from the Dispur Press Club for his contributions to rural development. Personal website:   https://sites.google.com/view/ bhaskar -kumar-kakati

 School of Liberal Arts 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Vidya Sarveswaran 
 
 
 
 
 
 Vidya Sarveswaran 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Vidya Sarveswaran
                         
 
                                Associate Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD From IIT Madras 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        vs[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Environmental Humanities, Environment and Culture, Environment and Heritage and Blue Humanities.  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        Vidya Sarveswaran
                     
 
 
 An Associate Professor of English with the School of Liberal Arts at IIT Jodhpur, since 2012. A University I rank holder and gold medallist at the Bachelors and Masters levels, her PHD is from IIT Madras. A Fulbright Fellow (2009) and a Rachel Carson Fellow from Ludwig Maximillian University, Munich (2016), Vidya works in the areas of Environmental Humanities, Environment and Culture, Environment and Heritage and Blue Humanities. She has co-edited four books: Ecoambiguity, Community and Development: Toward a Politicized Ecocriticism (Lexington Press Maryland, 2014), Ecocriticism of the Global South (Lexington Press, Maryland 2015), The Routledge Handbook of Ecocriticism and Environmental Communication (Routledge 2019) and The Bloomsbury Handbook to Medical Environmental Humanities (2023).  Her most recent book titled Pravesh: Rite of Passage (co-authored) was released by the Honourable Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi in the year 2024. She was the recipient of the Public Outreach Grant (2017) from the Rachel Carson Centre for Environment and Society, Munich to make her debut documentary film Under Another Sky (2020). Her collaborative project with IIT Madras on temple conservation, was recently awarded the UNESCO Asia Pacific Award of Distinction (2024), for exceptional conservation efforts.

 People 
 
 
 Prasenjeet A Tribhuvan 
 
 
 
 
 
 Prasenjeet A Tribhuvan 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Prasenjeet A Tribhuvan
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Delhi School of Economics, University of Delhi 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        prasenjeet[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Anthropology of Material Objects, STS studies in Sociology, Political Ecology, Tourism and Youth Subcultures 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1408 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        Prasenjeet Tribhuvan
                     
 
 
 A Social Anthropologist. He is currently an Assistant Professor of Sociology at School of Liberal Arts, IIT, Jodhpur and a former Fellow at Indian Institute of Advanced Study, Shimla. His research interests lie mainly in the areas of Science and Technology Studies, Ethnographic studies of communities in ecologically sensitive regions, Ethnomusicology and relationship between law and society, typically existence of illegality in society as public secrets. Dr. Prasenjeet has a few publications to his name in journals, edited volumes and news platforms and is currently finishing his monograph titled “Decolonizing Cannabis: History, Hegemony and Opportunities” from IIAS press.

 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Rachel Philip 
 
 
 
 
 
 Rachel Philip 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Rachel Philip
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Central Institute of Education, University of Delhi 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        rachel[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Sociology of Education; Education Policy; Childhood Studies; Social Stratification; Science, Technology, Society Studies 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1412 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        Rachel Philip
                     
 
 
 An Assistant Professor in the School of Liberal Arts, IIT 
Jodhpur. Rachel’s interdisciplinary background includes a BA (Hons) in 
English from St. Stephen’s College, University of Delhi, a MA in 
Sociology from the Department of Sociology, Delhi School of Economics 
and a PhD in Education from the Central Institute of Education, 
University of Delhi. Working at the intersection  of the Sociology of 
Education, Social stratification, and Science Technology Society (STS) 
studies, her research traces the socio-material histories of education 
interventions, and how they bridge the gap between policy, institutions 
and individuals. Her first book, a monograph on India's oldest Talent 
Search Examination for school students, titled   The Nation's Got Talent: Education, Experimentation and Policy Discourses in India   was
 published by Routledge in 2023. Her research interests (also spanning 
her present work and projects) include sociological approaches to 
technology-enhanced learning, professional development of teachers, and 
educational interventions for children in vulnerable contexts. Prior to 
joining to IIT Jodhpur, she has also taught at the Departments of 
Sociology at Lady Shri Ram College for Women and Indraprastha College 
for Women    University of Delhi. Apart from academia, she 
also brings experience from the Corporate Social Responsibility domain, 
having worked at the Center for Elementary Education, ICICI Foundation, 
Pune.

 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Sunil Kumar Lohar 
 
 
 
 
 
 Sunil Kumar Lohar 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Sunil Kumar Lohar
                         
 
                                 Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
  International Institute of Information Technology Hyderabad 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                         sunillohar[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
  Visual Arts, Indian Folk Paintings, Aesthetics, Communication Design, and Formal Theories of Arts

 
 
 
 
 call 
 
  0291 280 1424 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        Sunil Kumar Lohar
                     
 
 
 An Assistant Professor of Art and Aesthetics at the School of Liberal Arts, IIT Jodhpur. He earned his PhD from the International Institute of Information Technology, Hyderabad, and has over 15 years of interdisciplinary experience at the intersection of art, design, and community-based research. His work focuses on Indigenous knowledge systems and developing sustainable solutions for artisan communities across India. Dr. Lohar has been associated with several research projects and initiatives documenting, preserving, and empowering traditional art practices. He has received numerous prestigious awards and fellowships, including the Junior Fellowship from the Centre for Cultural Resources and Training (CCRT), and has participated in international artist residencies. His scholarly interests include Indian folk aesthetics, visual grammar, and the media empowerment of traditional artists. Through his work, Dr. Lohar contributes meaningfully to both academic scholarship and real-world applications that benefit marginalized creative communities.

 People 
 
 
 Ankita Sharma 
 
 
 
 
 
 Ankita Sharma 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Ankita Sharma
                         
 
                                Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Banaras Hindu University 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        ankitasharma[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Psychology: Gerontology, Clinical and Positive Psychology 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1406 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        Ankita Sharma
                     
 
 
 Professor of Psychology at IIT Jodhpur with a deep interest in Positive Psychology, especially in understanding human strengths and weaknesses through the lens of virtues, wisdom, happiness, and wellbeing. Her work also explores misinformation, decision-making, and social cognition, supported by expertise in psychometrics and research methodology. She has been involved in projects funded by DRDO, DST, and ICSSR, and collaborates with institutions in India and abroad, including the University of Queensland, EPFL, and PACE University. With over 15 years of experience, she remains committed to research, teaching, and promoting holistic human development.

 People 
 
 
 Tonisha Guin 
 
 
 
 
 
 Tonisha Guin 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Tonisha Guin
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 The English and Foreign Languages University, Hyderabad 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        tonisha[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Knowledge systems in Global South, Decoloniality, Identity Studies, Space Studies, New Media Issues

 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1407 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        Tonisha Guin
                     
 
 
 Trained
 in English Literary and Cultural Studies, Tonisha Guin’s academic 
interests are identity & spatiality studies, popular culture, 
decoloniality, knowledge systems in the Global South, and New Media 
Studies. She has studied at EFL University, Hyderabad. Her doctoral 
research looked at mainstream bhadralok identity formation around 
Barabazar, Kolkata.  For
 the last few years, she has been looking at biopolitical and ecological
 concerns around the Sundarbans area in West Bengal, India through ghost
 stories for young adults, popular belief, practices of ecological 
conservation, and lores around tigers. She
 joined the School of Liberal Arts, IIT Jodhpur, as an Assistant 
Professor in July 2022. She is also an affiliated faculty member of the 
Inter-Disciplinary Research Division of Digital Humanities and the 
Centre for Education and Technology for Education at IIT Jodhpur.  To learn more, visit  https://sites.google. com/view/tonishaguin/home

 People 
 
 
 Gurujegan Murugesan 
 
 
 
 
 
 Gurujegan Murugesan 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Gurujegan Murugesan
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 University of Leipzig, Germany 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        gurujeganm[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Theoretical/Formal Linguistics, Syntax. 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1409 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        Gurujegan Murugesan
                     
 
 
 Gurujegan Murugesan is  linguist and his research focus is primarily on theoretical/formal linguistics with a fieldwork-oriented approach. He did his PhD at Universität Leipzig, Germany, on the topic Predicting the Anaphor Agreement Effect and its Violations. Currently, he has been working on non-canonical grammatical patterns of endangered/tribal languages of South Asia and the general challenges they pose to the current syntactic theory.

 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Sreedevi D 
 
 
 
 
 
 Sreedevi D 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Sreedevi D
                         
 
                                 Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
  Jawaharlal Nehru University, New Delhi 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                         sreedevid[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
  Sensory Studies; Literary and Critical Theory; Senses and Writing; Touch and Aesthetics

 
 
 
 
 call 
 
  0291 280 1418 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        Sreedevi D
                     
 
 
 Joined the School of Liberal Arts, Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur, as an Assistant Professor in August 2022. Her doctoral work examined the theorisation of sense experiences, particularly haptic experiences in literary and cultural theory. She received early career fellowship, 2022-23 from Kerala Council for Historical Research for her work which examines the cultural history of artificial illumination and the subsequent transformation of the sensorial registers of public spaces in Kerala . Her areas of research are literary and critical theory; culture and technology; senses and writing.
